verses.greek: Ἀρχὴ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου Ἰησοῦ ⸀χριστοῦ.
verses.mistral: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ.
verses.kjv: The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ, the Son of God;

verses.greek: ⸀Καθὼς γέγραπται ἐν ⸂τῷ Ἠσαΐᾳ τῷ προφήτῃ⸃· ⸀Ἰδοὺ ἀποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου πρὸ προσώπου σου, ὃς κατασκευάσει τὴν ὁδόν ⸀σου·
verses.mistral: Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who shall prepare your way.
verses.kjv: As it is written in the prophets, Behold, I send my messenger before thy face, which shall prepare thy way before thee.

verses.greek: φωνὴ βοῶντος ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ· Ἑτοιμάσατε τὴν ὁδὸν κυρίου, εὐθείας ποιεῖτε τὰς τρίβους αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Prepare in the wilderness the way of the Lord; Make straight in the desert the paths of our God.
verses.kjv: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

verses.greek: ἐγένετο Ἰωάννης ⸀ὁ βαπτίζων ἐν τῇ ⸀ἐρήμῳ κηρύσσων βάπτισμα μετανοίας εἰς ἄφεσιν ἁμαρτιῶν.
verses.mistral: John appeared in the wilderness baptizing and proclaiming a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
verses.kjv: John did baptize in the wilderness, and preach the baptism of repentance for the remission of sins.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξεπορεύετο πρὸς αὐτὸν πᾶσα ἡ Ἰουδαία χώρα καὶ οἱ Ἱεροσολυμῖται ⸂πάντες, καὶ ἐβαπτίζοντο⸃ ⸂ὑπ’ αὐτοῦ ἐν τῷ Ἰορδάνῃ ποταμῷ⸃ ἐξομολογούμενοι τὰς ἁμαρτίας αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: All of Judea and all Jerusalem were going out to him, and they were being baptized by him in the Jordan River confessing their sins.
verses.kjv: And there went out unto him all the land of Judaea, and they of Jerusalem, and were all baptized of him in the river of Jordan, confessing their sins.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἦν⸃ ὁ Ἰωάννης ἐνδεδυμένος τρίχας καμήλου καὶ ζώνην δερματίνην περὶ τὴν ὀσφὺν αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἔσθων ἀκρίδας καὶ μέλι ἄγριον.
verses.mistral: John was clothed with clothes of camel's hair and a leather belt around his waist, and he ate locusts and wild honey.
verses.kjv: And John was clothed with camel's hair, and with a girdle of a skin about his loins; and he did eat locusts and wild honey;

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκήρυσσεν λέγων· Ἔρχεται ὁ ἰσχυρότερός μου ὀπίσω μου, οὗ οὐκ εἰμὶ ἱκανὸς κύψας λῦσαι τὸν ἱμάντα τῶν ὑποδημάτων αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: And he was preaching, saying, "After me comes one who is stronger than I, and I am not fit to stoop down and untie the thong of his sandals."
verses.kjv: And preached, saying, There cometh one mightier than I after me, the latchet of whose shoes I am not worthy to stoop down and unloose.

verses.greek: ⸀ἐγὼ ἐβάπτισα ⸀ὑμᾶς ὕδατι, αὐτὸς δὲ βαπτίσει ὑμᾶς ⸀ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ.
verses.mistral: I have baptized you with water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: I indeed have baptized you with water: but he shall baptize you with the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις ἦλθεν Ἰησοῦς ἀπὸ Ναζαρὲτ τῆς Γαλιλαίας καὶ ἐβαπτίσθη ⸂εἰς τὸν Ἰορδάνην ὑπὸ Ἰωάννου⸃.
verses.mistral: In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass in those days, that Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee, and was baptized of John in Jordan.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀναβαίνων ⸀ἐκ τοῦ ὕδατος εἶδεν σχιζομένους τοὺς οὐρανοὺς καὶ τὸ πνεῦμα ⸀ὡς περιστερὰν καταβαῖνον ⸀εἰς αὐτόν·
verses.mistral: And immediately coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens being torn apart and the Spirit, like a dove, descending upon him.
verses.kjv: And straightway coming up out of the water, he saw the heavens opened, and the Spirit like a dove descending upon him:

verses.greek: καὶ φωνὴ ἐγένετο ἐκ τῶν οὐρανῶν· Σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός, ἐν ⸀σοὶ εὐδόκησα.
verses.mistral: And a voice came from heaven: "You are my beloved Son; in you I am well-pleased."
verses.kjv: And there came a voice from heaven, saying, Thou art my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.

verses.greek: Καὶ εὐθὺς τὸ πνεῦμα αὐτὸν ἐκβάλλει εἰς τὴν ἔρημον.
verses.mistral: Immediately, the Spirit drove him.
verses.kjv: And immediately the Spirit driveth him into the wilderness.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἦν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ⸂τεσσεράκοντα ἡμέρας⸃ πειραζόμενος ὑπὸ τοῦ Σατανᾶ, καὶ ἦν μετὰ τῶν θηρίων, καὶ οἱ ἄγγελοι διηκόνουν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, being tempted by Satan, and being with the wild animals, and the angels ministered to him.
verses.kjv: And he was there in the wilderness forty days, tempted of Satan; and was with the wild beasts; and the angels ministered unto him.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ μετὰ⸃ τὸ παραδοθῆναι τὸν Ἰωάννην ἦλθεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν κηρύσσων τὸ ⸀εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.mistral: And after John was handed over, Jesus came to Galilee, proclaiming the gospel of God
verses.kjv: Now after that John was put in prison, Jesus came into Galilee, preaching the gospel of the kingdom of God,

verses.greek: καὶ λέγων ὅτι Πεπλήρωται ὁ καιρὸς καὶ ἤγγικεν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ· μετανοεῖτε καὶ πιστεύετε ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ.
verses.mistral: And saying, "The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel."
verses.kjv: And saying, The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand: repent ye, and believe the gospel.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ παράγων⸃ παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν τῆς Γαλιλαίας εἶδεν Σίμωνα καὶ Ἀνδρέαν τὸν ⸀ἀδελφὸν Σίμωνος ⸀ἀμφιβάλλοντας ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ, ἦσαν γὰρ ἁλιεῖς·
verses.mistral: Emerging from the Galilee sea, he saw Simon and Andrew, Simon's brother, casting a net in the sea; for they were fishermen.
verses.kjv: Now as he walked by the sea of Galilee, he saw Simon and Andrew his brother casting a net into the sea: for they were fishers.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Δεῦτε ὀπίσω μου, καὶ ποιήσω ὑμᾶς γενέσθαι ἁλιεῖς ἀνθρώπων.
verses.mistral: Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Come ye after me, and I will make you to become fishers of men.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀφέντες τὰ ⸀δίκτυα ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Immediately leaving their nets, they followed him.
verses.kjv: And straightway they forsook their nets, and followed him.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀προβὰς ὀλίγον εἶδεν Ἰάκωβον τὸν τοῦ Ζεβεδαίου καὶ Ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν αὐτοῦ, καὶ αὐτοὺς ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ καταρτίζοντας τὰ δίκτυα,
verses.mistral: And immediately he saw James the son of Zebedee and John his brother, and they were mending nets in the boat.
verses.kjv: And when he had gone a little further thence, he saw James the son of Zebedee, and John his brother, who also were in the ship mending their nets.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐκάλεσεν αὐτούς. καὶ ἀφέντες τὸν πατέρα αὐτῶν Ζεβεδαῖον ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ μετὰ τῶν μισθωτῶν ἀπῆλθον ὀπίσω αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Immediately he called them. And leaving their father Zebedee in the boat with the hired servants, they went after him.
verses.kjv: And straightway he called them: and they left their father Zebedee in the ship with the hired servants, and went after him.

verses.greek: Καὶ εἰσπορεύονται εἰς Καφαρναούμ. καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς τοῖς σάββασιν ⸂ἐδίδασκεν εἰς τὴν συναγωγήν⸃.
verses.mistral: Entering Capernaum, he began to teach in the synagogue on the Sabbath.
verses.kjv: And they went into Capernaum; and straightway on the sabbath day he entered into the synagogue, and taught.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξεπλήσσοντο ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ, ἦν γὰρ διδάσκων αὐτοὺς ὡς ἐξουσίαν ἔχων καὶ οὐχ ὡς οἱ γραμματεῖς.
verses.mistral: And they were astonished at His teaching, for He was teaching them with authority, and not as the scribes.
verses.kjv: And they were astonished at his doctrine: for he taught them as one that had authority, and not as the scribes.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἦν ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ αὐτῶν ἄνθρωπος ἐν πνεύματι ἀκαθάρτῳ καὶ ἀνέκραξεν
verses.mistral: Immediately there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit, and he cried out
verses.kjv: And there was in their synagogue a man with an unclean spirit; and he cried out,

verses.greek: λέγων· ⸀Τί ἡμῖν καὶ σοί, Ἰησοῦ Ναζαρηνέ; ἦλθες ἀπολέσαι ἡμᾶς; οἶδά σε τίς εἶ, ὁ ἅγιος τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Saying to Him: "What have You to do with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to destroy us? I know who You are—the Holy One of God."
verses.kjv: Saying, Let us alone; what have we to do with thee, thou Jesus of Nazareth? art thou come to destroy us? I know thee who thou art, the Holy One of God.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγων· Φιμώθητι καὶ ἔξελθε ἐξ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, 'Be silent and come out of him.'
verses.kjv: And Jesus rebuked him, saying, Hold thy peace, and come out of him.

verses.greek: καὶ σπαράξαν αὐτὸν τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἀκάθαρτον καὶ ⸀φωνῆσαν φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ἐξῆλθεν ἐξ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And the unclean spirit, convulsed and with a loud voice, came out of him.
verses.kjv: And when the unclean spirit had torn him, and cried with a loud voice, he came out of him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐθαμβήθησαν ⸀ἅπαντες, ὥστε συζητεῖν ⸂πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς⸃ λέγοντας· Τί ἐστιν τοῦτο; ⸂διδαχὴ καινή⸃· κατ’ ἐξουσίαν καὶ τοῖς πνεύμασι τοῖς ἀκαθάρτοις ἐπιτάσσει, καὶ ὑπακούουσιν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: They were all amazed, so that they questioned among themselves, saying, "What is this? A new teaching with authority! He even commands the unclean spirits and they obey him."
verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, insomuch that they questioned among themselves, saying, What thing is this? what new doctrine is this? for with authority commandeth he even the unclean spirits, and they do obey him.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἐξῆλθεν⸃ ἡ ἀκοὴ αὐτοῦ εὐθὺς ⸀πανταχοῦ εἰς ὅλην τὴν περίχωρον τῆς Γαλιλαίας.
verses.mistral: Immediately the news about him spread everywhere in all the surrounding region of Galilee.
verses.kjv: And immediately his fame spread abroad throughout all the region round about Galilee.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐκ τῆς συναγωγῆς ⸂ἐξελθόντες ἦλθον⸃ εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν Σίμωνος καὶ Ἀνδρέου μετὰ Ἰακώβου καὶ Ἰωάννου.
verses.mistral: Immediately, from the synagogue, they went out and came to the house of Simon and Andrew with James and John.
verses.kjv: And forthwith, when they were come out of the synagogue, they entered into the house of Simon and Andrew, with James and John.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ πενθερὰ Σίμωνος κατέκειτο πυρέσσουσα, καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς λέγουσιν αὐτῷ περὶ αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: The daughter of Simon is lying in bed sick, and they immediately told him about her.
verses.kjv: But Simon's wife's mother lay sick of a fever, and anon they tell him of her.

verses.greek: καὶ προσελθὼν ἤγειρεν αὐτὴν κρατήσας τῆς ⸀χειρός· καὶ ἀφῆκεν αὐτὴν ὁ ⸀πυρετός, καὶ διηκόνει αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: Approaching, He took her by the hand and raised her up; and the fever left her, and she served them.
verses.kjv: And he came and took her by the hand, and lifted her up; and immediately the fever left her, and she ministered unto them.

verses.greek: Ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης, ὅτε ⸀ἔδυ ὁ ἥλιος, ἔφερον πρὸς αὐτὸν πάντας τοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας καὶ τοὺς δαιμονιζομένους·
verses.mistral: Evening having come, and when the sun had set, they brought to Him all who were sick and those who were demon-possessed.
verses.kjv: And at even, when the sun did set, they brought unto him all that were diseased, and them that were possessed with devils.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ἦν ὅλη ἡ πόλις ἐπισυνηγμένη⸃ πρὸς τὴν θύραν.
verses.mistral: Entire city gathered at the door.
verses.kjv: And all the city was gathered together at the door.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐθεράπευσεν πολλοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας ποικίλαις νόσοις, καὶ δαιμόνια πολλὰ ἐξέβαλεν, καὶ οὐκ ἤφιεν λαλεῖν τὰ δαιμόνια, ὅτι ᾔδεισαν ⸀αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Healed many who were sick with various diseases, and drove out many demons; and he would not allow the demons to speak, because they knew him.
verses.kjv: And he healed many that were sick of divers diseases, and cast out many devils; and suffered not the devils to speak, because they knew him.

verses.greek: Καὶ πρωῒ ἔννυχα λίαν ἀναστὰς ἐξῆλθεν καὶ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς ἔρημον τόπον κἀκεῖ προσηύχετο.
verses.mistral: Rising very early before dawn, he went out and left for a solitary place, there he prayed.
verses.kjv: And in the morning, rising up a great while before day, he went out, and departed into a solitary place, and there prayed.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀κατεδίωξεν αὐτὸν ⸀Σίμων καὶ οἱ μετ’ αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Pursued him also Simon and those with him
verses.kjv: And Simon and they that were with him followed after him.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂εὗρον αὐτὸν καὶ⸃ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ὅτι Πάντες ⸂ζητοῦσίν σε⸃.
verses.mistral: Found him and saying to him, "All seek you."
verses.kjv: And when they had found him, they said unto him, All men seek for thee.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ἄγωμεν ⸀ἀλλαχοῦ εἰς τὰς ἐχομένας κωμοπόλεις, ἵνα καὶ ἐκεῖ κηρύξω, εἰς τοῦτο γὰρ ⸀ἐξῆλθον.
verses.mistral: Let us go on to the nearby towns, that I may preach there also; for this reason have I come.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Let us go into the next towns, that I may preach there also: for therefore came I forth.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἦλθεν κηρύσσων ⸂εἰς τὰς συναγωγὰς⸃ αὐτῶν εἰς ὅλην τὴν Γαλιλαίαν καὶ τὰ δαιμόνια ἐκβάλλων.
verses.mistral: Preaching in their synagogues throughout Galilee, He drove out demons.
verses.kjv: And he preached in their synagogues throughout all Galilee, and cast out devils.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχεται πρὸς αὐτὸν λεπρὸς παρακαλῶν αὐτὸν καὶ ⸀γονυπετῶν λέγων αὐτῷ ὅτι Ἐὰν θέλῃς δύνασαί με καθαρίσαι.
verses.mistral: Approaches him a leper, imploring him and on bended knee saying to him, "If you want, you can make me clean."
verses.kjv: And there came a leper to him, beseeching him, and kneeling down to him, and saying unto him, If thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ⸀ὀργισθεὶς ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα ⸂αὐτοῦ ἥψατο⸃ καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Θέλω, καθαρίσθητι·
verses.mistral: Made clean. [Mark 1:41] (The leper is addressing Jesus.)
verses.kjv: And Jesus, moved with compassion, put forth his hand, and touched him, and saith unto him, I will; be thou clean.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀπῆλθεν ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ ἡ λέπρα, καὶ ἐκαθαρίσθη.
verses.mistral: Immediately the leprosy left him, and he was cleansed.
verses.kjv: And as soon as he had spoken, immediately the leprosy departed from him, and he was cleansed.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐμβριμησάμενος αὐτῷ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐξέβαλεν αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Expelled him immediately
verses.kjv: And he straitly charged him, and forthwith sent him away;

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Ὅρα μηδενὶ μηδὲν εἴπῃς, ἀλλὰ ὕπαγε σεαυτὸν δεῖξον τῷ ἱερεῖ καὶ προσένεγκε περὶ τοῦ καθαρισμοῦ σου ἃ προσέταξεν Μωϋσῆς εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: Say to no one anything, but go show yourself to the priest and offer for your purification what Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.
verses.kjv: And saith unto him, See thou say nothing to any man: but go thy way, shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing those things which Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐξελθὼν ἤρξατο κηρύσσειν πολλὰ καὶ διαφημίζειν τὸν λόγον, ὥστε μηκέτι αὐτὸν δύνασθαι φανερῶς εἰς πόλιν εἰσελθεῖν, ἀλλὰ ἔξω ⸀ἐπ’ ἐρήμοις τόποις ἦν· καὶ ἤρχοντο πρὸς αὐτὸν ⸀πάντοθεν.
verses.mistral: He went out and began to preach widely, proclaiming the message, so that he could no longer openly enter a town, but was staying outside in remote places. People were coming to him from all over.
verses.kjv: But he went out, and began to publish it much, and to blaze abroad the matter, insomuch that Jesus could no more openly enter into the city, but was without in desert places: and they came to him from every quarter.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀εἰσελθὼν πάλιν εἰς Καφαρναοὺμ δι’ ⸀ἡμερῶν ἠκούσθη ὅτι ⸂ἐν οἴκῳ⸃ ἐστίν·
verses.mistral: And entering again into Capernaum after some days, it was heard that he was at home.
verses.kjv: And again he entered into Capernaum after some days; and it was noised that he was in the house.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ συνήχθησαν πολλοὶ ὥστε μηκέτι χωρεῖν μηδὲ τὰ πρὸς τὴν θύραν, καὶ ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς τὸν λόγον.
verses.mistral: And many were gathered so as not to be able to go out anymore nor at the door, and he spoke to them the word.
verses.kjv: And straightway many were gathered together, insomuch that there was no room to receive them, no, not so much as about the door: and he preached the word unto them.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔρχονται ⸂φέροντες πρὸς αὐτὸν παραλυτικὸν⸃ αἰρόμενον ὑπὸ τεσσάρων.
verses.mistral: Bringing a paralytic to him, they were lowering him through the roof
verses.kjv: And they come unto him, bringing one sick of the palsy, which was borne of four.

verses.greek: καὶ μὴ δυνάμενοι ⸀προσενέγκαι αὐτῷ διὰ τὸν ὄχλον ἀπεστέγασαν τὴν στέγην ὅπου ἦν, καὶ ἐξορύξαντες χαλῶσι τὸν κράβαττον ⸀ὅπου ὁ παραλυτικὸς κατέκειτο.
verses.mistral: They could not bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, so they removed the tiles and, having dug through it, lowered the paralytic where he lay.
verses.kjv: And when they could not come nigh unto him for the press, they uncovered the roof where he was: and when they had broken it up, they let down the bed wherein the sick of the palsy lay.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἰδὼν⸃ ὁ Ἰησοῦς τὴν πίστιν αὐτῶν λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ· Τέκνον, ⸂ἀφίενταί σου⸃ αἱ ⸀ἁμαρτίαι.
verses.mistral: Jesus seeing their faith said to the paralytic, "Your sins are forgiven."
verses.kjv: When Jesus saw their faith, he said unto the sick of the palsy, Son, thy sins be forgiven thee.

verses.greek: ἦσαν δέ τινες τῶν γραμματέων ἐκεῖ καθήμενοι καὶ διαλογιζόμενοι ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις αὐτῶν·
verses.mistral: Some of the scribes were sitting there, and discussing in their hearts.
verses.kjv: But there were certain of the scribes sitting there, and reasoning in their hearts,

verses.greek: Τί οὗτος οὕτως λαλεῖ; ⸀βλασφημεῖ· τίς δύναται ἀφιέναι ἁμαρτίας εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός;
verses.mistral: This man speaks thus with authority; he blasphemes; who can forgive sins but God?
verses.kjv: Why doth this man thus speak blasphemies? who can forgive sins but God only?

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐπιγνοὺς ὁ Ἰησοῦς τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ ὅτι ⸀οὕτως διαλογίζονται ἐν ἑαυτοῖς ⸀λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τί ταῦτα διαλογίζεσθε ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν;
verses.mistral: Immediately knowing in His spirit that they thus reasoned within themselves, He said to them: "What do you reason among yourselves?"
verses.kjv: And immediately when Jesus perceived in his spirit that they so reasoned within themselves, he said unto them, Why reason ye these things in your hearts?

verses.greek: τί ἐστιν εὐκοπώτερον, εἰπεῖν τῷ παραλυτικῷ· ⸀Ἀφίενταί σου αἱ ἁμαρτίαι, ἢ εἰπεῖν· ⸀Ἔγειρε καὶ ἆρον ⸂τὸν κράβαττόν σου⸃ καὶ περιπάτει;
verses.mistral: Forgiven are your sins, or stand up, pick up your mat and walk.
verses.kjv: Whether is it easier to say to the sick of the palsy, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and take up thy bed, and walk?

verses.greek: ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε ὅτι ἐξουσίαν ἔχει ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ⸂ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἀφιέναι ἁμαρτίας⸃— λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ·
verses.mistral: The Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins. He says to the paralytic
verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (he saith to the sick of the palsy,)

verses.greek: Σοὶ λέγω, ⸀ἔγειρε ἆρον τὸν κράβαττόν σου καὶ ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου.
verses.mistral: Rise, take up your bed and go to your house.
verses.kjv: I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy bed, and go thy way into thine house.

verses.greek: καὶ ἠγέρθη ⸂καὶ εὐθὺς⸃ ἄρας τὸν κράβαττον ἐξῆλθεν ⸀ἔμπροσθεν πάντων, ὥστε ἐξίστασθαι πάντας καὶ δοξάζειν τὸν θεὸν λέγοντας ὅτι ⸂Οὕτως οὐδέποτε⸃ εἴδομεν.
verses.mistral: And he arose, taking up his bed, and went out before them all; so that they were amazed and glorified God, saying, "We have never seen this before."
verses.kjv: And immediately he arose, took up the bed, and went forth before them all; insomuch that they were all amazed, and glorified God, saying, We never saw it on this fashion.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξῆλθεν πάλιν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν· καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ἤρχετο πρὸς αὐτόν, καὶ ἐδίδασκεν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Returned again by the sea; and all the crowd came to him, and he taught them.
verses.kjv: And he went forth again by the sea side; and all the multitude resorted unto him, and he taught them.

verses.greek: καὶ παράγων εἶδεν Λευὶν τὸν τοῦ Ἁλφαίου καθήμενον ἐπὶ τὸ τελώνιον, καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Ἀκολούθει μοι. καὶ ἀναστὰς ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And passing along, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the tax collector's booth; and he said to him, "Follow me." And getting up, he followed him.
verses.kjv: And as he passed by, he saw Levi the son of Alphaeus sitting at the receipt of custom, and said unto him, Follow me. And he arose and followed him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀γίνεται κατακεῖσθαι αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ αὐτοῦ, καὶ πολλοὶ τελῶναι καὶ ἁμαρτωλοὶ συνανέκειντο τῷ Ἰησοῦ καὶ τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ, ἦσαν γὰρ πολλοὶ καὶ ⸀ἠκολούθουν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And it came to pass that he was staying in his own house; and many sinners and tax collectors were gathered around Jesus and his disciples, for there were many, and they followed him.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as Jesus sat at meat in his house, many publicans and sinners sat also together with Jesus and his disciples: for there were many, and they followed him.

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς ⸂τῶν Φαρισαίων⸃ ⸀ἰδόντες ⸂ὅτι ἐσθίει⸃ μετὰ τῶν ⸂ἁμαρτωλῶν καὶ τελωνῶν⸃ ἔλεγον τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· ⸀Ὅτι μετὰ τῶν ⸂τελωνῶν καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν⸃ ⸀ἐσθίει;
verses.mistral: Eating with sinners and tax collectors, the scribes of the Pharisees said to his disciples, "Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?"
verses.kjv: And when the scribes and Pharisees saw him eat with publicans and sinners, they said unto his disciples, How is it that he eateth and drinketh with publicans and sinners?

verses.greek: καὶ ἀκούσας ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς ⸀ὅτι Οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν οἱ ἰσχύοντες ἰατροῦ ἀλλ’ οἱ κακῶς ἔχοντες· οὐκ ἦλθον καλέσαι δικαίους ἀλλὰ ⸀ἁμαρτωλούς.
verses.mistral: Healthy people don't need a doctor, but the sick do. I didn't come to call the righteous, but sinners.
verses.kjv: When Jesus heard it, he saith unto them, They that are whole have no need of the physician, but they that are sick: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἦσαν οἱ μαθηταὶ Ἰωάννου καὶ οἱ ⸀Φαρισαῖοι νηστεύοντες. καὶ ἔρχονται καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Διὰ τί οἱ μαθηταὶ Ἰωάννου καὶ οἱ ⸀μαθηταὶ τῶν Φαρισαίων νηστεύουσιν, οἱ δὲ σοὶ μαθηταὶ οὐ νηστεύουσιν;
verses.mistral: And the disciples of John were fasting, and the Pharisees came and said to him, "Why do the disciples of John and the disciples of the Pharisees fast, but yours do not fast?"
verses.kjv: And the disciples of John and of the Pharisees used to fast: and they come and say unto him, Why do the disciples of John and of the Pharisees fast, but thy disciples fast not?

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Μὴ δύνανται οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος ἐν ᾧ ὁ νυμφίος μετ’ αὐτῶν ἐστιν νηστεύειν; ὅσον χρόνον ⸂ἔχουσιν τὸν νυμφίον μετ’ αὐτῶν⸃ οὐ δύνανται νηστεύειν·
verses.mistral: And Jesus said to them, "Can the bridesmaids fast while the bridegroom is with them? So long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast."
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them? as long as they have the bridegroom with them, they cannot fast.

verses.greek: ἐλεύσονται δὲ ἡμέραι ὅταν ἀπαρθῇ ἀπ’ αὐτῶν ὁ νυμφίος, καὶ τότε νηστεύσουσιν ἐν ⸂ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ⸃.
verses.mistral: They will come days when the bridegroom is taken away from them, and then they shall fast in that day.
verses.kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.

verses.greek: ⸀Οὐδεὶς ἐπίβλημα ῥάκους ἀγνάφου ἐπιράπτει ἐπὶ ⸂ἱμάτιον παλαιόν⸃· εἰ δὲ μή, αἴρει τὸ πλήρωμα ⸀ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ τὸ καινὸν τοῦ παλαιοῦ, καὶ χεῖρον σχίσμα γίνεται.
verses.mistral: Old garments are not put with new; else the new makes a rent, and a worse tear is made.
verses.kjv: No man also seweth a piece of new cloth on an old garment: else the new piece that filled it up taketh away from the old, and the rent is made worse.

verses.greek: καὶ οὐδεὶς βάλλει οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς παλαιούς· εἰ δὲ μή, ⸂ῥήξει ὁ οἶνος⸃ τοὺς ἀσκούς, καὶ ὁ οἶνος ⸂ἀπόλλυται καὶ οἱ ἀσκοί⸃. ἀλλὰ οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς ⸀καινούς.
verses.mistral: Does not new wine go into old wineskins? Else the new wine will burst the skins, and both are lost. But new wine is for fresh wineskins.
verses.kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles: else the new wine doth burst the bottles, and the wine is spilled, and the bottles will be marred: but new wine must be put into new bottles.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐγένετο ⸂αὐτὸν ἐν τοῖς σάββασιν παραπορεύεσθαι⸃ διὰ τῶν σπορίμων, καὶ ⸂οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἤρξαντο⸃ ὁδὸν ποιεῖν τίλλοντες τοὺς στάχυας.
verses.mistral: And he went through the cornfields on the sabbath; and his disciples began to make their way plucking the ears of grain.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that he went through the corn fields on the sabbath day; and his disciples began, as they went, to pluck the ears of corn.

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι ἔλεγον αὐτῷ· Ἴδε τί ⸀ποιοῦσιν τοῖς σάββασιν ὃ οὐκ ἔξεστιν;
verses.mistral: And the Pharisees said to him, 'Look, what they are doing on the Sabbath that is not lawful?'
verses.kjv: And the Pharisees said unto him, Behold, why do they on the sabbath day that which is not lawful?

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀λέγει αὐτοῖς· Οὐδέποτε ἀνέγνωτε τί ἐποίησεν Δαυὶδ ὅτε χρείαν ἔσχεν καὶ ἐπείνασεν αὐτὸς καὶ οἱ μετ’ αὐτοῦ;
verses.mistral: Have you never read what David did when he was in need and was hungry, along with those who were with him?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Have ye never read what David did, when he had need, and was an hungred, he, and they that were with him?

verses.greek: ⸀πῶς εἰσῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ θεοῦ ἐπὶ Ἀβιαθὰρ ἀρχιερέως καὶ τοὺς ἄρτους τῆς προθέσεως ἔφαγεν, οὓς οὐκ ἔξεστιν φαγεῖν εἰ μὴ ⸂τοὺς ἱερεῖς⸃, καὶ ἔδωκεν καὶ τοῖς σὺν αὐτῷ οὖσιν;
verses.mistral: Entered into the house of God, during Abiathar the high priest's tenure, he ate the consecrated bread, which is not lawful to eat except by the priests, and gave some also to those who were with him.
verses.kjv: How he went into the house of God in the days of Abiathar the high priest, and did eat the shewbread, which is not lawful to eat but for the priests, and gave also to them which were with him?

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Τὸ σάββατον διὰ τὸν ἄνθρωπον ἐγένετο ⸀καὶ οὐχ ὁ ἄνθρωπος διὰ τὸ σάββατον·
verses.mistral: The man was made for the Sabbath, not the Sabbath for the man.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, The sabbath was made for man, and not man for the sabbath:

verses.greek: ὥστε κύριός ἐστιν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου καὶ τοῦ σαββάτου.
verses.mistral: The Lord is the Son of Man and of Sabbath.
verses.kjv: Therefore the Son of man is Lord also of the sabbath.

verses.greek: Καὶ εἰσῆλθεν πάλιν ⸀εἰς συναγωγήν, καὶ ἦν ἐκεῖ ἄνθρωπος ἐξηραμμένην ἔχων τὴν χεῖρα.
verses.mistral: Entered again into the synagogue, and there was a man with a withered hand.
verses.kjv: And he entered again into the synagogue; and there was a man there which had a withered hand.

verses.greek: καὶ παρετήρουν αὐτὸν εἰ τοῖς σάββασιν θεραπεύσει αὐτόν, ἵνα ⸀κατηγορήσωσιν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: They watched him to see if on the Sabbath he would heal him, so that they might accuse him.
verses.kjv: And they watched him, whether he would heal him on the sabbath day; that they might accuse him.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ τῷ ⸂τὴν χεῖρα ἔχοντι ξηράν⸃· ⸀Ἔγειρε εἰς τὸ μέσον.
verses.mistral: Rise, take your mat and walk
verses.kjv: And he saith unto the man which had the withered hand, Stand forth.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ἔξεστιν τοῖς σάββασιν ⸀ἀγαθοποιῆσαι ἢ κακοποιῆσαι, ψυχὴν σῶσαι ἢ ἀποκτεῖναι; οἱ δὲ ἐσιώπων.
verses.mistral: And he says to them: "Is it lawful on the Sabbath days to do good or to do evil, to save life or to kill?" But they kept silent.
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Is it lawful to do good on the sabbath days, or to do evil? to save life, or to kill? But they held their peace.

verses.greek: καὶ περιβλεψάμενος αὐτοὺς μετ’ ὀργῆς, συλλυπούμενος ἐπὶ τῇ πωρώσει τῆς καρδίας αὐτῶν, λέγει τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ· Ἔκτεινον τὴν ⸀χεῖρα· καὶ ἐξέτεινεν, καὶ ἀπεκατεστάθη ἡ χεὶρ ⸀αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Stretch out your hand And he stretched it out, and his hand was restored
verses.kjv: And when he had looked round about on them with anger, being grieved for the hardness of their hearts, he saith unto the man, Stretch forth thine hand. And he stretched it out: and his hand was restored whole as the other.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξελθόντες οἱ Φαρισαῖοι ⸀εὐθὺς μετὰ τῶν Ἡρῳδιανῶν συμβούλιον ⸀ἐδίδουν κατ’ αὐτοῦ ὅπως αὐτὸν ἀπολέσωσιν.
verses.mistral: And going out, the Pharisees with Herodians conspired against him how to destroy him.
verses.kjv: And the Pharisees went forth, and straightway took counsel with the Herodians against him, how they might destroy him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ⸂μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ἀνεχώρησεν⸃ πρὸς τὴν θάλασσαν· καὶ πολὺ πλῆθος ἀπὸ τῆς Γαλιλαίας ⸀ἠκολούθησεν, καὶ ἀπὸ τῆς Ἰουδαίας
verses.mistral: Jesus, with his disciples, went out to the sea; and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judea.
verses.kjv: But Jesus withdrew himself with his disciples to the sea: and a great multitude from Galilee followed him, and from Judaea,

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπὸ Ἱεροσολύμων καὶ ἀπὸ τῆς Ἰδουμαίας ⸀καὶ πέραν τοῦ Ἰορδάνου ⸁καὶ περὶ Τύρον καὶ Σιδῶνα, πλῆθος πολύ, ⸀ἀκούοντες ὅσα ⸀ἐποίει ἦλθον πρὸς αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Came also from Jerusalem and Judea beyond the Jordan, and from Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, hearing what things he did.
verses.kjv: And from Jerusalem, and from Idumaea, and from beyond Jordan; and they about Tyre and Sidon, a great multitude, when they had heard what great things he did, came unto him.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ἵνα πλοιάριον προσκαρτερῇ αὐτῷ διὰ τὸν ὄχλον ἵνα μὴ θλίβωσιν αὐτόν·
verses.mistral: Bring them some figures to sit all around him, so that the crowd may not press upon him.
verses.kjv: And he spake to his disciples, that a small ship should wait on him because of the multitude, lest they should throng him.

verses.greek: πολλοὺς γὰρ ἐθεράπευσεν, ὥστε ἐπιπίπτειν αὐτῷ ἵνα αὐτοῦ ἅψωνται ὅσοι εἶχον μάστιγας.
verses.mistral: Many he healed, so that they might touch him; and all those with sickles.
verses.kjv: For he had healed many; insomuch that they pressed upon him for to touch him, as many as had plagues.

verses.greek: καὶ τὰ πνεύματα τὰ ἀκάθαρτα, ὅταν αὐτὸν ⸂ἐθεώρουν, προσέπιπτον⸃ αὐτῷ καὶ ⸀ἔκραζον ⸀λέγοντα ὅτι Σὺ εἶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: And the unclean spirits, when they saw Him, fell down before Him and cried out, saying, "You are the Son of God."
verses.kjv: And unclean spirits, when they saw him, fell down before him, and cried, saying, Thou art the Son of God.

verses.greek: καὶ πολλὰ ἐπετίμα αὐτοῖς ἵνα μὴ ⸂αὐτὸν φανερὸν⸃ ⸀ποιήσωσιν.
verses.mistral: Command them to keep silent and not to make Him known.
verses.kjv: And he straitly charged them that they should not make him known.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀναβαίνει εἰς τὸ ὄρος καὶ προσκαλεῖται οὓς ἤθελεν αὐτός, καὶ ἀπῆλθον πρὸς αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Ascends to the mountain and calls those whom He wanted, and they went to Him.
verses.kjv: And he goeth up into a mountain, and calleth unto him whom he would: and they came unto him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐποίησεν ⸀δώδεκα, ἵνα ὦσιν μετ’ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἵνα ἀποστέλλῃ αὐτοὺς κηρύσσειν
verses.mistral: And He appointed twelve, so that they might be with Him and that He might send them out to preach
verses.kjv: And he ordained twelve, that they should be with him, and that he might send them forth to preach,

verses.greek: καὶ ἔχειν ⸀ἐξουσίαν ἐκβάλλειν τὰ δαιμόνια·
verses.mistral: Has authority to drive out unclean spirits
verses.kjv: And to have power to heal sicknesses, and to cast out devils:

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ἐποίησεν τοὺς δώδεκα, καὶ⸃ ἐπέθηκεν ⸂ὄνομα τῷ Σίμωνι⸃ Πέτρον,
verses.mistral: Made the twelve, and named Simon Peter.
verses.kjv: And Simon he surnamed Peter;

verses.greek: καὶ Ἰάκωβον τὸν τοῦ Ζεβεδαίου καὶ Ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν τοῦ Ἰακώβου (καὶ ἐπέθηκεν αὐτοῖς ⸀ὀνόματα Βοανηργές, ὅ ἐστιν Υἱοὶ Βροντῆς),
verses.mistral: And James the son of Zebedee and John the brother of James (and he gave them the names Boanerges, which means Sons of Thunder).
verses.kjv: And James the son of Zebedee, and John the brother of James; and he surnamed them Boanerges, which is, The sons of thunder:

verses.greek: καὶ Ἀνδρέαν καὶ Φίλιππον καὶ Βαρθολομαῖον καὶ Μαθθαῖον καὶ Θωμᾶν καὶ Ἰάκωβον τὸν τοῦ Ἁλφαίου καὶ Θαδδαῖον καὶ Σίμωνα τὸν ⸀Καναναῖον
verses.mistral: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean
verses.kjv: And Andrew, and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Matthew, and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus, and Simon the Canaanite,

verses.greek: καὶ Ἰούδαν ⸀Ἰσκαριώθ, ὃς καὶ παρέδωκεν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And Judas Iscariot, who also betrayed him.
verses.kjv: And Judas Iscariot, which also betrayed him: and they went into an house.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀ἔρχεται εἰς οἶκον· καὶ συνέρχεται πάλιν ⸀ὁ ὄχλος, ὥστε μὴ δύνασθαι αὐτοὺς ⸀μηδὲ ἄρτον φαγεῖν.
verses.mistral: And he enters the house; and again the crowd gathers, so that they could not even eat bread.
verses.kjv: And the multitude cometh together again, so that they could not so much as eat bread.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ παρ’ αὐτοῦ ἐξῆλθον κρατῆσαι αὐτόν, ἔλεγον γὰρ ὅτι ἐξέστη.
verses.mistral: They went out to seize Him, saying, "He is out of His mind."
verses.kjv: And when his friends heard of it, they went out to lay hold on him: for they said, He is beside himself.

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς οἱ ἀπὸ Ἱεροσολύμων καταβάντες ἔλεγον ὅτι Βεελζεβοὺλ ἔχει καὶ ὅτι ἐν τῷ ἄρχοντι τῶν δαιμονίων ἐκβάλλει τὰ δαιμόνια.
verses.mistral: And the scribes who came down from Jerusalem were saying, "He has Beelzebul, and by the chief of the demons he casts out demons."
verses.kjv: ¶ And the scribes which came down from Jerusalem said, He hath Beelzebub, and by the prince of the devils casteth he out devils.

verses.greek: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος αὐτοὺς ἐν παραβολαῖς ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Πῶς δύναται Σατανᾶς Σατανᾶν ἐκβάλλειν;
verses.mistral: How can Satan cast out Satan?
verses.kjv: And he called them unto him, and said unto them in parables, How can Satan cast out Satan?

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν βασιλεία ἐφ’ ἑαυτὴν μερισθῇ, οὐ δύναται σταθῆναι ἡ βασιλεία ἐκείνη·
verses.mistral: Cannot a kingdom divide against itself, and a house against itself?
verses.kjv: And if a kingdom be divided against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν οἰκία ἐφ’ ἑαυτὴν μερισθῇ, οὐ ⸀δυνήσεται ⸂ἡ οἰκία ἐκείνη σταθῆναι⸃·
verses.mistral: And if a house divides against itself, it cannot stand.
verses.kjv: And if a house be divided against itself, that house cannot stand.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ ὁ Σατανᾶς ἀνέστη ἐφ’ ἑαυτὸν καὶ ⸀ἐμερίσθη, οὐ δύναται ⸀στῆναι ἀλλὰ τέλος ἔχει.
verses.mistral: And if Satan rose up against himself and divided, he cannot stand but has an end.
verses.kjv: And if Satan rise up against himself, and be divided, he cannot stand, but hath an end.

verses.greek: ⸂ἀλλ’ οὐδεὶς δύναται⸃ ⸂εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν τοῦ ἰσχυροῦ εἰσελθὼν τὰ σκεύη⸃ αὐτοῦ διαρπάσαι ἐὰν μὴ πρῶτον τὸν ἰσχυρὸν δήσῃ, καὶ τότε τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ ⸀διαρπάσει.
verses.mistral: Cannot enter a strong man's house and plunder his possessions unless he first binds the strong man, and then he will plunder his house.
verses.kjv: No man can enter into a strong man's house, and spoil his goods, except he will first bind the strong man; and then he will spoil his house.

verses.greek: Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πάντα ἀφεθήσεται ⸂τοῖς υἱοῖς τῶν ἀνθρώπων, τὰ ἁμαρτήματα⸃ καὶ ⸂αἱ βλασφημίαι ὅσα ἐὰν⸃ βλασφημήσωσιν·
verses.mistral: All sins and blasphemies will be forgiven them (Translated from the Greek: "Amen dico vobis, hoc est, omnia aperientur peccatis hominum, et haec blasphemiarum quae quicquid commiserint")
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, All sins shall be forgiven unto the sons of men, and blasphemies wherewith soever they shall blaspheme:

verses.greek: ὃς δ’ ἂν βλασφημήσῃ εἰς τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον, οὐκ ἔχει ἄφεσιν εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα, ἀλλὰ ἔνοχός ἐστιν αἰωνίου ⸀ἁμαρτήματος.
verses.mistral: Whoever blasphemes against the Holy Spirit never has forgiveness, but is subject to eternal condemnation.
verses.kjv: But he that shall blaspheme against the Holy Ghost hath never forgiveness, but is in danger of eternal damnation:

verses.greek: ὅτι ἔλεγον· Πνεῦμα ἀκάθαρτον ἔχει.
verses.mistral: Has an unclean spirit.
verses.kjv: Because they said, He hath an unclean spirit.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ ἔρχονται⸃ ⸂ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ⸃ καὶ ἔξω ⸀στήκοντες ἀπέστειλαν πρὸς αὐτὸν ⸀καλοῦντες αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And come his mother and his brothers, and standing without sent to him calling him.
verses.kjv: ¶ There came then his brethren and his mother, and, standing without, sent unto him, calling him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐκάθητο ⸂περὶ αὐτὸν ὄχλος⸃, ⸂καὶ λέγουσιν⸃ αὐτῷ· Ἰδοὺ ἡ μήτηρ σου καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοί ⸀σου ἔξω ζητοῦσίν σε.
verses.mistral: And a crowd sat around him, and they said to him, "Behold, your mother and your brothers are outside seeking you."
verses.kjv: And the multitude sat about him, and they said unto him, Behold, thy mother and thy brethren without seek for thee.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ἀποκριθεὶς αὐτοῖς λέγει⸃· Τίς ἐστιν ἡ μήτηρ μου ⸀ἢ οἱ ἀδελφοί ⸀μου;
verses.mistral: Who is my mother, or my brothers?
verses.kjv: And he answered them, saying, Who is my mother, or my brethren?

verses.greek: καὶ περιβλεψάμενος ⸂τοὺς περὶ αὐτὸν κύκλῳ⸃ καθημένους λέγει· Ἴδε ἡ μήτηρ μου καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοί μου·
verses.kjv: And he looked round about on them which sat about him, and said, Behold my mother and my brethren!

verses.greek: ὃς ⸀γὰρ ἂν ποιήσῃ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ θεοῦ, οὗτος ἀδελφός μου καὶ ⸀ἀδελφὴ καὶ μήτηρ ἐστίν.
verses.mistral: Whoever does the will of God is my brother and sister and mother.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall do the will of God, the same is my brother, and my sister, and mother.

verses.greek: Καὶ πάλιν ἤρξατο διδάσκειν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν. καὶ ⸀συνάγεται πρὸς αὐτὸν ὄχλος ⸀πλεῖστος, ὥστε αὐτὸν ⸂εἰς πλοῖον ἐμβάντα⸃ καθῆσθαι ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ, καὶ πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος πρὸς τὴν θάλασσαν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ⸀ἦσαν.
verses.mistral: Again he began to teach by the sea. And a great crowd gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat and sat in the sea, and all the crowd was on the land toward the sea.
verses.kjv: And he began again to teach by the sea side: and there was gathered unto him a great multitude, so that he entered into a ship, and sat in the sea; and the whole multitude was by the sea on the land.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐδίδασκεν αὐτοὺς ἐν παραβολαῖς πολλά καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς ἐν τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: And he taught them in parables many and said to them in His teaching:
verses.kjv: And he taught them many things by parables, and said unto them in his doctrine,

verses.greek: Ἀκούετε. ἰδοὺ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ ⸀σπείρων σπεῖραι.
verses.mistral: Listen! The sower went out to sow.
verses.kjv: Hearken; Behold, there went out a sower to sow:

verses.greek: καὶ ἐγένετο ἐν τῷ σπείρειν ὃ μὲν ἔπεσεν παρὰ τὴν ὁδόν, καὶ ἦλθεν τὰ πετεινὰ καὶ κατέφαγεν αὐτό.
verses.mistral: And as he sowed, some seed fell by the wayside, and the birds came and devoured it.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he sowed, some fell by the way side, and the fowls of the air came and devoured it up.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἄλλο⸃ ἔπεσεν ἐπὶ τὸ πετρῶδες ⸀ὅπου οὐκ εἶχεν γῆν πολλήν, καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐξανέτειλεν διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν βάθος γῆς·
verses.mistral: Some fell among the rocks, where it had not much soil, and immediately sprang up because it had no depth of soil.
verses.kjv: And some fell on stony ground, where it had not much earth; and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of earth:

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ὅτε ἀνέτειλεν ὁ ἥλιος⸃ ⸀ἐκαυματίσθη καὶ διὰ τὸ μὴ ἔχειν ῥίζαν ἐξηράνθη.
verses.mistral: When the sun rose, it was scorched and because it had no root, it withered away.
verses.kjv: But when the sun was up, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.

verses.greek: καὶ ἄλλο ἔπεσεν εἰς τὰς ἀκάνθας, καὶ ἀνέβησαν αἱ ἄκανθαι καὶ συνέπνιξαν αὐτό, καὶ καρπὸν οὐκ ἔδωκεν.
verses.mistral: Another fell into the thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked him, and it bore no fruit.
verses.kjv: And some fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἄλλα ἔπεσεν εἰς τὴν γῆν τὴν καλήν, καὶ ἐδίδου καρπὸν ἀναβαίνοντα καὶ ⸀αὐξανόμενα, καὶ ἔφερεν ⸂ἓν τριάκοντα καὶ ἓν ἑξήκοντα καὶ ἓν⸃ ἑκατόν.
verses.mistral: And another fell on good ground, and yielded a crop bearing sixty and thirty and one hundredfold.
verses.kjv: And other fell on good ground, and did yield fruit that sprang up and increased; and brought forth, some thirty, and some sixty, and some an hundred.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν· ⸂Ὃς ἔχει⸃ ὦτα ἀκούειν ἀκουέτω.
verses.mistral: Whoever has ears to hear, let them hear.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ ὅτε⸃ ἐγένετο κατὰ μόνας, ⸀ἠρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ περὶ αὐτὸν σὺν τοῖς δώδεκα ⸂τὰς παραβολάς⸃.
verses.mistral: And when they were alone, they asked him about the parables.
verses.kjv: And when he was alone, they that were about him with the twelve asked of him the parable.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Ὑμῖν ⸂τὸ μυστήριον δέδοται⸃ τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ· ἐκείνοις δὲ τοῖς ἔξω ἐν παραβολαῖς τὰ πάντα γίνεται,
verses.mistral: Given: To you it has been given the mystery of God's kingdom; but to those outside everything is in parables.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Unto you it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of God: but unto them that are without, all these things are done in parables:

verses.greek: ἵνα βλέποντες βλέπωσι καὶ μὴ ἴδωσιν, καὶ ἀκούοντες ἀκούωσι καὶ μὴ συνιῶσιν, μήποτε ἐπιστρέψωσιν καὶ ἀφεθῇ ⸀αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: Seeing they may see and not perceive, Hearing they may hear and not understand, Lest they turn again and their sins be forgiven.
verses.kjv: That seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest at any time they should be converted, and their sins should be forgiven them.

verses.greek: Καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Οὐκ οἴδατε τὴν παραβολὴν ταύτην, καὶ πῶς πάσας τὰς παραβολὰς γνώσεσθε;
verses.mistral: Do you not understand this parable, and how will you understand all the parables?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Know ye not this parable? and how then will ye know all parables?

verses.greek: ὁ σπείρων τὸν λόγον σπείρει.
verses.mistral: Sows the seed.
verses.kjv: ¶ The sower soweth the word.

verses.greek: οὗτοι δέ εἰσιν οἱ παρὰ τὴν ὁδὸν ὅπου σπείρεται ὁ λόγος, καὶ ὅταν ἀκούσωσιν ⸀εὐθὺς ἔρχεται ὁ Σατανᾶς καὶ αἴρει τὸν λόγον τὸν ἐσπαρμένον ⸂εἰς αὐτούς⸃.
verses.mistral: These are the ones along the path where the word is sown, and when they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
verses.kjv: And these are they by the way side, where the word is sown; but when they have heard, Satan cometh immediately, and taketh away the word that was sown in their hearts.

verses.greek: καὶ οὗτοί εἰσιν ⸀ὁμοίως οἱ ἐπὶ τὰ πετρώδη σπειρόμενοι, οἳ ὅταν ἀκούσωσιν τὸν λόγον ⸀εὐθὺς μετὰ χαρᾶς λαμβάνουσιν αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: And these are like those on the rocky ground, who when they hear the word with joy receive it.
verses.kjv: And these are they likewise which are sown on stony ground; who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with gladness;

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἔχουσιν ῥίζαν ἐν ἑαυτοῖς ἀλλὰ πρόσκαιροί εἰσιν, εἶτα γενομένης θλίψεως ἢ διωγμοῦ διὰ τὸν λόγον ⸀εὐθὺς σκανδαλίζονται.
verses.mistral: Immediately stumble or fall away Mark 4:17 (Koine Greek to English translation): And they have not root in themselves, but are temporary; then, when affliction or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble or fall away.
verses.kjv: And have no root in themselves, and so endure but for a time: afterward, when affliction or persecution ariseth for the word's sake, immediately they are offended.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἄλλοι εἰσὶν οἱ εἰς τὰς ἀκάνθας σπειρόμενοι· ⸂οὗτοί εἰσιν⸃ οἱ τὸν λόγον ⸀ἀκούσαντες,
verses.mistral: Others are the ones who are sown among the thorns. These are the ones who have heard the word.
verses.kjv: And these are they which are sown among thorns; such as hear the word,

verses.greek: καὶ αἱ μέριμναι τοῦ ⸀αἰῶνος καὶ ἡ ἀπάτη τοῦ πλούτου καὶ αἱ περὶ τὰ λοιπὰ ἐπιθυμίαι εἰσπορευόμεναι συμπνίγουσιν τὸν λόγον, καὶ ἄκαρπος γίνεται.
verses.mistral: The worries of this world and the deceitfulness of riches and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
verses.kjv: And the cares of this world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in, choke the word, and it becometh unfruitful.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἐκεῖνοί εἰσιν οἱ ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν τὴν καλὴν σπαρέντες, οἵτινες ἀκούουσιν τὸν λόγον καὶ παραδέχονται καὶ καρποφοροῦσιν ⸂ἓν τριάκοντα καὶ ἓν ἑξήκοντα καὶ ἓν⸃ ἑκατόν.
verses.mistral: And those who are sowing seeds on the good earth are those who hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold.
verses.kjv: And these are they which are sown on good ground; such as hear the word, and receive it, and bring forth fruit, some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some an hundred.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔλεγεν ⸀αὐτοῖς· Μήτι ⸂ἔρχεται ὁ λύχνος⸃ ἵνα ὑπὸ τὸν μόδιον τεθῇ ἢ ὑπὸ τὴν κλίνην, οὐχ ἵνα ἐπὶ τὴν λυχνίαν ⸀τεθῇ;
verses.mistral: And he said to them: Is not the lamp brought in order to be set upon a stand, or under a bed? Rather, it is put upon a lampstand.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he said unto them, Is a candle brought to be put under a bushel, or under a bed? and not to be set on a candlestick?

verses.greek: οὐ γάρ ἐστιν ⸀κρυπτὸν ἐὰν μὴ ⸀ἵνα φανερωθῇ, οὐδὲ ἐγένετο ἀπόκρυφον ἀλλ’ ἵνα ⸂ἔλθῃ εἰς φανερόν⸃.
verses.mistral: Is not that which is hidden, brought forth to be revealed? Nor did it become hidden, but rather, for it to come into the open.
verses.kjv: For there is nothing hid, which shall not be manifested; neither was any thing kept secret, but that it should come abroad.

verses.greek: εἴ τις ἔχει ὦτα ἀκούειν ἀκουέτω.
verses.mistral: Listen.
verses.kjv: If any man have ears to hear, let him hear.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Βλέπετε τί ἀκούετε. ἐν ᾧ μέτρῳ μετρεῖτε μετρηθήσεται ὑμῖν καὶ προστεθήσεται ⸀ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: And he said to them, "What you measure out will be measured to you."
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Take heed what ye hear: with what measure ye mete, it shall be measured to you: and unto you that hear shall more be given.

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ ⸀ἔχει, δοθήσεται αὐτῷ· καὶ ὃς οὐκ ἔχει, καὶ ⸀ὃ ἔχει ἀρθήσεται ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Whoever has, will be given more; and whoever does not have, even what they have will be taken from them.
verses.kjv: For he that hath, to him shall be given: and he that hath not, from him shall be taken even that which he hath.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔλεγεν· Οὕτως ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ ⸀ὡς ἄνθρωπος βάλῃ τὸν σπόρον ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς
verses.mistral: The kingdom of God is as a man that casteth seed upon the earth.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, So is the kingdom of God, as if a man should cast seed into the ground;

verses.greek: καὶ καθεύδῃ καὶ ἐγείρηται νύκτα καὶ ἡμέραν, καὶ ὁ σπόρος ⸀βλαστᾷ καὶ μηκύνηται ὡς οὐκ οἶδεν αὐτός.
verses.mistral: And day by day, night and day, the seed sprouts and grows—he does not know how.
verses.kjv: And should sleep, and rise night and day, and the seed should spring and grow up, he knoweth not how.

verses.greek: ⸀αὐτομάτη ἡ γῆ καρποφορεῖ, πρῶτον χόρτον, ⸂εἶτα στάχυν, εἶτα⸃ ⸂πλήρης σῖτον⸃ ἐν τῷ στάχυϊ.
verses.mistral: The earth produces seed by itself; first the grain, then the ears of corn, then full grain in the ears.
verses.kjv: For the earth bringeth forth fruit of herself; first the blade, then the ear, after that the full corn in the ear.

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ ⸀παραδοῖ ὁ καρπός, ⸀εὐθὺς ἀποστέλλει τὸ δρέπανον, ὅτι παρέστηκεν ὁ θερισμός.
verses.mistral: When the harvest is ready, immediately the reaper comes, for the harvest has come.
verses.kjv: But when the fruit is brought forth, immediately he putteth in the sickle, because the harvest is come.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔλεγεν· ⸀Πῶς ὁμοιώσωμεν τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ, ἢ ἐν ⸀τίνι ⸂αὐτὴν παραβολῇ θῶμεν⸃;
verses.mistral: And he said, "How shall we compare the kingdom of God, or in what parable shall we put it?"
verses.kjv: ¶ And he said, Whereunto shall we liken the kingdom of God? or with what comparison shall we compare it?

verses.greek: ὡς ⸀κόκκῳ σινάπεως, ὃς ὅταν σπαρῇ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, ⸂μικρότερον ὂν⸃ πάντων τῶν ⸀σπερμάτων τῶν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς—
verses.mistral: Smaller than all the seeds that are on earth is the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, is less than all the seeds that be in the earth:

verses.greek: καὶ ὅταν σπαρῇ, ἀναβαίνει καὶ γίνεται ⸂μεῖζον πάντων τῶν λαχάνων⸃ καὶ ποιεῖ κλάδους μεγάλους, ὥστε δύνασθαι ὑπὸ τὴν σκιὰν αὐτοῦ τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ κατασκηνοῦν.
verses.mistral: And when it is sown, it grows up and becomes greater than all the garden plants, and it produces large branches, so that birds of the sky can nest under its shade.
verses.kjv: But when it is sown, it groweth up, and becometh greater than all herbs, and shooteth out great branches; so that the fowls of the air may lodge under the shadow of it.

verses.greek: Καὶ τοιαύταις παραβολαῖς πολλαῖς ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς τὸν λόγον, καθὼς ἠδύναντο ἀκούειν·
verses.mistral: With many such parables He spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear.
verses.kjv: And with many such parables spake he the word unto them, as they were able to hear it.

verses.greek: χωρὶς δὲ παραβολῆς οὐκ ἐλάλει αὐτοῖς, κατ’ ἰδίαν δὲ τοῖς ⸂ἰδίοις μαθηταῖς⸃ ἐπέλυεν πάντα.
verses.mistral: He does not speak to them without a parable, but privately to his own disciples he explained all things.
verses.kjv: But without a parable spake he not unto them: and when they were alone, he expounded all things to his disciples.

verses.greek: Καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ὀψίας γενομένης· Διέλθωμεν εἰς τὸ πέραν.
verses.mistral: Go into the boat
verses.kjv: And the same day, when the even was come, he saith unto them, Let us pass over unto the other side.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀφέντες τὸν ὄχλον παραλαμβάνουσιν αὐτὸν ὡς ἦν ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ, καὶ ἄλλα ⸀πλοῖα ἦν μετ’ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Leaving the crowd, they took him with them as he was in the boat, and other boats were with him.
verses.kjv: And when they had sent away the multitude, they took him even as he was in the ship. And there were also with him other little ships.

verses.greek: καὶ γίνεται λαῖλαψ ⸂μεγάλη ἀνέμου⸃, ⸂καὶ τὰ⸃ κύματα ἐπέβαλλεν εἰς τὸ πλοῖον, ὥστε ⸂ἤδη γεμίζεσθαι τὸ πλοῖον⸃.
verses.mistral: And a great windstorm arose, and the waves were driving into the boat, so that the boat was already being filled.
verses.kjv: And there arose a great storm of wind, and the waves beat into the ship, so that it was now full.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂αὐτὸς ἦν⸃ ⸀ἐν τῇ πρύμνῃ ἐπὶ τὸ προσκεφάλαιον καθεύδων· καὶ ⸀ἐγείρουσιν αὐτὸν καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Διδάσκαλε, οὐ μέλει σοι ὅτι ἀπολλύμεθα;
verses.mistral: He was sleeping on a pillow, his head, and they woke him up and said to him, "Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?"
verses.kjv: And he was in the hinder part of the ship, asleep on a pillow: and they awake him, and say unto him, Master, carest thou not that we perish?

verses.greek: καὶ διεγερθεὶς ἐπετίμησεν τῷ ἀνέμῳ καὶ εἶπεν τῇ θαλάσσῃ· Σιώπα, πεφίμωσο. καὶ ἐκόπασεν ὁ ἄνεμος, καὶ ἐγένετο γαλήνη μεγάλη.
verses.mistral: And awaking, He rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Be still, be quiet." And the wind ceased, and there was made a great calm.
verses.kjv: And he arose, and rebuked the wind, and said unto the sea, Peace, be still. And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί δειλοί ἐστε; ⸀οὔπω ἔχετε πίστιν;
verses.mistral: Have you no faith yet?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Why are ye so fearful? how is it that ye have no faith?

verses.greek: καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν φόβον μέγαν, καὶ ἔλεγον πρὸς ἀλλήλους· Τίς ἄρα οὗτός ἐστιν ὅτι καὶ ὁ ἄνεμος καὶ ἡ θάλασσα ⸀ὑπακούει αὐτῷ;
verses.mistral: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, "Who is this that even the wind and the sea obey him?"
verses.kjv: And they feared exceedingly, and said one to another, What manner of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?

verses.greek: Καὶ ἦλθον εἰς τὸ πέραν τῆς θαλάσσης εἰς τὴν χώραν τῶν ⸀Γερασηνῶν.
verses.mistral: Came to the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gerasenes.
verses.kjv: And they came over unto the other side of the sea, into the country of the Gadarenes.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ἐξελθόντος αὐτοῦ⸃ ἐκ τοῦ πλοίου ⸂εὐθὺς ὑπήντησεν⸃ αὐτῷ ἐκ τῶν μνημείων ἄνθρωπος ἐν πνεύματι ἀκαθάρτῳ,
verses.mistral: Emerged from the boat, a man immediately approached Him out of the tombs, with an unclean spirit.
verses.kjv: And when he was come out of the ship, immediately there met him out of the tombs a man with an unclean spirit,

verses.greek: ὃς τὴν κατοίκησιν εἶχεν ἐν τοῖς μνήμασιν, καὶ ⸂οὐδὲ ἁλύσει οὐκέτι⸃ οὐδεὶς ἐδύνατο αὐτὸν δῆσαι
verses.mistral: Had no one been able to bind him
verses.kjv: Who had his dwelling among the tombs; and no man could bind him, no, not with chains:

verses.greek: διὰ τὸ αὐτὸν πολλάκις πέδαις καὶ ἁλύσεσι δεδέσθαι καὶ διεσπάσθαι ὑπ’ αὐτοῦ τὰς ἁλύσεις καὶ τὰς πέδας συντετρῖφθαι, καὶ οὐδεὶς ⸂ἴσχυεν αὐτὸν⸃ δαμάσαι·
verses.mistral: Could not be subdued by chains and restraints, for he would often break them apart, and no one was able to tame him.
verses.kjv: Because that he had been often bound with fetters and chains, and the chains had been plucked asunder by him, and the fetters broken in pieces: neither could any man tame him.

verses.greek: καὶ διὰ παντὸς νυκτὸς καὶ ἡμέρας ἐν τοῖς ⸂μνήμασιν καὶ ἐν τοῖς ὄρεσιν⸃ ἦν κράζων καὶ κατακόπτων ἑαυτὸν λίθοις.
verses.mistral: And through all night and day in the tombs and in the mountains he was crying out and cutting himself with stones.
verses.kjv: And always, night and day, he was in the mountains, and in the tombs, crying, and cutting himself with stones.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἰδὼν⸃ τὸν Ἰησοῦν ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ἔδραμεν καὶ προσεκύνησεν ⸀αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Approaching from afar, Jesus saw him and he ran up and fell at his feet.
verses.kjv: But when he saw Jesus afar off, he ran and worshipped him,

verses.greek: καὶ κράξας φωνῇ μεγάλῃ ⸀λέγει· Τί ἐμοὶ καὶ σοί, Ἰησοῦ υἱὲ τοῦ θεοῦ τοῦ ὑψίστου; ὁρκίζω σε τὸν θεόν, μή με βασανίσῃς.
verses.mistral: And crying out with a loud voice, he said: "What do we have to do with each other, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I swear to God, do not put me to the test."
verses.kjv: And cried with a loud voice, and said, What have I to do with thee, Jesus, thou Son of the most high God? I adjure thee by God, that thou torment me not.

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ αὐτῷ· Ἔξελθε τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἀκάθαρτον ἐκ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου.
verses.mistral: Exorcise the unclean spirit from the man.
verses.kjv: For he said unto him, Come out of the man, thou unclean spirit.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτα αὐτόν· Τί ⸂ὄνομά σοι⸃; καὶ ⸂λέγει αὐτῷ⸃· ⸀Λεγιὼν ὄνομά μοι, ὅτι πολλοί ἐσμεν·
verses.mistral: My name is Legion, for we are many.
verses.kjv: And he asked him, What is thy name? And he answered, saying, My name is Legion: for we are many.

verses.greek: καὶ παρεκάλει αὐτὸν πολλὰ ἵνα μὴ ⸀αὐτὰ ἀποστείλῃ ἔξω τῆς χώρας.
verses.mistral: And He begged Him much that He might not send them away out of the country.
verses.kjv: And he besought him much that he would not send them away out of the country.

verses.greek: ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ πρὸς τῷ ὄρει ἀγέλη χοίρων μεγάλη βοσκομένη·
verses.mistral: There was a large herd of swine near the hill.
verses.kjv: Now there was there nigh unto the mountains a great herd of swine feeding.

verses.greek: καὶ παρεκάλεσαν ⸀αὐτὸν λέγοντες· Πέμψον ἡμᾶς εἰς τοὺς χοίρους, ἵνα εἰς αὐτοὺς εἰσέλθωμεν.
verses.mistral: Call on him to send us into the swine, that we may enter them.
verses.kjv: And all the devils besought him, saying, Send us into the swine, that we may enter into them.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπέτρεψεν ⸀αὐτοῖς. καὶ ἐξελθόντα τὰ πνεύματα τὰ ἀκάθαρτα εἰσῆλθον εἰς τοὺς χοίρους, καὶ ὥρμησεν ἡ ἀγέλη κατὰ τοῦ κρημνοῦ εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν, ⸀ὡς δισχίλιοι, καὶ ἐπνίγοντο ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ.
verses.mistral: And he permitted them. And the unclean spirits went out and entered the swine; and the herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea, about two thousand; and they were suffocating in the sea.
verses.kjv: And forthwith Jesus gave them leave. And the unclean spirits went out, and entered into the swine: and the herd ran violently down a steep place into the sea, (they were about two thousand;) and were choked in the sea.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ οἱ⸃ βόσκοντες ⸀αὐτοὺς ἔφυγον καὶ ⸀ἀπήγγειλαν εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ εἰς τοὺς ἀγρούς· καὶ ⸀ἦλθον ἰδεῖν τί ἐστιν τὸ γεγονός.
verses.mistral: And those who tended them fled and reported in the city and the countryside, and they came to see what had happened.
verses.kjv: And they that fed the swine fled, and told it in the city, and in the country. And they went out to see what it was that was done.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔρχονται πρὸς τὸν Ἰησοῦν, καὶ θεωροῦσιν τὸν δαιμονιζόμενον ⸀καθήμενον ἱματισμένον καὶ σωφρονοῦντα, τὸν ἐσχηκότα τὸν ⸀λεγιῶνα, καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν.
verses.mistral: And they came to Jesus, and seeing the one who was possessed by the legion and clothed and in his right mind, and having received the swine, they were afraid.
verses.kjv: And they come to Jesus, and see him that was possessed with the devil, and had the legion, sitting, and clothed, and in his right mind: and they were afraid.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ διηγήσαντο⸃ αὐτοῖς οἱ ἰδόντες πῶς ἐγένετο τῷ δαιμονιζομένῳ καὶ περὶ τῶν χοίρων.
verses.mistral: And they told the others how the demon-possessed man had been restored to health regarding the pigs.
verses.kjv: And they that saw it told them how it befell to him that was possessed with the devil, and also concerning the swine.

verses.greek: καὶ ἤρξαντο παρακαλεῖν αὐτὸν ἀπελθεῖν ἀπὸ τῶν ὁρίων αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Began to entreat him to depart from their borders.
verses.kjv: And they began to pray him to depart out of their coasts.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἐμβαίνοντος αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ πλοῖον παρεκάλει αὐτὸν ὁ δαιμονισθεὶς ἵνα ⸂μετ’ αὐτοῦ ᾖ⸃.
verses.mistral: And as He was getting into the boat, the demon-possessed man begged Him to let him go with Him.
verses.kjv: And when he was come into the ship, he that had been possessed with the devil prayed him that he might be with him.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ οὐκ ἀφῆκεν αὐτόν, ἀλλὰ λέγει αὐτῷ· Ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου πρὸς τοὺς σούς, καὶ ⸀ἀπάγγειλον αὐτοῖς ὅσα ⸂ὁ κύριός σοι⸃ πεποίηκεν καὶ ἠλέησέν σε.
verses.mistral: He did not let him, but said to him, "Go to your house, to your people, and tell them what the Lord has done for you and had mercy on you."
verses.kjv: Howbeit Jesus suffered him not, but saith unto him, Go home to thy friends, and tell them how great things the Lord hath done for thee, and hath had compassion on thee.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν καὶ ἤρξατο κηρύσσειν ἐν τῇ Δεκαπόλει ὅσα ἐποίησεν αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς, καὶ πάντες ἐθαύμαζον.
verses.mistral: And he departed and began to proclaim in the Decapolis what Jesus had done for him, and all marveled.
verses.kjv: And he departed, and began to publish in Decapolis how great things Jesus had done for him: and all men did marvel.

verses.greek: Καὶ διαπεράσαντος τοῦ Ἰησοῦ ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ πάλιν εἰς τὸ πέραν συνήχθη ὄχλος πολὺς ἐπ’ αὐτόν, καὶ ἦν παρὰ τὴν θάλασσαν.
verses.mistral: And after Jesus had crossed again in the boat, a great crowd gathered around Him, and He was by the sea.
verses.kjv: And when Jesus was passed over again by ship unto the other side, much people gathered unto him: and he was nigh unto the sea.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ἔρχεται εἷς τῶν ἀρχισυναγώγων, ὀνόματι Ἰάϊρος, καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὸν πίπτει πρὸς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ
verses.mistral: Falls at Jesus' feet Greek: ,
verses.kjv: And, behold, there cometh one of the rulers of the synagogue, Jairus by name; and when he saw him, he fell at his feet,

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀παρακαλεῖ αὐτὸν πολλὰ λέγων ὅτι Τὸ θυγάτριόν μου ἐσχάτως ἔχει, ἵνα ἐλθὼν ἐπιθῇς ⸂τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῇ⸃ ⸀ἵνα σωθῇ καὶ ⸀ζήσῃ.
verses.mistral: And he calls him often, saying, My daughter, your faith has made you well; go in peace and be healed, and be whole.
verses.kjv: And besought him greatly, saying, My little daughter lieth at the point of death: I pray thee, come and lay thy hands on her, that she may be healed; and she shall live.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπῆλθεν μετ’ αὐτοῦ. Καὶ ἠκολούθει αὐτῷ ὄχλος πολύς, καὶ συνέθλιβον αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And she went with him. And a great crowd followed him and pressed upon him.
verses.kjv: And Jesus went with him; and much people followed him, and thronged him.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀γυνὴ οὖσα ἐν ῥύσει αἵματος ⸂δώδεκα ἔτη⸃
verses.mistral: Woman in bleeding for twelve years
verses.kjv: And a certain woman, which had an issue of blood twelve years,

verses.greek: καὶ πολλὰ παθοῦσα ὑπὸ πολλῶν ἰατρῶν καὶ δαπανήσασα τὰ παρ’ αὐτῆς πάντα καὶ μηδὲν ὠφεληθεῖσα ἀλλὰ μᾶλλον εἰς τὸ χεῖρον ἐλθοῦσα,
verses.mistral: Suffered much under many physicians and spent all that she had, yet became worse, instead.
verses.kjv: And had suffered many things of many physicians, and had spent all that she had, and was nothing bettered, but rather grew worse,

verses.greek: ⸀ἀκούσασα περὶ τοῦ Ἰησοῦ, ἐλθοῦσα ἐν τῷ ὄχλῳ ὄπισθεν ἥψατο τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: Touching the hem of His garment
verses.kjv: When she had heard of Jesus, came in the press behind, and touched his garment.

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ὅτι ⸂Ἐὰν ἅψωμαι κἂν τῶν ἱματίων αὐτοῦ⸃ σωθήσομαι.
verses.mistral: For he said, 'If I but touch his clothes, I shall be made well.'
verses.kjv: For she said, If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐξηράνθη ἡ πηγὴ τοῦ αἵματος αὐτῆς, καὶ ἔγνω τῷ σώματι ὅτι ἴαται ἀπὸ τῆς μάστιγος.
verses.mistral: The fountain of her blood was immediately dried up, and she felt in her body that she was healed from the scourge.
verses.kjv: And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up; and she felt in her body that she was healed of that plague.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐπιγνοὺς ἐν ἑαυτῷ τὴν ἐξ αὐτοῦ δύναμιν ἐξελθοῦσαν ἐπιστραφεὶς ἐν τῷ ὄχλῳ ἔλεγεν· Τίς μου ἥψατο τῶν ἱματίων;
verses.mistral: Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that power had gone forth from him, turned about in the crowd and said, "Who touched my clothes?"
verses.kjv: And Jesus, immediately knowing in himself that virtue had gone out of him, turned him about in the press, and said, Who touched my clothes?

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγον αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ· Βλέπεις τὸν ὄχλον συνθλίβοντά σε, καὶ λέγεις· Τίς μου ἥψατο;
verses.mistral: Who said to him his disciples, "Seeing the crowd press upon you, and you say, 'Who touched me?'"
verses.kjv: And his disciples said unto him, Thou seest the multitude thronging thee, and sayest thou, Who touched me?

verses.greek: καὶ περιεβλέπετο ἰδεῖν τὴν τοῦτο ποιήσασαν.
verses.mistral: And he looked around to see who had done this.
verses.kjv: And he looked round about to see her that had done this thing.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ γυνὴ φοβηθεῖσα καὶ τρέμουσα, εἰδυῖα ὃ γέγονεν ⸀αὐτῇ, ἦλθεν καὶ προσέπεσεν αὐτῷ καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ πᾶσαν τὴν ἀλήθειαν.
verses.mistral: The woman, fearing and trembling, realizing what had happened to her, approached him and told him the whole truth.
verses.kjv: But the woman fearing and trembling, knowing what was done in her, came and fell down before him, and told him all the truth.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτῇ· ⸀Θυγάτηρ, ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε· ὕπαγε εἰς εἰρήνην, καὶ ἴσθι ὑγιὴς ἀπὸ τῆς μάστιγός σου.
verses.mistral: He said to her, "Daughter, your faith has saved you; go in peace, and be healed from your disease."
verses.kjv: And he said unto her, Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace, and be whole of thy plague.

verses.greek: Ἔτι αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος ἔρχονται ἀπὸ τοῦ ἀρχισυναγώγου λέγοντες ὅτι Ἡ θυγάτηρ σου ἀπέθανεν· τί ἔτι σκύλλεις τὸν διδάσκαλον;
verses.mistral: They are coming from the chief priests saying that your daughter has died; why further trouble the Teacher?
verses.kjv: While he yet spake, there came from the ruler of the synagogue's house certain which said, Thy daughter is dead: why troublest thou the Master any further?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ⸀παρακούσας τὸν λόγον λαλούμενον λέγει τῷ ἀρχισυναγώγῳ· Μὴ φοβοῦ, μόνον πίστευε.
verses.mistral: Jesus hearing the word spoken to him by the chief priests said to the ruler of the synagogue, "Do not be afraid, only believe."
verses.kjv: As soon as Jesus heard the word that was spoken, he saith unto the ruler of the synagogue, Be not afraid, only believe.

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἀφῆκεν οὐδένα ⸂μετ’ αὐτοῦ⸃ συνακολουθῆσαι εἰ μὴ ⸀τὸν Πέτρον καὶ Ἰάκωβον καὶ Ἰωάννην τὸν ἀδελφὸν Ἰακώβου.
verses.mistral: And no one dared to follow Him, except Peter, James, and John the brother of James.
verses.kjv: And he suffered no man to follow him, save Peter, and James, and John the brother of James.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἔρχονται εἰς τὸν οἶκον τοῦ ἀρχισυναγώγου, καὶ θεωρεῖ θόρυβον ⸀καὶ κλαίοντας καὶ ἀλαλάζοντας πολλά,
verses.mistral: Entering the high priest's house, he saw a commotion and people wailing and shouting loudly.
verses.kjv: And he cometh to the house of the ruler of the synagogue, and seeth the tumult, and them that wept and wailed greatly.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰσελθὼν λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τί θορυβεῖσθε καὶ κλαίετε; τὸ παιδίον οὐκ ἀπέθανεν ἀλλὰ καθεύδει.
verses.mistral: He said to them, "Why do you make a commotion and weep? The child has not died but is asleep."
verses.kjv: And when he was come in, he saith unto them, Why make ye this ado, and weep? the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth.

verses.greek: καὶ κατεγέλων αὐτοῦ. ⸀αὐτὸς δὲ ἐκβαλὼν πάντας παραλαμβάνει τὸν πατέρα τοῦ παιδίου καὶ τὴν μητέρα καὶ τοὺς μετ’ αὐτοῦ, καὶ εἰσπορεύεται ὅπου ἦν τὸ ⸀παιδίον·
verses.mistral: Laughed at Himself (And He himself expelling all took the child's father and mother and those with him, and entered where the child was)
verses.kjv: And they laughed him to scorn. But when he had put them all out, he taketh the father and the mother of the damsel, and them that were with him, and entereth in where the damsel was lying.

verses.greek: καὶ κρατήσας τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ παιδίου λέγει αὐτῇ· Ταλιθα ⸀κουμ, ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον· Τὸ κοράσιον, σοὶ λέγω, ⸀ἔγειρε.
verses.mistral: Rise, daughter. Your translation is "Arise, daughter." for Mark 5:41 (Koine Greek). This phrase is directed to the child who was dead and addressed as a daughter in the previous verses' context.
verses.kjv: And he took the damsel by the hand, and said unto her, Talitha cumi; which is, being interpreted, Damsel, I say unto thee, arise.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀνέστη τὸ κοράσιον καὶ περιεπάτει, ἦν γὰρ ἐτῶν δώδεκα. καὶ ἐξέστησαν ⸁εὐθὺς ἐκστάσει μεγάλῃ.
verses.mistral: The child is immediately raised and began to walk; for she was twelve years old. And they were immediately filled with great amazement.
verses.kjv: And straightway the damsel arose, and walked; for she was of the age of twelve years. And they were astonished with a great astonishment.

verses.greek: καὶ διεστείλατο αὐτοῖς πολλὰ ἵνα μηδεὶς γνοῖ τοῦτο, καὶ εἶπεν δοθῆναι αὐτῇ φαγεῖν.
verses.mistral: He commanded them to tell no one, but said, "Give her something to eat."
verses.kjv: And he charged them straitly that no man should know it; and commanded that something should be given her to eat.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἐκεῖθεν, καὶ ⸀ἔρχεται εἰς τὴν πατρίδα αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἀκολουθοῦσιν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And he departed thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples followed him.
verses.kjv: And he went out from thence, and came into his own country; and his disciples follow him.

verses.greek: καὶ γενομένου σαββάτου ἤρξατο ⸂διδάσκειν ἐν τῇ συναγωγῇ⸃· καὶ ⸀οἱ πολλοὶ ἀκούοντες ἐξεπλήσσοντο λέγοντες· Πόθεν τούτῳ ταῦτα, καὶ τίς ἡ σοφία ἡ δοθεῖσα ⸀τούτῳ, καὶ ⸀αἱ δυνάμεις τοιαῦται διὰ τῶν χειρῶν αὐτοῦ ⸀γινόμεναι;
verses.mistral: And on the Sabbath he began to teach in the synagogue, and many who heard him were astonished, saying, "Where did this man get these things? What is the wisdom given to him? What are such mighty works as these performed by his hands?"
verses.kjv: And when the sabbath day was come, he began to teach in the synagogue: and many hearing him were astonished, saying, From whence hath this man these things? and what wisdom is this which is given unto him, that even such mighty works are wrought by his hands?

verses.greek: οὐχ οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ τέκτων, ὁ υἱὸς ⸀τῆς Μαρίας ⸂καὶ ἀδελφὸς⸃ Ἰακώβου καὶ ⸀Ἰωσῆτος καὶ Ἰούδα καὶ Σίμωνος; καὶ οὐκ εἰσὶν αἱ ἀδελφαὶ αὐτοῦ ὧδε πρὸς ἡμᾶς; καὶ ἐσκανδαλίζοντο ἐν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: This man is not the carpenter, the son of Mary, and the brother of James, Joses, Judas, and Simon? And are not his sisters here with us? And they were astonished at him.
verses.kjv: Is not this the carpenter, the son of Mary, the brother of James, and Joses, and of Juda, and Simon? and are not his sisters here with us? And they were offended at him.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἔλεγεν⸃ αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὅτι Οὐκ ἔστιν προφήτης ἄτιμος εἰ μὴ ἐν τῇ πατρίδι αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐν τοῖς ⸂συγγενεῦσιν αὐτοῦ⸃ καὶ ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Jesus told them, "Indeed, a prophet is not without honor except in his own town and among his relatives and in his household."
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, A prophet is not without honour, but in his own country, and among his own kin, and in his own house.

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἐδύνατο ἐκεῖ ⸂ποιῆσαι οὐδεμίαν δύναμιν⸃, εἰ μὴ ὀλίγοις ἀρρώστοις ἐπιθεὶς τὰς χεῖρας ἐθεράπευσεν·
verses.mistral: Could not do there any mighty work, except to a few sick ones, he put his hands and healed.
verses.kjv: And he could there do no mighty work, save that he laid his hands upon a few sick folk, and healed them.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἐθαύμαζεν διὰ τὴν ἀπιστίαν αὐτῶν. Καὶ περιῆγεν τὰς κώμας κύκλῳ διδάσκων.
verses.mistral: And he marveled because of their unbelief. And he went around the villages teaching.
verses.kjv: And he marvelled because of their unbelief. And he went round about the villages, teaching.

verses.greek: καὶ προσκαλεῖται τοὺς δώδεκα, καὶ ἤρξατο αὐτοὺς ἀποστέλλειν δύο δύο, καὶ ἐδίδου αὐτοῖς ἐξουσίαν τῶν πνευμάτων τῶν ἀκαθάρτων,
verses.mistral: And he summoned the twelve, and began to send them out two by two, and gave them authority over unclean spirits.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he called unto him the twelve, and began to send them forth by two and two; and gave them power over unclean spirits;

verses.greek: καὶ παρήγγειλεν αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδὲν αἴρωσιν εἰς ὁδὸν εἰ μὴ ῥάβδον μόνον, μὴ ⸂ἄρτον, μὴ πήραν⸃, μὴ εἰς τὴν ζώνην χαλκόν,
verses.mistral: Carry nothing on the road except a staff; no bread, no bag, no money in a belt.
verses.kjv: And commanded them that they should take nothing for their journey, save a staff only; no scrip, no bread, no money in their purse:

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ὑποδεδεμένους σανδάλια, καὶ μὴ ⸀ἐνδύσησθε δύο χιτῶνας.
verses.mistral: Wear sandals, and put on no two tunics.
verses.kjv: But be shod with sandals; and not put on two coats.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Ὅπου ⸀ἐὰν εἰσέλθητε εἰς οἰκίαν, ἐκεῖ μένετε ἕως ἂν ἐξέλθητε ἐκεῖθεν.
verses.mistral: Stay in the house until you leave there.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, In what place soever ye enter into an house, there abide till ye depart from that place.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ὃς ἂν τόπος μὴ δέξηται⸃ ὑμᾶς μηδὲ ἀκούσωσιν ὑμῶν, ἐκπορευόμενοι ἐκεῖθεν ἐκτινάξατε τὸν χοῦν τὸν ὑποκάτω τῶν ποδῶν ὑμῶν εἰς μαρτύριον ⸀αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: And if any place will not receive you, as you depart there, shake off the dust from your feet for a testimony against them.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall not receive you, nor hear you, when ye depart thence, shake off the dust under your feet for a testimony against them. Verily I say unto you, It shall be more tolerable for Sodom and Gomorrha in the day of judgment, than for that city.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξελθόντες ⸀ἐκήρυξαν ἵνα ⸀μετανοῶσιν,
verses.mistral: Repent (Directly translating " " from the given Koine Greek text. The original verse continues with " ," but it is not explicitly stated as part of the request to be translated.)
verses.kjv: And they went out, and preached that men should repent.

verses.greek: καὶ δαιμόνια πολλὰ ἐξέβαλλον, καὶ ἤλειφον ἐλαίῳ πολλοὺς ἀρρώστους καὶ ἐθεράπευον.
verses.mistral: Expelled many demons, anointed many sick with oil, and healed them.
verses.kjv: And they cast out many devils, and anointed with oil many that were sick, and healed them.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἤκουσεν ὁ βασιλεὺς Ἡρῴδης, φανερὸν γὰρ ἐγένετο τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ, καὶ ⸀ἔλεγον ὅτι Ἰωάννης ὁ βαπτίζων ⸂ἐγήγερται ἐκ νεκρῶν⸃, καὶ διὰ τοῦτο ἐνεργοῦσιν αἱ δυνάμεις ἐν αὐτῷ·
verses.mistral: Listened the king Herod, for indeed his name was manifest, and they were saying that John had been raised from the dead, and for this reason the powers are working through him.
verses.kjv: And king Herod heard of him; (for his name was spread abroad:) and he said, That John the Baptist was risen from the dead, and therefore mighty works do shew forth themselves in him.

verses.greek: ἄλλοι ⸀δὲ ἔλεγον ὅτι Ἠλίας ἐστίν· ἄλλοι δὲ ἔλεγον ὅτι ⸀προφήτης ὡς εἷς τῶν προφητῶν.
verses.mistral: Others were saying that he is Elijah, and others were saying that he is a prophet like one of the prophets.
verses.kjv: Others said, That it is Elias. And others said, That it is a prophet, or as one of the prophets.

verses.greek: ἀκούσας δὲ ⸀ὁ Ἡρῴδης ⸀ἔλεγεν· Ὃν ἐγὼ ἀπεκεφάλισα Ἰωάννην, οὗτος ⸀ἠγέρθη.
verses.mistral: This one was John whom I beheaded; he has risen.
verses.kjv: But when Herod heard thereof, he said, It is John, whom I beheaded: he is risen from the dead.

verses.greek: Αὐτὸς γὰρ ὁ Ἡρῴδης ἀποστείλας ἐκράτησεν τὸν Ἰωάννην καὶ ἔδησεν αὐτὸν ἐν φυλακῇ διὰ Ἡρῳδιάδα τὴν γυναῖκα Φιλίππου τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ αὐτοῦ, ὅτι αὐτὴν ἐγάμησεν·
verses.mistral: Herod arrested John and put him in prison on account of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip, because he had married her.
verses.kjv: For Herod himself had sent forth and laid hold upon John, and bound him in prison for Herodias' sake, his brother Philip's wife: for he had married her.

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ὁ Ἰωάννης τῷ Ἡρῴδῃ ὅτι Οὐκ ἔξεστίν σοι ἔχειν τὴν γυναῖκα τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ σου.
verses.mistral: John said to Herod, 'It is not lawful for you to have the wife of your brother.'
verses.kjv: For John had said unto Herod, It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother's wife.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ Ἡρῳδιὰς ἐνεῖχεν αὐτῷ καὶ ἤθελεν αὐτὸν ἀποκτεῖναι, καὶ οὐκ ἠδύνατο·
verses.mistral: Herodias held him and desired to kill him, but could not.
verses.kjv: Therefore Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him; but she could not:

verses.greek: ὁ γὰρ Ἡρῴδης ἐφοβεῖτο τὸν Ἰωάννην, εἰδὼς αὐτὸν ἄνδρα δίκαιον καὶ ἅγιον, καὶ συνετήρει αὐτόν, καὶ ἀκούσας αὐτοῦ πολλὰ ⸀ἠπόρει, καὶ ἡδέως αὐτοῦ ἤκουεν.
verses.mistral: Fearful of John, Herod respected him; and hearing him, he was perplexed, and he listened to him with delight.
verses.kjv: For Herod feared John, knowing that he was a just man and an holy, and observed him; and when he heard him, he did many things, and heard him gladly.

verses.greek: Καὶ γενομένης ἡμέρας εὐκαίρου ὅτε Ἡρῴδης τοῖς γενεσίοις αὐτοῦ δεῖπνον ⸀ἐποίησεν τοῖς μεγιστᾶσιν αὐτοῦ καὶ τοῖς χιλιάρχοις καὶ τοῖς πρώτοις τῆς Γαλιλαίας,
verses.mistral: And on a suitable day, Herod held a banquet for his high officials, military commanders, and the foremost men of Galilee.
verses.kjv: And when a convenient day was come, that Herod on his birthday made a supper to his lords, high captains, and chief estates of Galilee;

verses.greek: καὶ εἰσελθούσης τῆς θυγατρὸς ⸂αὐτῆς τῆς⸃ Ἡρῳδιάδος καὶ ὀρχησαμένης ⸂καὶ ἀρεσάσης⸃ τῷ Ἡρῴδῃ καὶ τοῖς συνανακειμένοις, ⸂εἶπεν ὁ βασιλεὺς⸃ τῷ κορασίῳ· Αἴτησόν με ὃ ἐὰν θέλῃς, καὶ δώσω σοι·
verses.mistral: The daughter of Herodias danced and pleased Herod and those who were seated at the table. Then the king said to the girl...
verses.kjv: And when the daughter of the said Herodias came in, and danced, and pleased Herod and them that sat with him, the king said unto the damsel, Ask of me whatsoever thou wilt, and I will give it thee.

verses.greek: καὶ ὤμοσεν ⸀αὐτῇ· ⸂Ὅ τι⸃ ἐάν με αἰτήσῃς δώσω σοι ἕως ἡμίσους τῆς βασιλείας μου.
verses.mistral: And he swore to her, "Whatever you shall ask of me, I will give you up to half of my kingdom.
verses.kjv: And he sware unto her, Whatsoever thou shalt ask of me, I will give it thee, unto the half of my kingdom.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ἐξελθοῦσα εἶπεν τῇ μητρὶ αὐτῆς· Τί ⸀αἰτήσωμαι; ἡ δὲ εἶπεν· Τὴν κεφαλὴν Ἰωάννου τοῦ ⸀βαπτίζοντος.
verses.mistral: She went out and said to her mother, "What shall I ask?" But she said, "The head of John the Baptist."
verses.kjv: And she went forth, and said unto her mother, What shall I ask? And she said, The head of John the Baptist.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰσελθοῦσα ⸀εὐθὺς μετὰ σπουδῆς πρὸς τὸν βασιλέα ᾐτήσατο λέγουσα· Θέλω ἵνα ⸂ἐξαυτῆς δῷς μοι⸃ ἐπὶ πίνακι τὴν κεφαλὴν Ἰωάννου τοῦ βαπτιστοῦ.
verses.mistral: Gave immediately with diligence to the king, asked saying: I want that from yourself forthwith you give me upon a platter the head of John the Baptist.
verses.kjv: And she came in straightway with haste unto the king, and asked, saying, I will that thou give me by and by in a charger the head of John the Baptist.

verses.greek: καὶ περίλυπος γενόμενος ὁ βασιλεὺς διὰ τοὺς ὅρκους καὶ τοὺς ⸀ἀνακειμένους οὐκ ἠθέλησεν ⸂ἀθετῆσαι αὐτήν⸃·
verses.mistral: Saddened, the king because of his oaths and the guests, did not wish to disregard (her)
verses.kjv: And the king was exceeding sorry; yet for his oath's sake, and for their sakes which sat with him, he would not reject her.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀποστείλας ὁ βασιλεὺς σπεκουλάτορα ἐπέταξεν ⸀ἐνέγκαι τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ. ⸀καὶ ἀπελθὼν ἀπεκεφάλισεν αὐτὸν ἐν τῇ φυλακῇ
verses.mistral: Immediately the king sent out a soldier to bring back his head. And going out, he beheaded him in prison.
verses.kjv: And immediately the king sent an executioner, and commanded his head to be brought: and he went and beheaded him in the prison,

verses.greek: καὶ ἤνεγκεν τὴν κεφαλὴν αὐτοῦ ἐπὶ πίνακι καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτὴν τῷ κορασίῳ, καὶ τὸ κοράσιον ἔδωκεν αὐτὴν τῇ μητρὶ αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: And brought his head upon a platter and gave it to the servant, and the servant gave it to his mother.
verses.kjv: And brought his head in a charger, and gave it to the damsel: and the damsel gave it to her mother.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἦλθον καὶ ἦραν τὸ πτῶμα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔθηκαν αὐτὸ ἐν μνημείῳ.
verses.mistral: And hearing the disciples, they came and took up his corpse and placed it in a tomb.
verses.kjv: And when his disciples heard of it, they came and took up his corpse, and laid it in a tomb.

verses.greek: Καὶ συνάγονται οἱ ἀπόστολοι πρὸς τὸν Ἰησοῦν, καὶ ἀπήγγειλαν αὐτῷ ⸀πάντα ὅσα ἐποίησαν καὶ ὅσα ἐδίδαξαν.
verses.mistral: And the disciples gathered to Jesus, and reported all things that they had done and taught.
verses.kjv: And the apostles gathered themselves together unto Jesus, and told him all things, both what they had done, and what they had taught.
