verses.greek: καὶ ⸀λέγει αὐτοῖς· Δεῦτε ὑμεῖς αὐτοὶ κατ’ ἰδίαν εἰς ἔρημον τόπον καὶ ⸀ἀναπαύσασθε ὀλίγον. ἦσαν γὰρ οἱ ἐρχόμενοι καὶ οἱ ὑπάγοντες πολλοί, καὶ οὐδὲ φαγεῖν εὐκαίρουν.
verses.mistral: Go yourselves into a secluded place and rest a while. For those coming and those going are many, and they have no time even to eat.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Come ye yourselves apart into a desert place, and rest a while: for there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπῆλθον ⸂ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ εἰς ἔρημον τόπον⸃ κατ’ ἰδίαν.
verses.mistral: Went into a deserted place by boat
verses.kjv: And they departed into a desert place by ship privately.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶδον αὐτοὺς ὑπάγοντας καὶ ⸀ἐπέγνωσαν πολλοί, καὶ πεζῇ ἀπὸ πασῶν τῶν πόλεων συνέδραμον ἐκεῖ καὶ προῆλθον ⸀αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: I saw them going away, and many knew them; and running together on foot from all the cities, they came there and approached them.
verses.kjv: And the people saw them departing, and many knew him, and ran afoot thither out of all cities, and outwent them, and came together unto him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξελθὼν ⸀εἶδεν πολὺν ὄχλον, καὶ ἐσπλαγχνίσθη ἐπ’ ⸀αὐτοὺς ὅτι ἦσαν ὡς πρόβατα μὴ ἔχοντα ποιμένα, καὶ ἤρξατο διδάσκειν αὐτοὺς πολλά.
verses.mistral: Many have become like sheep without a shepherd. He began to teach them many things.
verses.kjv: And Jesus, when he came out, saw much people, and was moved with compassion toward them, because they were as sheep not having a shepherd: and he began to teach them many things.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἤδη ὥρας πολλῆς γενομένης προσελθόντες αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ⸀ἔλεγον ὅτι Ἔρημός ἐστιν ὁ τόπος, καὶ ἤδη ὥρα πολλή·
verses.mistral: And already it is becoming late, coming to Him His disciples said that the place is desolate, and it is now very late
verses.kjv: And when the day was now far spent, his disciples came unto him, and said, This is a desert place, and now the time is far passed:

verses.greek: ἀπόλυσον αὐτούς, ἵνα ἀπελθόντες εἰς τοὺς κύκλῳ ἀγροὺς καὶ κώμας ἀγοράσωσιν ἑαυτοῖς ⸂τί φάγωσιν⸃.
verses.mistral: Release them, and go into the surrounding countryside and villages, and buy themselves something to eat.
verses.kjv: Send them away, that they may go into the country round about, and into the villages, and buy themselves bread: for they have nothing to eat.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Δότε αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς φαγεῖν. καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Ἀπελθόντες ἀγοράσωμεν δηναρίων διακοσίων ἄρτους καὶ ⸀δώσομεν αὐτοῖς φαγεῖν;
verses.mistral: Give them something to eat yourselves. And they ask Him, "As we go, should we buy two hundred denarii worth of bread and give them something to eat?"
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Give ye them to eat. And they say unto him, Shall we go and buy two hundred pennyworth of bread, and give them to eat?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Πόσους ⸂ἔχετε ἄρτους⸃; ⸀ὑπάγετε ἴδετε. καὶ γνόντες λέγουσιν· Πέντε, καὶ δύο ἰχθύας.
verses.mistral: Five loaves and two fish.
verses.kjv: He saith unto them, How many loaves have ye? go and see. And when they knew, they say, Five, and two fishes.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπέταξεν αὐτοῖς ⸀ἀνακλῖναι πάντας συμπόσια συμπόσια ἐπὶ τῷ χλωρῷ χόρτῳ.
verses.mistral: All sit down, in groups, on the green grass.
verses.kjv: And he commanded them to make all sit down by companies upon the green grass.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀνέπεσαν πρασιαὶ πρασιαὶ ⸀κατὰ ἑκατὸν καὶ ⸁κατὰ πεντήκοντα.
verses.mistral: And prostrate themselves, prostrate ones, by hundreds and fifties.
verses.kjv: And they sat down in ranks, by hundreds, and by fifties.

verses.greek: καὶ λαβὼν τοὺς πέντε ἄρτους καὶ τοὺς δύο ἰχθύας ἀναβλέψας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν εὐλόγησεν καὶ κατέκλασεν τοὺς ἄρτους καὶ ἐδίδου τοῖς μαθηταῖς ⸀αὐτοῦ ἵνα ⸀παρατιθῶσιν αὐτοῖς, καὶ τοὺς δύο ἰχθύας ἐμέρισεν πᾶσιν.
verses.mistral: And taking the five loaves and the two fish, looking up to heaven, He blessed and broke the loaves, and gave them to His disciples to set before them; and He divided the two fish among them all.
verses.kjv: And when he had taken the five loaves and the two fishes, he looked up to heaven, and blessed, and brake the loaves, and gave them to his disciples to set before them; and the two fishes divided he among them all.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔφαγον πάντες καὶ ἐχορτάσθησαν·
verses.mistral: All ate and were satisfied
verses.kjv: And they did all eat, and were filled.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦραν ⸂κλάσματα δώδεκα κοφίνων πληρώματα⸃ καὶ ἀπὸ τῶν ἰχθύων.
verses.mistral: And they collected twelve baskets full of fragments from the fish.
verses.kjv: And they took up twelve baskets full of the fragments, and of the fishes.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦσαν οἱ φαγόντες ⸂τοὺς ἄρτους⸃ πεντακισχίλιοι ἄνδρες.
verses.mistral: And those eating the loaves were five thousand men.
verses.kjv: And they that did eat of the loaves were about five thousand men.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἠνάγκασεν τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἐμβῆναι εἰς τὸ πλοῖον καὶ προάγειν ⸂εἰς τὸ πέραν⸃ πρὸς Βηθσαϊδάν, ἕως αὐτὸς ⸀ἀπολύει τὸν ὄχλον.
verses.mistral: Immediately He compelled His disciples to get into the boat and go to Bethsaida on the other side, until He himself dismisses the crowd.
verses.kjv: And straightway he constrained his disciples to get into the ship, and to go to the other side before unto Bethsaida, while he sent away the people.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποταξάμενος αὐτοῖς ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸ ὄρος προσεύξασθαι.
verses.mistral: He went away to the mountain to pray.
verses.kjv: And when he had sent them away, he departed into a mountain to pray.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὀψίας γενομένης ἦν τὸ πλοῖον ἐν μέσῳ τῆς θαλάσσης, καὶ αὐτὸς μόνος ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.
verses.mistral: Evening came, and the boat was in the middle of the sea, and He alone on land.
verses.kjv: And when even was come, the ship was in the midst of the sea, and he alone on the land.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἰδὼν αὐτοὺς βασανιζομένους ἐν τῷ ἐλαύνειν, ἦν γὰρ ὁ ἄνεμος ἐναντίος ⸀αὐτοῖς, περὶ τετάρτην φυλακὴν τῆς νυκτὸς ἔρχεται πρὸς αὐτοὺς περιπατῶν ἐπὶ τῆς θαλάσσης· καὶ ἤθελεν παρελθεῖν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Approaching them struggling in the rowing, for the wind was against them; during the fourth watch of the night he comes to them walking on the sea. And he intended to pass by them.
verses.kjv: And he saw them toiling in rowing; for the wind was contrary unto them: and about the fourth watch of the night he cometh unto them, walking upon the sea, and would have passed by them.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν ⸂ἐπὶ τῆς θαλάσσης περιπατοῦντα⸃ ἔδοξαν ⸂ὅτι φάντασμά ἐστιν⸃ καὶ ἀνέκραξαν,
verses.mistral: They thought it was a ghost and cried out
verses.kjv: But when they saw him walking upon the sea, they supposed it had been a spirit, and cried out:

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ αὐτὸν εἶδον καὶ ἐταράχθησαν. ⸂ὁ δὲ εὐθὺς⸃ ἐλάλησεν μετ’ αὐτῶν, καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Θαρσεῖτε, ἐγώ εἰμι, μὴ φοβεῖσθε.
verses.mistral: All saw Him and were disturbed. Immediately He spoke with them, and said to them: "Be comforted, I am here; do not be afraid."
verses.kjv: For they all saw him, and were troubled. And immediately he talked with them, and saith unto them, Be of good cheer: it is I; be not afraid.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀνέβη πρὸς αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸ πλοῖον, καὶ ἐκόπασεν ὁ ἄνεμος. καὶ λίαν ⸂ἐκ περισσοῦ⸃ ἐν ἑαυτοῖς ⸀ἐξίσταντο,
verses.mistral: And He ascended to them into the boat, and the wind ceased. And they marveled greatly among themselves.
verses.kjv: And he went up unto them into the ship; and the wind ceased: and they were sore amazed in themselves beyond measure, and wondered.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ συνῆκαν ἐπὶ τοῖς ἄρτοις, ⸂ἀλλ’ ἦν⸃ αὐτῶν ἡ καρδία πεπωρωμένη.
verses.mistral: Their hearts were troubled.
verses.kjv: For they considered not the miracle of the loaves: for their heart was hardened.

verses.greek: Καὶ διαπεράσαντες ⸂ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν ἦλθον εἰς⸃ Γεννησαρὲτ καὶ προσωρμίσθησαν.
verses.mistral: Approaching the earth, they came to Gennesaret and anchored
verses.kjv: And when they had passed over, they came into the land of Gennesaret, and drew to the shore.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξελθόντων αὐτῶν ἐκ τοῦ πλοίου ⸀εὐθὺς ἐπιγνόντες αὐτὸν
verses.mistral: Recognized Him (Mark 6:54 - "And when they had come out of the boat, immediately they recognized Him.")
verses.kjv: And when they were come out of the ship, straightway they knew him,

verses.greek: ⸂περιέδραμον ὅλην τὴν χώραν ἐκείνην καὶ⸃ ἤρξαντο ἐπὶ τοῖς κραβάττοις τοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας περιφέρειν ὅπου ἤκουον ⸀ὅτι ἐστίν.
verses.mistral: Heal the sick who are there and proclaim, "He is here."
verses.kjv: And ran through that whole region round about, and began to carry about in beds those that were sick, where they heard he was.

verses.greek: καὶ ὅπου ἂν εἰσεπορεύετο εἰς κώμας ἢ ⸂εἰς πόλεις ἢ εἰς⸃ ἀγροὺς ἐν ταῖς ἀγοραῖς ⸀ἐτίθεσαν τοὺς ἀσθενοῦντας, καὶ παρεκάλουν αὐτὸν ἵνα κἂν τοῦ κρασπέδου τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ ἅψωνται· καὶ ὅσοι ἂν ⸀ἥψαντο αὐτοῦ ἐσῴζοντο.
verses.mistral: And wherever He entered villages or cities or the countryside, they laid the sick in the marketplaces and begged Him to let them just touch the tassel of His robe, and those who touched Him were healed.
verses.kjv: And whithersoever he entered, into villages, or cities, or country, they laid the sick in the streets, and besought him that they might touch if it were but the border of his garment: and as many as touched him were made whole.

verses.greek: Καὶ συνάγονται πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ Φαρισαῖοι καί τινες τῶν γραμματέων ἐλθόντες ἀπὸ Ἱεροσολύμων
verses.mistral: And the Pharisees and some of the teachers of the law, who had come from Jerusalem, gather to Him
verses.kjv: Then came together unto him the Pharisees, and certain of the scribes, which came from Jerusalem.

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδόντες τινὰς τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ⸂ὅτι κοιναῖς χερσίν, τοῦτ’ ἔστιν ἀνίπτοις, ἐσθίουσιν⸃ ⸀τοὺς ⸀ἄρτους—
verses.mistral: And they saw some of His disciples eating bread with unwashed hands.
verses.kjv: And when they saw some of his disciples eat bread with defiled, that is to say, with unwashen, hands, they found fault.

verses.greek: οἱ γὰρ Φαρισαῖοι καὶ πάντες οἱ Ἰουδαῖοι ἐὰν μὴ πυγμῇ νίψωνται τὰς χεῖρας οὐκ ἐσθίουσιν, κρατοῦντες τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν πρεσβυτέρων,
verses.mistral: They do not eat without washing their hands, holding to the tradition of the elders.
verses.kjv: For the Pharisees, and all the Jews, except they wash their hands oft, eat not, holding the tradition of the elders.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπ’ ⸀ἀγορᾶς ἐὰν μὴ ⸀βαπτίσωνται οὐκ ἐσθίουσιν, καὶ ἄλλα πολλά ἐστιν ἃ παρέλαβον κρατεῖν, βαπτισμοὺς ποτηρίων καὶ ξεστῶν καὶ χαλκίων ⸂καὶ κλινῶν⸃—
verses.mistral: And from the market, if they do not baptize themselves, they will not eat; and many other things you have received to hold: baptisms of cups and of dining couches and bronze statues and beds.
verses.kjv: And when they come from the market, except they wash, they eat not. And many other things there be, which they have received to hold, as the washing of cups, and pots, brasen vessels, and of tables.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ἐπερωτῶσιν αὐτὸν οἱ Φαρισαῖοι καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς· Διὰ τί ⸂οὐ περιπατοῦσιν οἱ μαθηταί σου⸃ κατὰ τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν πρεσβυτέρων, ἀλλὰ ⸀κοιναῖς χερσὶν ἐσθίουσιν τὸν ἄρτον;
verses.mistral: The Pharisees and the teachers questioned him: "Why do your disciples not walk according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unclean hands?"
verses.kjv: Then the Pharisees and scribes asked him, Why walk not thy disciples according to the tradition of the elders, but eat bread with unwashen hands?

verses.greek: ὁ ⸀δὲ εἶπεν ⸀αὐτοῖς· Καλῶς ἐπροφήτευσεν Ἠσαΐας περὶ ὑμῶν τῶν ὑποκριτῶν, ὡς γέγραπται ⸀ὅτι Οὗτος ὁ λαὸς τοῖς χείλεσίν με τιμᾷ, ἡ δὲ καρδία αὐτῶν πόρρω ἀπέχει ἀπ’ ἐμοῦ·
verses.mistral: He replied to them, "Isaiah indeed prophesied correctly about you hypocrites, as it is written: 'This people honors me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.'"
verses.kjv: He answered and said unto them, Well hath Esaias prophesied of you hypocrites, as it is written, This people honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.

verses.greek: μάτην δὲ σέβονταί με, διδάσκοντες διδασκαλίας ἐντάλματα ἀνθρώπων·
verses.mistral: Teach for doctrines the commandments of men.
verses.kjv: Howbeit in vain do they worship me, teaching for doctrines the commandments of men.

verses.greek: ⸀ἀφέντες τὴν ἐντολὴν τοῦ θεοῦ κρατεῖτε τὴν παράδοσιν τῶν ⸀ἀνθρώπων.
verses.mistral: Obey human tradition, disregarding the commandment of God.
verses.kjv: For laying aside the commandment of God, ye hold the tradition of men, as the washing of pots and cups: and many other such like things ye do.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Καλῶς ἀθετεῖτε τὴν ἐντολὴν τοῦ θεοῦ, ἵνα τὴν παράδοσιν ὑμῶν ⸀τηρήσητε·
verses.mistral: You negate God's commandment, to hold your tradition.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Full well ye reject the commandment of God, that ye may keep your own tradition.

verses.greek: Μωϋσῆς γὰρ εἶπεν· Τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα σου, καί· Ὁ κακολογῶν πατέρα ἢ μητέρα θανάτῳ τελευτάτω·
verses.mistral: Honors father or mother is to be regarded as death declared.
verses.kjv: For Moses said, Honour thy father and thy mother; and, Whoso curseth father or mother, let him die the death:

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς δὲ λέγετε· Ἐὰν εἴπῃ ἄνθρωπος τῷ πατρὶ ἢ τῇ μητρί· Κορβᾶν, ὅ ἐστιν Δῶρον, ὃ ἐὰν ἐξ ἐμοῦ ὠφεληθῇς,
verses.mistral: You say: If a man tells his father or his mother, 'Whatever is given to me and I have used for you as a gift,' This translation corresponds to Mark 7:11 in the Koine Greek text. The previous verse provides context by stating that the Pharisees criticized Jesus' disciples for not following Jewish tradition regarding handwashing, but Jesus responded by stating that honoring one's father or mother is more important than following man-made traditions and is equivalent to declaring oneself dead. The Greek text provided in this question continues the Pharisees' critique of Jesus' disciples and their apparent disregard for Jewish tradition, as they are accused of disobeying the commandment to honor one's parents by using gifts given to them as if they were corban (dedicated to God) rather than using them to provide for their parents.
verses.kjv: But ye say, If a man shall say to his father or mother, It is Corban, that is to say, a gift, by whatsoever thou mightest be profited by me; he shall be free.

verses.greek: ⸀οὐκέτι ἀφίετε αὐτὸν οὐδὲν ποιῆσαι τῷ ⸀πατρὶ ἢ τῇ ⸀μητρί,
verses.mistral: Do not allow him to do anything for father or mother.
verses.kjv: And ye suffer him no more to do ought for his father or his mother;

verses.greek: ἀκυροῦντες τὸν λόγον τοῦ θεοῦ τῇ παραδόσει ὑμῶν ᾗ παρεδώκατε· καὶ παρόμοια τοιαῦτα πολλὰ ποιεῖτε.
verses.mistral: Suppress the word of God, and so you do: many such things like these you do.
verses.kjv: Making the word of God of none effect through your tradition, which ye have delivered: and many such like things do ye.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος ⸀πάλιν τὸν ὄχλον ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· ⸀Ἀκούσατέ μου πάντες καὶ ⸀σύνετε.
verses.mistral: Hear me all, and understand.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called all the people unto him, he said unto them, Hearken unto me every one of you, and understand:

verses.greek: οὐδέν ἐστιν ἔξωθεν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου εἰσπορευόμενον εἰς αὐτὸν ὃ δύναται ⸂κοινῶσαι αὐτόν⸃· ἀλλὰ τὰ ⸂ἐκ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκπορευόμενά⸃ ἐστιν τὰ κοινοῦντα τὸν ⸀ἄνθρωπον.
verses.mistral: Nothing from outside a man is able to enter into him and make him unclean, but the things which come out of a man are what defile him.
verses.kjv: There is nothing from without a man, that entering into him can defile him: but the things which come out of him, those are they that defile the man.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὅτε εἰσῆλθεν ⸀εἰς οἶκον ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου, ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ⸂τὴν παραβολήν⸃.
verses.mistral: And when he entered a house from the crowd, his disciples asked him about the parable.
verses.kjv: And when he was entered into the house from the people, his disciples asked him concerning the parable.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ἀσύνετοί ἐστε; οὐ νοεῖτε ὅτι πᾶν τὸ ἔξωθεν εἰσπορευόμενον εἰς τὸν ἄνθρωπον οὐ δύναται αὐτὸν κοινῶσαι,
verses.mistral: And he says to them: "So also you are without understanding? Do you not understand that all which comes from outside cannot make itself at home?"
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Are ye so without understanding also? Do ye not perceive, that whatsoever thing from without entereth into the man, it cannot defile him;

verses.greek: ὅτι οὐκ εἰσπορεύεται αὐτοῦ εἰς τὴν καρδίαν ἀλλ’ εἰς τὴν κοιλίαν, καὶ εἰς τὸν ἀφεδρῶνα ἐκπορεύεται; —⸀καθαρίζων πάντα τὰ βρώματα.
verses.mistral: Does not enter his heart, but into the belly, and out of the body it goes; thus he cleanses all foods.
verses.kjv: Because it entereth not into his heart, but into the belly, and goeth out into the draught, purging all meats?

verses.greek: ἔλεγεν δὲ ὅτι Τὸ ἐκ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκπορευόμενον ἐκεῖνο κοινοῖ τὸν ἄνθρωπον·
verses.mistral: The man goes out of the one. (Implies: Whatever comes from a man makes him unclean.)
verses.kjv: And he said, That which cometh out of the man, that defileth the man.

verses.greek: ἔσωθεν γὰρ ἐκ τῆς καρδίας τῶν ἀνθρώπων οἱ διαλογισμοὶ οἱ κακοὶ ἐκπορεύονται, ⸂πορνεῖαι, κλοπαί, φόνοι,
verses.mistral: Evil thoughts come from within the hearts of men: fornication, theft, murder.
verses.kjv: For from within, out of the heart of men, proceed evil thoughts, adulteries, fornications, murders,

verses.greek: μοιχεῖαι⸃, πλεονεξίαι, πονηρίαι, δόλος, ἀσέλγεια, ὀφθαλμὸς πονηρός, βλασφημία, ὑπερηφανία, ἀφροσύνη·
verses.mistral: Adulteries, greed, wickedness, deceit, senselessness, evil eye, blasphemy, arrogance, folly
verses.kjv: Thefts, covetousness, wickedness, deceit, lasciviousness, an evil eye, blasphemy, pride, foolishness:

verses.greek: πάντα ταῦτα τὰ πονηρὰ ἔσωθεν ἐκπορεύεται καὶ κοινοῖ τὸν ἄνθρωπον.
verses.mistral: Emerges from within, all these evil things corrupt the human being.
verses.kjv: All these evil things come from within, and defile the man.

verses.greek: ⸂Ἐκεῖθεν δὲ⸃ ἀναστὰς ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὰ ⸀ὅρια ⸀Τύρου. καὶ εἰσελθὼν εἰς οἰκίαν οὐδένα ἤθελεν γνῶναι, καὶ οὐκ ἠδυνήθη λαθεῖν·
verses.mistral: From there he rose and went out to the outskirts of Tyre. And entering into a house, he desired not to know anyone, nor was he able to remain hidden.
verses.kjv: ¶ And from thence he arose, and went into the borders of Tyre and Sidon, and entered into an house, and would have no man know it: but he could not be hid.

verses.greek: ⸂ἀλλ’ εὐθὺς ἀκούσασα⸃ γυνὴ περὶ αὐτοῦ, ἧς εἶχεν τὸ θυγάτριον αὐτῆς πνεῦμα ἀκάθαρτον, ἐλθοῦσα προσέπεσεν πρὸς τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: Immediately a woman heard about him, whose daughter had an unclean spirit. Coming, she fell at his feet.
verses.kjv: For a certain woman, whose young daughter had an unclean spirit, heard of him, and came and fell at his feet:

verses.greek: ⸂ἡ δὲ γυνὴ ἦν⸃ Ἑλληνίς, Συροφοινίκισσα τῷ γένει· καὶ ἠρώτα αὐτὸν ἵνα τὸ δαιμόνιον ἐκβάλῃ ἐκ τῆς θυγατρὸς αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophoenician by birth; and she begged him that he would cast the demon out of her daughter.
verses.kjv: The woman was a Greek, a Syrophenician by nation; and she besought him that he would cast forth the devil out of her daughter.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἔλεγεν⸃ αὐτῇ· Ἄφες πρῶτον χορτασθῆναι τὰ τέκνα, οὐ γὰρ ⸂καλόν ἐστιν⸃ λαβεῖν τὸν ἄρτον τῶν τέκνων καὶ ⸂τοῖς κυναρίοις βαλεῖν⸃.
verses.mistral: Let the children first be fed, for it is not right to take the children's bread and throw it to the dogs.
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto her, Let the children first be filled: for it is not meet to take the children's bread, and to cast it unto the dogs.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ ἀπεκρίθη καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· ⸀Κύριε, ⸀καὶ τὰ κυνάρια ὑποκάτω τῆς τραπέζης ⸀ἐσθίουσιν ἀπὸ τῶν ψιχίων τῶν παιδίων.
verses.mistral: The dog under the table eats the crumbs from the children's bread.
verses.kjv: And she answered and said unto him, Yes, Lord: yet the dogs under the table eat of the children's crumbs.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ· Διὰ τοῦτον τὸν λόγον ὕπαγε, ἐξελήλυθεν ⸂ἐκ τῆς θυγατρός σου τὸ δαιμόνιον⸃.
verses.mistral: And He said to her, 'Because of this reply, go; the demon has gone out of your daughter.'
verses.kjv: And he said unto her, For this saying go thy way; the devil is gone out of thy daughter.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπελθοῦσα εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτῆς εὗρεν τὸ ⸂παιδίον βεβλημένον ἐπὶ τὴν κλίνην καὶ τὸ δαιμόνιον ἐξεληλυθός⸃.
verses.mistral: Returning to her home, she found the child lying on the bed and the demon expelled.
verses.kjv: And when she was come to her house, she found the devil gone out, and her daughter laid upon the bed.

verses.greek: Καὶ πάλιν ἐξελθὼν ἐκ τῶν ὁρίων Τύρου ⸂ἦλθεν διὰ Σιδῶνος εἰς⸃ τὴν θάλασσαν τῆς Γαλιλαίας ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν ὁρίων Δεκαπόλεως.
verses.mistral: Again, going out from the boundaries of Tyre, she went through Sidon to the sea of Galilee in the midst of Decapolis.
verses.kjv: ¶ And again, departing from the coasts of Tyre and Sidon, he came unto the sea of Galilee, through the midst of the coasts of Decapolis.

verses.greek: καὶ φέρουσιν αὐτῷ κωφὸν ⸀καὶ ⸀μογιλάλον, καὶ παρακαλοῦσιν αὐτὸν ἵνα ἐπιθῇ αὐτῷ τὴν χεῖρα.
verses.mistral: Bring a deaf and mute man to him, and they implore him to place his hand on him.
verses.kjv: And they bring unto him one that was deaf, and had an impediment in his speech; and they beseech him to put his hand upon him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπολαβόμενος αὐτὸν ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄχλου κατ’ ἰδίαν ἔβαλεν τοὺς δακτύλους αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰ ὦτα αὐτοῦ καὶ πτύσας ἥψατο τῆς γλώσσης αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Placed his fingers into the man's ears, and after spitting touched the man's tongue.
verses.kjv: And he took him aside from the multitude, and put his fingers into his ears, and he spit, and touched his tongue;

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναβλέψας εἰς τὸν οὐρανὸν ἐστέναξεν, καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Εφφαθα, ὅ ἐστιν Διανοίχθητι·
verses.mistral: Opened, be thou opened.
verses.kjv: And looking up to heaven, he sighed, and saith unto him, Ephphatha, that is, Be opened.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ⸀ἠνοίγησαν αὐτοῦ αἱ ἀκοαί, καὶ ἐλύθη ὁ δεσμὸς τῆς γλώσσης αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἐλάλει ὀρθῶς·
verses.mistral: And his ears were opened, and the bond of his tongue was loosed, and he spoke correctly.
verses.kjv: And straightway his ears were opened, and the string of his tongue was loosed, and he spake plain.

verses.greek: καὶ διεστείλατο αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ ⸀λέγωσιν· ὅσον δὲ ⸂αὐτοῖς διεστέλλετο, αὐτοὶ⸃ μᾶλλον περισσότερον ἐκήρυσσον.
verses.mistral: And he commanded them to tell no one. But the more they were commanded not to speak about it, the more they proclaimed it.
verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man: but the more he charged them, so much the more a great deal they published it;

verses.greek: καὶ ὑπερπερισσῶς ἐξεπλήσσοντο λέγοντες· Καλῶς πάντα πεποίηκεν, καὶ τοὺς κωφοὺς ποιεῖ ἀκούειν ⸀καὶ ἀλάλους λαλεῖν.
verses.mistral: Amazes them exceedingly, saying: Well has he done all things; even the deaf he makes to hear, and the mute to speak.
verses.kjv: And were beyond measure astonished, saying, He hath done all things well: he maketh both the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak.

verses.greek: Ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις ⸂πάλιν πολλοῦ⸃ ὄχλου ὄντος καὶ μὴ ἐχόντων τί φάγωσιν, ⸀προσκαλεσάμενος τοὺς ⸀μαθητὰς λέγει αὐτοῖς·
verses.mistral: In those days, again a great crowd having nothing to eat came to Him. Calling his disciples, he says to them:
verses.kjv: In those days the multitude being very great, and having nothing to eat, Jesus called his disciples unto him, and saith unto them,

verses.greek: Σπλαγχνίζομαι ἐπὶ τὸν ὄχλον ὅτι ἤδη ἡμέραι τρεῖς προσμένουσίν μοι καὶ οὐκ ἔχουσιν τί φάγωσιν·
verses.mistral: I have compassion on the crowd, because they have been with me now for three days and have nothing to eat.
verses.kjv: I have compassion on the multitude, because they have now been with me three days, and have nothing to eat:

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν ἀπολύσω αὐτοὺς νήστεις εἰς οἶκον αὐτῶν, ἐκλυθήσονται ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ· ⸂καί τινες⸃ αὐτῶν ⸀ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ⸀ἥκασιν.
verses.mistral: And if I send them away fasting to their home, they will faint on the way; and some of them have come from far.
verses.kjv: And if I send them away fasting to their own houses, they will faint by the way: for divers of them came from far.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπεκρίθησαν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ⸀ὅτι Πόθεν τούτους δυνήσεταί τις ὧδε χορτάσαι ἄρτων ἐπ’ ἐρημίας;
verses.mistral: And His disciples answered Him, "From where can anyone here in the wilderness find bread to eat?"
verses.kjv: And his disciples answered him, From whence can a man satisfy these men with bread here in the wilderness?

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἠρώτα αὐτούς· Πόσους ἔχετε ἄρτους; οἱ δὲ εἶπαν· Ἑπτά.
verses.mistral: Five (The Greek question in this verse is "How many loaves have you?" and the disciples responded "Seven.")
verses.kjv: And he asked them, How many loaves have ye? And they said, Seven.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀παραγγέλλει τῷ ὄχλῳ ἀναπεσεῖν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς· καὶ λαβὼν τοὺς ἑπτὰ ἄρτους εὐχαριστήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ ἐδίδου τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ἵνα ⸀παρατιθῶσιν καὶ παρέθηκαν τῷ ὄχλῳ.
verses.mistral: Distributes the loaves among the crowd, and taking the seven loaves of bread he gave thanks, broke them and began to give to his disciples to set before the crowd.
verses.kjv: And he commanded the people to sit down on the ground: and he took the seven loaves, and gave thanks, and brake, and gave to his disciples to set before them; and they did set them before the people.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶχον ἰχθύδια ὀλίγα· καὶ εὐλογήσας ⸀αὐτὰ εἶπεν ⸂καὶ ταῦτα παρατιθέναι⸃.
verses.mistral: And they had little fish; and, having given thanks, he said, "Also these to set before them."
verses.kjv: And they had a few small fishes: and he blessed, and commanded to set them also before them.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἔφαγον⸃ καὶ ἐχορτάσθησαν, καὶ ἦραν περισσεύματα κλασμάτων ἑπτὰ σπυρίδας.
verses.mistral: And they were satisfied, and took up seven large baskets of fragments.
verses.kjv: So they did eat, and were filled: and they took up of the broken meat that was left seven baskets.

verses.greek: ἦσαν ⸀δὲ ὡς τετρακισχίλιοι. καὶ ἀπέλυσεν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: Were as four thousand. And He dismissed them.
verses.kjv: And they that had eaten were about four thousand: and he sent them away.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐμβὰς εἰς τὸ πλοῖον μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ ἦλθεν εἰς τὰ μέρη Δαλμανουθά.
verses.mistral: Immediately, getting into the boat with His disciples, He went to the region of Dalmanutha.
verses.kjv: ¶ And straightway he entered into a ship with his disciples, and came into the parts of Dalmanutha.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξῆλθον οἱ Φαρισαῖοι καὶ ἤρξαντο συζητεῖν αὐτῷ, ζητοῦντες παρ’ αὐτοῦ σημεῖον ἀπὸ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, πειράζοντες αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And the Pharisees went out and began to question Him, seeking a sign from heaven, testing Him.
verses.kjv: And the Pharisees came forth, and began to question with him, seeking of him a sign from heaven, tempting him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναστενάξας τῷ πνεύματι αὐτοῦ λέγει· Τί ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη ⸂ζητεῖ σημεῖον⸃; ἀμὴν λέγω ⸀ὑμῖν, εἰ δοθήσεται τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ σημεῖον.
verses.mistral: And rising up with a mighty breath He says: "Why does this generation seek a sign? Assuredly, I say to you, no sign shall be given to this generation."
verses.kjv: And he sighed deeply in his spirit, and saith, Why doth this generation seek after a sign? verily I say unto you, There shall no sign be given unto this generation.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀφεὶς αὐτοὺς ⸂πάλιν ἐμβὰς⸃ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸ πέραν.
verses.mistral: Returning once more, He went over to the other side.
verses.kjv: And he left them, and entering into the ship again departed to the other side.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐπελάθοντο λαβεῖν ἄρτους, καὶ εἰ μὴ ἕνα ἄρτον οὐκ εἶχον μεθ’ ἑαυτῶν ἐν τῷ πλοίῳ.
verses.mistral: They forgot to take bread, and they had no more than one loaf with them in the boat.
verses.kjv: ¶ Now the disciples had forgotten to take bread, neither had they in the ship with them more than one loaf.

verses.greek: καὶ διεστέλλετο αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ὁρᾶτε, βλέπετε ἀπὸ τῆς ζύμης τῶν Φαρισαίων καὶ τῆς ζύμης Ἡρῴδου.
verses.mistral: See, look at the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod.
verses.kjv: And he charged them, saying, Take heed, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees, and of the leaven of Herod.

verses.greek: καὶ διελογίζοντο πρὸς ⸀ἀλλήλους ὅτι ἄρτους οὐκ ⸀ἔχουσιν.
verses.mistral: And they were discussing among themselves that they had no bread.
verses.kjv: And they reasoned among themselves, saying, It is because we have no bread.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀γνοὺς λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τί διαλογίζεσθε ὅτι ἄρτους οὐκ ἔχετε; οὔπω νοεῖτε οὐδὲ συνίετε; ⸀πεπωρωμένην ἔχετε τὴν καρδίαν ὑμῶν;
verses.mistral: And knowing, he says to them: Why are you discussing that you have no bread? Do you not yet perceive or understand? Have you not a heart hardened?
verses.kjv: And when Jesus knew it, he saith unto them, Why reason ye, because ye have no bread? perceive ye not yet, neither understand? have ye your heart yet hardened?

verses.greek: ὀφθαλμοὺς ἔχοντες οὐ βλέπετε καὶ ὦτα ἔχοντες οὐκ ἀκούετε; καὶ οὐ μνημονεύετε
verses.mistral: Do you not see with your eyes, and do you not hear with your ears? And can you not remember?
verses.kjv: Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?

verses.greek: ὅτε τοὺς πέντε ἄρτους ἔκλασα εἰς τοὺς πεντακισχιλίους, πόσους κοφίνους ⸂κλασμάτων πλήρεις⸃ ἤρατε; λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Δώδεκα.
verses.mistral: When the five loaves I broke for the five thousand, how many baskets full of broken pieces did you take up? They answered him: Twelve.
verses.kjv: When I brake the five loaves among five thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? They say unto him, Twelve.

verses.greek: ὅτε ⸀καὶ τοὺς ἑπτὰ εἰς τοὺς τετρακισχιλίους, πόσων σπυρίδων πληρώματα κλασμάτων ἤρατε; ⸂καὶ λέγουσιν αὐτῷ⸃· Ἑπτά.
verses.mistral: Seven baskets full of broken pieces.
verses.kjv: And when the seven among four thousand, how many baskets full of fragments took ye up? And they said, Seven.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· ⸀Οὔπω συνίετε;
verses.mistral: Understand yet?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, How is it that ye do not understand?

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀ἔρχονται εἰς Βηθσαϊδάν. καὶ φέρουσιν αὐτῷ τυφλὸν καὶ παρακαλοῦσιν αὐτὸν ἵνα αὐτοῦ ἅψηται.
verses.mistral: Coming into Bethsaida are they, bringing to Him a blind man and imploring Him to touch him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And he cometh to Bethsaida; and they bring a blind man unto him, and besought him to touch him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπιλαβόμενος τῆς χειρὸς τοῦ τυφλοῦ ⸀ἐξήνεγκεν αὐτὸν ἔξω τῆς κώμης, καὶ πτύσας εἰς τὰ ὄμματα αὐτοῦ, ἐπιθεὶς τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῷ, ἐπηρώτα αὐτόν· Εἴ τι ⸀βλέπεις;
verses.mistral: Touching the blind man's hand, He led him out of the town; and spitting on his eyes and laying hands upon him, He asked him, "Do you see anything?"
verses.kjv: And he took the blind man by the hand, and led him out of the town; and when he had spit on his eyes, and put his hands upon him, he asked him if he saw ought.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναβλέψας ἔλεγεν· Βλέπω τοὺς ἀνθρώπους ὅτι ὡς δένδρα ὁρῶ περιπατοῦντας.
verses.mistral: And looking up, he said, "I see men like trees walking."
verses.kjv: And he looked up, and said, I see men as trees, walking.

verses.greek: εἶτα πάλιν ⸀ἐπέθηκεν τὰς χεῖρας ἐπὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς αὐτοῦ, καὶ ⸂διέβλεψεν καὶ ἀπεκατέστη καὶ ἐνέβλεπεν⸃ τηλαυγῶς ⸀ἅπαντα.
verses.mistral: Then again he placed his hands upon his eyes, and shone with great brightness, and looked steadfastly at all.
verses.kjv: After that he put his hands again upon his eyes, and made him look up: and he was restored, and saw every man clearly.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν αὐτὸν ⸀εἰς οἶκον αὐτοῦ λέγων· Μηδὲ εἰς τὴν κώμην ⸀εἰσέλθῃς.
verses.mistral: Send him to his home, saying, "Do not go into the village."
verses.kjv: And he sent him away to his house, saying, Neither go into the town, nor tell it to any in the town.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὰς κώμας Καισαρείας τῆς Φιλίππου· καὶ ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ ἐπηρώτα τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ λέγων αὐτοῖς· Τίνα με λέγουσιν οἱ ἄνθρωποι εἶναι;
verses.mistral: Who do people say that I am?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus went out, and his disciples, into the towns of Caesarea Philippi: and by the way he asked his disciples, saying unto them, Whom do men say that I am?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ⸂εἶπαν αὐτῷ λέγοντες⸃ ⸀ὅτι Ἰωάννην τὸν βαπτιστήν, καὶ ἄλλοι Ἠλίαν, ἄλλοι δὲ ⸂ὅτι εἷς⸃ τῶν προφητῶν.
verses.mistral: They replied to Him, saying, "John the Baptist, and others Elias, and others one of the prophets."
verses.kjv: And they answered, John the Baptist: but some say, Elias; and others, One of the prophets.

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτὸς ⸂ἐπηρώτα αὐτούς⸃· Ὑμεῖς δὲ τίνα με λέγετε εἶναι; ⸀ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Πέτρος λέγει αὐτῷ· Σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστός.
verses.mistral: You ask them, "Who do you say that I am?" Responding, Peter says to Him, "You are the Christ."
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, But whom say ye that I am? And Peter answereth and saith unto him, Thou art the Christ.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπετίμησεν αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ λέγωσιν περὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And he charged them that they should not tell anyone anything about him.
verses.kjv: And he charged them that they should tell no man of him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἤρξατο διδάσκειν αὐτοὺς ὅτι δεῖ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου πολλὰ παθεῖν καὶ ἀποδοκιμασθῆναι ⸀ὑπὸ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων καὶ τῶν ἀρχιερέων καὶ τῶν γραμματέων καὶ ἀποκτανθῆναι καὶ μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ἀναστῆναι·
verses.mistral: The Son of Man must suffer many things and be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.
verses.kjv: And he began to teach them, that the Son of man must suffer many things, and be rejected of the elders, and of the chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and after three days rise again.

verses.greek: καὶ παρρησίᾳ τὸν λόγον ἐλάλει. καὶ προσλαβόμενος ⸂ὁ Πέτρος αὐτὸν⸃ ἤρξατο ἐπιτιμᾶν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And with frankness He spoke the word. And, taking Him aside, Peter began to rebuke Him.
verses.kjv: And he spake that saying openly. And Peter took him, and began to rebuke him.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐπιστραφεὶς καὶ ἰδὼν τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ἐπετίμησεν ⸀Πέτρῳ ⸂καὶ λέγει⸃· Ὕπαγε ὀπίσω μου, Σατανᾶ, ὅτι οὐ φρονεῖς τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ ἀλλὰ τὰ τῶν ἀνθρώπων.
verses.mistral: Returns and seeing His disciples, He rebuked Peter, saying, "Go behind Me, Satan! You are not mindful of the things of God but the things of man."
verses.kjv: But when he had turned about and looked on his disciples, he rebuked Peter, saying, Get thee behind me, Satan: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τὸν ὄχλον σὺν τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· ⸂Εἴ τις⸃ θέλει ὀπίσω μου ⸀ἐλθεῖν, ἀπαρνησάσθω ἑαυτὸν καὶ ἀράτω τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἀκολουθείτω μοι.
verses.mistral: Follow Me, and let him who wants to follow Me deny himself, take up his cross, and follow Me.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he had called the people unto him with his disciples also, he said unto them, Whosoever will come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross, and follow me.

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ ⸀ἐὰν θέλῃ τὴν ⸂ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ⸃ σῶσαι ἀπολέσει αὐτήν· ὃς δ’ ἂν ⸀ἀπολέσει τὴν ⸄ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ⸅ ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ καὶ τοῦ εὐαγγελίου ⸀σώσει αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: Whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake and the gospel's will save it.
verses.kjv: For whosoever will save his life shall lose it; but whosoever shall lose his life for my sake and the gospel's, the same shall save it.

verses.greek: τί γὰρ ⸀ὠφελεῖ ⸀ἄνθρωπον ⸂κερδῆσαι τὸν κόσμον ὅλον καὶ ζημιωθῆναι⸃ τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ;
verses.mistral: What profit is it to a man if he gains the whole world and loses his soul?
verses.kjv: For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?

verses.greek: ⸂τί γὰρ⸃ ⸀δοῖ ἄνθρωπος ἀντάλλαγμα τῆς ψυχῆς αὐτοῦ;
verses.mistral: Therefore, what does it profit a man to gain the whole world?
verses.kjv: Or what shall a man give in exchange for his soul?

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ ἐὰν ἐπαισχυνθῇ με καὶ τοὺς ἐμοὺς λόγους ἐν τῇ γενεᾷ ταύτῃ τῇ μοιχαλίδι καὶ ἁμαρτωλῷ, καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐπαισχυνθήσεται αὐτὸν ὅταν ἔλθῃ ἐν τῇ δόξῃ τοῦ πατρὸς αὐτοῦ μετὰ τῶν ἀγγέλων τῶν ἁγίων.
verses.mistral: Whoever acknowledges me before others, I will also acknowledge before my Father in heaven. But whoever denies me before others, I will also deny them before my Father in heaven.
verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall be ashamed of me and of my words in this adulterous and sinful generation; of him also shall the Son of man be ashamed, when he cometh in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι εἰσίν τινες ⸂τῶν ὧδε⸃ ἑστηκότων οἵτινες οὐ μὴ γεύσωνται θανάτου ἕως ἂν ἴδωσιν τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ἐληλυθυῖαν ἐν δυνάμει.
verses.mistral: And he said to them, "Truly, I say to you, some who are standing here will not taste death until they see the kingdom of God coming with power."
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Verily I say unto you, That there be some of them that stand here, which shall not taste of death, till they have seen the kingdom of God come with power.

verses.greek: Καὶ μετὰ ἡμέρας ἓξ παραλαμβάνει ὁ Ἰησοῦς τὸν Πέτρον καὶ τὸν Ἰάκωβον ⸀καὶ Ἰωάννην, καὶ ἀναφέρει αὐτοὺς εἰς ὄρος ὑψηλὸν κατ’ ἰδίαν μόνους. καὶ μετεμορφώθη ἔμπροσθεν αὐτῶν,
verses.mistral: And after six days Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John, and led them up to a high mountain by themselves. And he was transfigured before them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And after six days Jesus taketh with him Peter, and James, and John, and leadeth them up into an high mountain apart by themselves: and he was transfigured before them.

verses.greek: καὶ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ ⸀ἐγένετο στίλβοντα λευκὰ ⸀λίαν οἷα γναφεὺς ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς οὐ δύναται ⸀οὕτως λευκᾶναι.
verses.mistral: And his garments became radiant, extremely white, like no fuller on earth could bleach them so white.
verses.kjv: And his raiment became shining, exceeding white as snow; so as no fuller on earth can white them.

verses.greek: καὶ ὤφθη αὐτοῖς Ἠλίας σὺν Μωϋσεῖ, καὶ ἦσαν συλλαλοῦντες τῷ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: And they saw Elijah with Moses, conversing with Jesus.
verses.kjv: And there appeared unto them Elias with Moses: and they were talking with Jesus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Πέτρος λέγει τῷ Ἰησοῦ· Ῥαββί, καλόν ἐστιν ἡμᾶς ὧδε εἶναι, καὶ ποιήσωμεν ⸂τρεῖς σκηνάς⸃, σοὶ μίαν καὶ Μωϋσεῖ μίαν καὶ Ἠλίᾳ μίαν.
verses.mistral: And Peter responding said to Jesus: Rabbi, it is good for us to be here; and let us make three tents - one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.
verses.kjv: And Peter answered and said to Jesus, Master, it is good for us to be here: and let us make three tabernacles; one for thee, and one for Moses, and one for Elias.

verses.greek: οὐ γὰρ ᾔδει τί ⸀ἀποκριθῇ, ⸂ἔκφοβοι γὰρ ἐγένοντο⸃.
verses.mistral: Fear overcame them.
verses.kjv: For he wist not what to say; for they were sore afraid.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐγένετο νεφέλη ἐπισκιάζουσα αὐτοῖς, καὶ ⸀ἐγένετο φωνὴ ἐκ τῆς νεφέλης· Οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ υἱός μου ὁ ἀγαπητός, ⸂ἀκούετε αὐτοῦ⸃.
verses.mistral: And a cloud took shape over them, and there was a voice from the cloud: "This is my beloved Son; listen to him."
verses.kjv: And there was a cloud that overshadowed them: and a voice came out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son: hear him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξάπινα περιβλεψάμενοι οὐκέτι οὐδένα εἶδον ⸂ἀλλὰ τὸν Ἰησοῦν μόνον μεθ’ ἑαυτῶν⸃.
verses.mistral: Immediately they saw no one with them, but Jesus only with themselves.
verses.kjv: And suddenly, when they had looked round about, they saw no man any more, save Jesus only with themselves.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ καταβαινόντων⸃ αὐτῶν ⸀ἐκ τοῦ ὄρους διεστείλατο αὐτοῖς ἵνα μηδενὶ ⸂ἃ εἶδον διηγήσωνται⸃, εἰ μὴ ὅταν ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκ νεκρῶν ἀναστῇ.
verses.mistral: And as they were going down from the mountain, he commanded them that they should not tell anyone what they had seen, until the Son of Man was raised from the dead.
verses.kjv: And as they came down from the mountain, he charged them that they should tell no man what things they had seen, till the Son of man were risen from the dead.

verses.greek: καὶ τὸν λόγον ἐκράτησαν πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς συζητοῦντες τί ἐστιν τὸ ἐκ νεκρῶν ἀναστῆναι.
verses.mistral: And they discussed with one another about what it means for someone to rise from the dead.
verses.kjv: And they kept that saying with themselves, questioning one with another what the rising from the dead should mean.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν λέγοντες· Ὅτι λέγουσιν οἱ γραμματεῖς ὅτι Ἠλίαν δεῖ ἐλθεῖν πρῶτον;
verses.mistral: Is it necessary that Elijah come first?
verses.kjv: ¶ And they asked him, saying, Why say the scribes that Elias must first come?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ⸀ἔφη αὐτοῖς· Ἠλίας μὲν ἐλθὼν πρῶτον ⸀ἀποκαθιστάνει πάντα, καὶ πῶς γέγραπται ἐπὶ τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἵνα πολλὰ πάθῃ καὶ ⸀ἐξουδενηθῇ;
verses.mistral: Elijah indeed came first and restored all things, and how is it written concerning the Son of Man that he should suffer many things and be rejected?
verses.kjv: And he answered and told them, Elias verily cometh first, and restoreth all things; and how it is written of the Son of man, that he must suffer many things, and be set at nought.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι καὶ Ἠλίας ἐλήλυθεν, καὶ ἐποίησαν αὐτῷ ὅσα ⸀ἤθελον, καθὼς γέγραπται ἐπ’ αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: But I tell you truly, Elijah has come; and they did to him whatever they wished, as it is written concerning him.
verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That Elias is indeed come, and they have done unto him whatsoever they listed, as it is written of him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸂ἐλθόντες πρὸς τοὺς μαθητὰς εἶδον⸃ ὄχλον πολὺν περὶ αὐτοὺς καὶ γραμματεῖς συζητοῦντας ⸂πρὸς αὐτούς⸃.
verses.mistral: Approaching the disciples, they saw a large crowd around them and some scribes debating with them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he came to his disciples, he saw a great multitude about them, and the scribes questioning with them.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς πᾶς ὁ ὄχλος ⸂ἰδόντες αὐτὸν ἐξεθαμβήθησαν⸃, καὶ προστρέχοντες ἠσπάζοντο αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: All the crowd, seeing Him, were amazed, and running up to Him, they greeted Him.
verses.kjv: And straightway all the people, when they beheld him, were greatly amazed, and running to him saluted him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτησεν ⸀αὐτούς· Τί συζητεῖτε πρὸς ⸀αὑτούς;
verses.mistral: Asked them, "What are you discussing among yourselves?"
verses.kjv: And he asked the scribes, What question ye with them?

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂ἀπεκρίθη αὐτῷ εἷς ἐκ τοῦ ὄχλου⸃· Διδάσκαλε, ἤνεγκα τὸν υἱόν μου πρὸς σέ, ἔχοντα πνεῦμα ἄλαλον·
verses.mistral: And one from the crowd answered Him, "Teacher, I brought my son to You, having an unresponsive spirit."
verses.kjv: And one of the multitude answered and said, Master, I have brought unto thee my son, which hath a dumb spirit;

verses.greek: καὶ ὅπου ⸀ἐὰν αὐτὸν καταλάβῃ ῥήσσει αὐτόν, καὶ ἀφρίζει καὶ τρίζει τοὺς ⸀ὀδόντας καὶ ξηραίνεται· καὶ εἶπα τοῖς μαθηταῖς σου ἵνα αὐτὸ ἐκβάλωσιν, καὶ οὐκ ἴσχυσαν.
verses.mistral: And where it can be seized, seize him, and he shrieks and foams and grinds his teeth and becomes dry. I said to your disciples that they should cast him out, and they could not.
verses.kjv: And wheresoever he taketh him, he teareth him: and he foameth, and gnasheth with his teeth, and pineth away: and I spake to thy disciples that they should cast him out; and they could not.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς ⸀αὐτοῖς λέγει· Ὦ γενεὰ ἄπιστος, ἕως πότε πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἔσομαι; ἕως πότε ἀνέξομαι ὑμῶν; φέρετε αὐτὸν πρός με.
verses.mistral: Unbelieving generation, how long shall I be with you? How long shall I bear with you? Bring him to me.
verses.kjv: He answereth him, and saith, O faithless generation, how long shall I be with you? how long shall I suffer you? bring him unto me.

verses.greek: καὶ ἤνεγκαν αὐτὸν πρὸς αὐτόν. καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὸν ⸂τὸ πνεῦμα εὐθὺς⸃ ⸀συνεσπάραξεν αὐτόν, καὶ πεσὼν ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἐκυλίετο ἀφρίζων.
verses.mistral: And they brought him to Him. And looking upon him immediately the spirit convulsed him, and he fell on the ground and foamed at the mouth.
verses.kjv: And they brought him unto him: and when he saw him, straightway the spirit tare him; and he fell on the ground, and wallowed foaming.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτησεν τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ· Πόσος χρόνος ἐστὶν ὡς τοῦτο γέγονεν αὐτῷ; ὁ δὲ εἶπεν· ⸀Ἐκ παιδιόθεν·
verses.mistral: Father, how long has this been happening to him? But he replied, "From childhood."
verses.kjv: And he asked his father, How long is it ago since this came unto him? And he said, Of a child.

verses.greek: καὶ πολλάκις ⸂καὶ εἰς πῦρ αὐτὸν⸃ ἔβαλεν καὶ εἰς ὕδατα ἵνα ἀπολέσῃ αὐτόν· ἀλλ’ εἴ τι ⸀δύνῃ, βοήθησον ἡμῖν σπλαγχνισθεὶς ἐφ’ ἡμᾶς.
verses.mistral: And oftentimes he cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.
verses.kjv: And ofttimes it hath cast him into the fire, and into the waters, to destroy him: but if thou canst do any thing, have compassion on us, and help us.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Τὸ Εἰ ⸀δύνῃ, πάντα δυνατὰ τῷ πιστεύοντι.
verses.mistral: Jesus said to him, "If you can believe, all things are possible to him who believes."
verses.kjv: Jesus said unto him, If thou canst believe, all things are possible to him that believeth.

verses.greek: ⸀εὐθὺς κράξας ὁ πατὴρ τοῦ ⸀παιδίου ἔλεγεν· ⸀Πιστεύω· βοήθει μου τῇ ἀπιστίᾳ.
verses.mistral: Immediately the father of the boy cried out and said, "I believe; help my unbelief."
verses.kjv: And straightway the father of the child cried out, and said with tears, Lord, I believe; help thou mine unbelief.

verses.greek: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὅτι ἐπισυντρέχει ὄχλος ἐπετίμησεν τῷ πνεύματι τῷ ἀκαθάρτῳ λέγων αὐτῷ· Τὸ ⸂ἄλαλον καὶ κωφὸν πνεῦμα⸃, ἐγὼ ⸂ἐπιτάσσω σοι⸃, ἔξελθε ἐξ αὐτοῦ καὶ μηκέτι εἰσέλθῃς εἰς αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Silent and deaf spirit, I command you, come out of him and do not enter again.
verses.kjv: When Jesus saw that the people came running together, he rebuked the foul spirit, saying unto him, Thou dumb and deaf spirit, I charge thee, come out of him, and enter no more into him.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂κράξας καὶ πολλὰ σπαράξας⸃ ἐξῆλθεν· καὶ ἐγένετο ὡσεὶ νεκρὸς ὥστε ⸀τοὺς πολλοὺς λέγειν ὅτι ἀπέθανεν.
verses.mistral: And crying out and throwing himself into convulsions, he came out. And he appeared to be dead so that many said he had died.
verses.kjv: And the spirit cried, and rent him sore, and came out of him: and he was as one dead; insomuch that many said, He is dead.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς κρατήσας ⸂τῆς χειρὸς αὐτοῦ⸃ ἤγειρεν αὐτόν, καὶ ἀνέστη.
verses.mistral: Jesus seized him by the hand and raised him up, and he stood.
verses.kjv: But Jesus took him by the hand, and lifted him up; and he arose.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂εἰσελθόντος αὐτοῦ⸃ εἰς οἶκον οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ⸂κατ’ ἰδίαν ἐπηρώτων αὐτόν⸃· Ὅτι ἡμεῖς οὐκ ἠδυνήθημεν ἐκβαλεῖν αὐτό;
verses.mistral: And entering into the house, his disciples asked him privately, "Why could we not cast him out?"
verses.kjv: And when he was come into the house, his disciples asked him privately, Why could not we cast him out?

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τοῦτο τὸ γένος ἐν οὐδενὶ δύναται ἐξελθεῖν εἰ μὴ ἐν ⸀προσευχῇ.
verses.mistral: This generation can't be driven out except by prayer.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, This kind can come forth by nothing, but by prayer and fasting.

verses.greek: Κἀκεῖθεν ἐξελθόντες ⸀παρεπορεύοντο διὰ τῆς Γαλιλαίας, καὶ οὐκ ἤθελεν ἵνα τις γνοῖ·
verses.mistral: Leaving there, they passed through Galilee. And He did not want anyone to know.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they departed thence, and passed through Galilee; and he would not that any man should know it.

verses.greek: ἐδίδασκεν γὰρ τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ καὶ ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς ὅτι Ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται εἰς χεῖρας ἀνθρώπων, καὶ ἀποκτενοῦσιν αὐτόν, καὶ ἀποκτανθεὶς ⸂μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας⸃ ἀναστήσεται.
verses.mistral: The Son of Man is delivered into the hands of men, and they will kill Him, and after He is killed, He will rise in three days.
verses.kjv: For he taught his disciples, and said unto them, The Son of man is delivered into the hands of men, and they shall kill him; and after that he is killed, he shall rise the third day.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἠγνόουν τὸ ῥῆμα, καὶ ἐφοβοῦντο αὐτὸν ἐπερωτῆσαι.
verses.mistral: They did not understand the saying, and they were afraid to ask him.
verses.kjv: But they understood not that saying, and were afraid to ask him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀ἦλθον εἰς Καφαρναούμ. καὶ ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ γενόμενος ἐπηρώτα αὐτούς· Τί ἐν τῇ ⸀ὁδῷ διελογίζεσθε;
verses.mistral: And he came to Capernaum. And being in the house, he asked them: "What do you discuss on the way?"
verses.kjv: ¶ And he came to Capernaum: and being in the house he asked them, What was it that ye disputed among yourselves by the way?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἐσιώπων, πρὸς ἀλλήλους γὰρ διελέχθησαν ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ τίς μείζων.
verses.mistral: They fell silent, for they were discussing among themselves on the way who was greater.
verses.kjv: But they held their peace: for by the way they had disputed among themselves, who should be the greatest.

verses.greek: καὶ καθίσας ἐφώνησεν τοὺς δώδεκα καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Εἴ τις θέλει πρῶτος εἶναι ἔσται πάντων ἔσχατος καὶ πάντων διάκονος.
verses.mistral: And sitting down, He called the twelve and said to them: "If anyone desires to be first, he shall be last of all and servant of all."
verses.kjv: And he sat down, and called the twelve, and saith unto them, If any man desire to be first, the same shall be last of all, and servant of all.

verses.greek: καὶ λαβὼν παιδίον ἔστησεν αὐτὸ ἐν μέσῳ αὐτῶν καὶ ἐναγκαλισάμενος αὐτὸ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς·
verses.mistral: Holds a child and sets it in their midst, and embracing him said to them:
verses.kjv: And he took a child, and set him in the midst of them: and when he had taken him in his arms, he said unto them,

verses.greek: Ὃς ⸀ἂν ἓν τῶν τοιούτων παιδίων δέξηται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου, ἐμὲ δέχεται· καὶ ὃς ⸁ἂν ἐμὲ ⸀δέχηται, οὐκ ἐμὲ δέχεται ἀλλὰ τὸν ἀποστείλαντά με.
verses.mistral: Whoever accepts one of these little children in my name, accepts me; and whoever accepts me, does not accept me but the one who sent me.
verses.kjv: Whosoever shall receive one of such children in my name, receiveth me: and whosoever shall receive me, receiveth not me, but him that sent me.

verses.greek: ⸂Ἔφη αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰωάννης⸃· Διδάσκαλε, εἴδομέν τινα ⸀ἐν τῷ ὀνόματί σου ἐκβάλλοντα δαιμόνια, ⸂καὶ ἐκωλύομεν αὐτόν, ὅτι οὐκ ἠκολούθει ἡμῖν⸃.
verses.mistral: Teacher, we have seen one who is casting out demons in your name; we tried to stop him because he does not follow us.
verses.kjv: ¶ And John answered him, saying, Master, we saw one casting out devils in thy name, and he followeth not us: and we forbad him, because he followeth not us.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν· Μὴ κωλύετε αὐτόν, οὐδεὶς γάρ ἐστιν ὃς ποιήσει δύναμιν ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου καὶ δυνήσεται ταχὺ κακολογῆσαί με·
verses.mistral: Do not stop him, for no one who performs a miracle in my name can soon speak ill of me.
verses.kjv: But Jesus said, Forbid him not: for there is no man which shall do a miracle in my name, that can lightly speak evil of me.

verses.greek: ὃς γὰρ οὐκ ἔστιν καθ’ ⸂ἡμῶν, ὑπὲρ ἡμῶν⸃ ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: He who is not for us, is against us.
verses.kjv: For he that is not against us is on our part.

verses.greek: Ὃς γὰρ ἂν ποτίσῃ ὑμᾶς ποτήριον ὕδατος ἐν ⸀ὀνόματι ὅτι χριστοῦ ἐστε, ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ⸀ὅτι οὐ μὴ ⸀ἀπολέσῃ τὸν μισθὸν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Whoever gives you a cup of water in my name, truly I say to you, he will not lose his reward.
verses.kjv: For whosoever shall give you a cup of water to drink in my name, because ye belong to Christ, verily I say unto you, he shall not lose his reward.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὃς ⸀ἂν σκανδαλίσῃ ἕνα τῶν μικρῶν ⸀τούτων τῶν πιστευόντων ⸂εἰς ἐμέ⸃, καλόν ἐστιν αὐτῷ μᾶλλον εἰ περίκειται ⸂μύλος ὀνικὸς⸃ περὶ τὸν τράχηλον αὐτοῦ καὶ βέβληται εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν.
verses.mistral: Whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if a millstone were hung around his neck and he were thrown into the sea.
verses.kjv: And whosoever shall offend one of these little ones that believe in me, it is better for him that a millstone were hanged about his neck, and he were cast into the sea.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐὰν ⸀σκανδαλίζῃ σε ἡ χείρ σου, ἀπόκοψον αὐτήν· καλόν ⸂ἐστίν σε⸃ κυλλὸν ⸂εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν ζωὴν⸃ ἢ τὰς δύο χεῖρας ἔχοντα ἀπελθεῖν εἰς τὴν γέενναν, εἰς τὸ πῦρ τὸ ⸀ἄσβεστον.
verses.mistral: Cut off your hand if it causes you to sin; it is better for you to enter life crippled than with two hands to go into the unquenchable fire, hell.
verses.kjv: And if thy hand offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter into life maimed, than having two hands to go into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν ὁ πούς σου σκανδαλίζῃ σε, ἀπόκοψον αὐτόν· καλόν ἐστίν ⸀σε εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν ζωὴν χωλὸν ἢ τοὺς δύο πόδας ἔχοντα βληθῆναι εἰς τὴν ⸀γέενναν.
verses.mistral: Cut off your hand if it causes you to sin; it is better for you to enter life crippled than with two hands to go into the unquenchable fire, hell.
verses.kjv: And if thy foot offend thee, cut it off: it is better for thee to enter halt into life, than having two feet to be cast into hell, into the fire that never shall be quenched:

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου σκανδαλίζῃ σε, ἔκβαλε αὐτόν· καλόν ⸀σέ ἐστιν μονόφθαλμον εἰσελθεῖν εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ἢ δύο ὀφθαλμοὺς ἔχοντα βληθῆναι εἰς ⸀τὴν ⸀γέενναν,
verses.mistral: And if your eye causes you to sin, pluck it out; it is better for you to enter the kingdom of God with one eye than having two eyes to be thrown into hell.
verses.kjv: And if thine eye offend thee, pluck it out: it is better for thee to enter into the kingdom of God with one eye, than having two eyes to be cast into hell fire:

verses.greek: ὅπου ὁ σκώληξ αὐτῶν οὐ τελευτᾷ καὶ τὸ πῦρ οὐ σβέννυται.
verses.mistral: Where the worm does not die, and the fire is not quenched.
verses.kjv: Where their worm dieth not, and the fire is not quenched.

verses.greek: Πᾶς γὰρ πυρὶ ⸀ἁλισθήσεται.
verses.mistral: All will be salted with fire.
verses.kjv: For every one shall be salted with fire, and every sacrifice shall be salted with salt.

verses.greek: καλὸν τὸ ἅλας· ἐὰν δὲ τὸ ἅλας ἄναλον γένηται, ἐν τίνι αὐτὸ ἀρτύσετε; ἔχετε ἐν ἑαυτοῖς ⸀ἅλα, καὶ εἰρηνεύετε ἐν ἀλλήλοις.
verses.mistral: Good salt is worthless if it loses its saltiness; how can you make it salty again? Have salt among yourselves and live in peace with each other.
verses.kjv: Salt is good: but if the salt have lost his saltness, wherewith will ye season it? Have salt in yourselves, and have peace one with another.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐκεῖθεν ἀναστὰς ἔρχεται εἰς τὰ ὅρια τῆς Ἰουδαίας ⸀καὶ πέραν τοῦ Ἰορδάνου, καὶ συμπορεύονται πάλιν ὄχλοι πρὸς αὐτόν, καὶ ὡς εἰώθει πάλιν ἐδίδασκεν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: And from there He arose and came to the borders of Judea on the other side of the Jordan; and again crowds gathered to Him, and as was His custom, He continued to teach them.
verses.kjv: And he arose from thence, and cometh into the coasts of Judaea by the farther side of Jordan: and the people resort unto him again; and, as he was wont, he taught them again.

verses.greek: ⸀Καὶ ⸀ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν εἰ ἔξεστιν ἀνδρὶ γυναῖκα ἀπολῦσαι, πειράζοντες αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And they asked Him if it was lawful for a man to divorce a woman, testing Him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And the Pharisees came to him, and asked him, Is it lawful for a man to put away his wife? tempting him.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί ὑμῖν ἐνετείλατο Μωϋσῆς;
verses.mistral: And He answered them, "What did Moses command you?"
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, What did Moses command you?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν· ⸂Ἐπέτρεψεν Μωϋσῆς⸃ βιβλίον ἀποστασίου γράψαι καὶ ἀπολῦσαι.
verses.mistral: Moses permitted a book of the dissenters to be written and released.
verses.kjv: And they said, Moses suffered to write a bill of divorcement, and to put her away.

verses.greek: ⸂ὁ δὲ⸃ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Πρὸς τὴν σκληροκαρδίαν ὑμῶν ἔγραψεν ὑμῖν τὴν ἐντολὴν ταύτην·
verses.mistral: Therefore Jesus said to them, 'Because of your unyielding hearts, I have given you this command:'
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, For the hardness of your heart he wrote you this precept.

verses.greek: ἀπὸ δὲ ἀρχῆς κτίσεως ἄρσεν καὶ θῆλυ ἐποίησεν ⸀αὐτούς·
verses.mistral: Created them male and female.
verses.kjv: But from the beginning of the creation God made them male and female.

verses.greek: ἕνεκεν τούτου καταλείψει ἄνθρωπος τὸν πατέρα αὐτοῦ καὶ τὴν μητέρα ⸂καὶ προσκολληθήσεται πρὸς τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ⸃,
verses.mistral: Leaves behind his father and mother for the sake of his wife.
verses.kjv: For this cause shall a man leave his father and mother, and cleave to his wife;

verses.greek: καὶ ἔσονται οἱ δύο εἰς σάρκα μίαν· ὥστε οὐκέτι εἰσὶν δύο ἀλλὰ μία σάρξ·
verses.mistral: They will be one flesh, so that they are no longer two but one flesh.
verses.kjv: And they twain shall be one flesh: so then they are no more twain, but one flesh.

verses.greek: ὃ οὖν ὁ θεὸς συνέζευξεν ἄνθρωπος μὴ χωριζέτω.
verses.mistral: Therefore what God has joined together, let not man separate.
verses.kjv: What therefore God hath joined together, let not man put asunder.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸂εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν⸃ πάλιν οἱ μαθηταὶ ⸂περὶ τούτου⸃ ⸀ἐπηρώτων αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And again, in the house, the disciples questioned Him about this.
verses.kjv: And in the house his disciples asked him again of the same matter.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ὃς ⸀ἂν ἀπολύσῃ τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ καὶ γαμήσῃ ἄλλην μοιχᾶται ἐπ’ αὐτήν,
verses.mistral: And He says to them, "Whoever divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery against her."
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Whosoever shall put away his wife, and marry another, committeth adultery against her.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐὰν ⸂αὐτὴ ἀπολύσασα τὸν ἄνδρα αὐτῆς γαμήσῃ ἄλλον⸃ μοιχᾶται.
verses.mistral: Marries another, she commits adultery.
verses.kjv: And if a woman shall put away her husband, and be married to another, she committeth adultery.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσέφερον αὐτῷ παιδία ἵνα ⸂αὐτῶν ἅψηται⸃· οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ ⸂ἐπετίμησαν αὐτοῖς⸃.
verses.mistral: And they brought children to Him, so that He might touch them; but the disciples rebuked them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they brought young children to him, that he should touch them: and his disciples rebuked those that brought them.

verses.greek: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἠγανάκτησεν καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ἄφετε τὰ παιδία ἔρχεσθαι πρός με, μὴ κωλύετε αὐτά, τῶν γὰρ τοιούτων ἐστὶν ἡ βασιλεία τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: Jesus looked at them with anger and said to them, "Allow the children to come to me; do not forbid it, for such is the kingdom of God."
verses.kjv: But when Jesus saw it, he was much displeased, and said unto them, Suffer the little children to come unto me, and forbid them not: for of such is the kingdom of God.

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ὃς ⸀ἂν μὴ δέξηται τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ ὡς παιδίον, οὐ μὴ εἰσέλθῃ εἰς αὐτήν.
verses.mistral: Whoever does not receive the kingdom of God as a child shall not enter it.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Whosoever shall not receive the kingdom of God as a little child, he shall not enter therein.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐναγκαλισάμενος αὐτὰ ⸂κατευλόγει τιθεὶς τὰς χεῖρας ἐπ’ αὐτά⸃.
verses.mistral: And he blessed them, laying his hands on them.
verses.kjv: And he took them up in his arms, put his hands upon them, and blessed them.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐκπορευομένου αὐτοῦ εἰς ὁδὸν προσδραμὼν εἷς καὶ γονυπετήσας αὐτὸν ἐπηρώτα αὐτόν· Διδάσκαλε ἀγαθέ, τί ποιήσω ἵνα ζωὴν αἰώνιον κληρονομήσω;
verses.mistral: Teacher of goodness, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
verses.kjv: ¶ And when he was gone forth into the way, there came one running, and kneeled to him, and asked him, Good Master, what shall I do that I may inherit eternal life?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Τί με λέγεις ἀγαθόν; οὐδεὶς ἀγαθὸς εἰ μὴ εἷς ὁ θεός.
verses.mistral: Jesus said to him, "Why do you call me good? No one is good except God."
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Why callest thou me good? there is none good but one, that is, God.

verses.greek: τὰς ἐντολὰς οἶδας· Μὴ ⸂φονεύσῃς, Μὴ μοιχεύσῃς⸃, Μὴ κλέψῃς, Μὴ ψευδομαρτυρήσῃς, Μὴ ἀποστερήσῃς, Τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα.
verses.mistral: Obey the commands: Do not kill, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony, do not defraud, honor your father and mother.
verses.kjv: Thou knowest the commandments, Do not commit adultery, Do not kill, Do not steal, Do not bear false witness, Defraud not, Honour thy father and mother.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ⸀ἔφη αὐτῷ· Διδάσκαλε, ταῦτα πάντα ἐφυλαξάμην ἐκ νεότητός μου.
verses.mistral: The man replied, "Teacher, I have kept all these things from my youth."
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto him, Master, all these have I observed from my youth.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ἐμβλέψας αὐτῷ ἠγάπησεν αὐτὸν καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Ἕν ⸀σε ὑστερεῖ· ὕπαγε ὅσα ἔχεις πώλησον καὶ δὸς ⸀τοῖς πτωχοῖς, καὶ ἕξεις θησαυρὸν ἐν οὐρανῷ, καὶ δεῦρο ἀκολούθει ⸀μοι.
verses.mistral: Jesus looked at him and loved him, and said to him, "One thing you lack: go, sell all that you have and give it to the poor, and you will have a treasure in heaven, and come follow me."
verses.kjv: Then Jesus beholding him loved him, and said unto him, One thing thou lackest: go thy way, sell whatsoever thou hast, and give to the poor, and thou shalt have treasure in heaven: and come, take up the cross, and follow me.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ στυγνάσας ἐπὶ τῷ λόγῳ ἀπῆλθεν λυπούμενος, ἦν γὰρ ἔχων κτήματα πολλά.
verses.mistral: Deeply saddened by the word, he went away sorrowful, for he had many possessions.
verses.kjv: And he was sad at that saying, and went away grieved: for he had great possessions.

verses.greek: Καὶ περιβλεψάμενος ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· Πῶς δυσκόλως οἱ τὰ χρήματα ἔχοντες εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελεύσονται.
verses.mistral: And Jesus looking at them says to his disciples: How hard it is for those who have possessions to enter the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus looked round about, and saith unto his disciples, How hardly shall they that have riches enter into the kingdom of God!

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ μαθηταὶ ἐθαμβοῦντο ἐπὶ τοῖς λόγοις αὐτοῦ. ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς πάλιν ἀποκριθεὶς λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τέκνα, πῶς δύσκολόν ⸀ἐστιν εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελθεῖν·
verses.mistral: The disciples were amazed at his words. But Jesus, again responding, says to them: "Children, how hard it is to enter the kingdom of God..."
verses.kjv: And the disciples were astonished at his words. But Jesus answereth again, and saith unto them, Children, how hard is it for them that trust in riches to enter into the kingdom of God!

verses.greek: εὐκοπώτερόν ἐστιν κάμηλον διὰ ⸂τῆς τρυμαλιᾶς τῆς⸃ ῥαφίδος ⸀διελθεῖν ἢ πλούσιον εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ εἰσελθεῖν.
verses.mistral: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ περισσῶς ἐξεπλήσσοντο λέγοντες πρὸς ⸀ἑαυτούς· Καὶ τίς δύναται σωθῆναι;
verses.mistral: Who can be saved?
verses.kjv: And they were astonished out of measure, saying among themselves, Who then can be saved?

verses.greek: ⸀ἐμβλέψας αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει· Παρὰ ἀνθρώποις ἀδύνατον ἀλλ’ οὐ παρὰ θεῷ, πάντα γὰρ ⸀δυνατὰ παρὰ τῷ θεῷ.
verses.mistral: All things are possible with God. > ⸀ · ’ , ⸀ . And the translation is: > All things are possible with God.
verses.kjv: And Jesus looking upon them saith, With men it is impossible, but not with God: for with God all things are possible.

verses.greek: Ἤρξατο ⸂λέγειν ὁ Πέτρος⸃ αὐτῷ· Ἰδοὺ ἡμεῖς ἀφήκαμεν πάντα καὶ ⸀ἠκολουθήκαμέν σοι.
verses.mistral: Began Peter to speak unto him, saying: Behold, we have left all, and followed thee.
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Peter began to say unto him, Lo, we have left all, and have followed thee.

verses.greek: ⸂ἔφη ὁ Ἰησοῦς⸃· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, οὐδείς ἐστιν ὃς ἀφῆκεν οἰκίαν ἢ ἀδελφοὺς ἢ ἀδελφὰς ἢ ⸂μητέρα ἢ πατέρα⸃ ἢ τέκνα ἢ ἀγροὺς ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ καὶ ἕνεκεν τοῦ εὐαγγελίου,
verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, there is no one who has left home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for my sake and for the gospel's sake, but they will receive a hundred times more in this age: homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and fields—with persecutions—and in the age to come eternal life."
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said, Verily I say unto you, There is no man that hath left house, or brethren, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my sake, and the gospel's,

verses.greek: ἐὰν μὴ λάβῃ ἑκατονταπλασίονα νῦν ἐν τῷ καιρῷ τούτῳ οἰκίας καὶ ἀδελφοὺς καὶ ἀδελφὰς καὶ ⸀μητέρας καὶ τέκνα καὶ ἀγροὺς μετὰ διωγμῶν, καὶ ἐν τῷ αἰῶνι τῷ ἐρχομένῳ ζωὴν αἰώνιον.
verses.mistral: Receives a hundredfold now in this age: homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children, and fields with persecutions, and in the coming age eternal life.
verses.kjv: But he shall receive an hundredfold now in this time, houses, and brethren, and sisters, and mothers, and children, and lands, with persecutions; and in the world to come eternal life.

verses.greek: πολλοὶ δὲ ἔσονται πρῶτοι ἔσχατοι καὶ ⸀οἱ ἔσχατοι πρῶτοι.
verses.mistral: Many will be first and last.
verses.kjv: But many that are first shall be last; and the last first.

verses.greek: Ἦσαν δὲ ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ ἀναβαίνοντες εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα, καὶ ἦν προάγων αὐτοὺς ὁ Ἰησοῦς, καὶ ἐθαμβοῦντο, ⸂οἱ δὲ⸃ ἀκολουθοῦντες ἐφοβοῦντο. καὶ παραλαβὼν πάλιν τοὺς δώδεκα ἤρξατο αὐτοῖς λέγειν τὰ μέλλοντα αὐτῷ συμβαίνειν
verses.mistral: Climbing the road towards Jerusalem were they, Jesus leading, and those following were astonished; but the ones following feared. And again taking the twelve, He began to speak to them concerning what was about to happen to Him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they were in the way going up to Jerusalem; and Jesus went before them: and they were amazed; and as they followed, they were afraid. And he took again the twelve, and began to tell them what things should happen unto him,

verses.greek: ὅτι Ἰδοὺ ἀναβαίνομεν εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα, καὶ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδοθήσεται τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν καὶ ⸀τοῖς γραμματεῦσιν, καὶ κατακρινοῦσιν αὐτὸν θανάτῳ καὶ παραδώσουσιν αὐτὸν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν
verses.mistral: They are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be betrayed to the chief priests and scribes. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the Gentiles.
verses.kjv: Saying, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of man shall be delivered unto the chief priests, and unto the scribes; and they shall condemn him to death, and shall deliver him to the Gentiles:

verses.greek: καὶ ἐμπαίξουσιν αὐτῷ καὶ ⸂ἐμπτύσουσιν αὐτῷ καὶ μαστιγώσουσιν αὐτὸν⸃ καὶ ⸀ἀποκτενοῦσιν, καὶ ⸂μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας⸃ ἀναστήσεται.
verses.mistral: And they will strike him and spit on him and flog him, and kill him; and after three days he will rise again.
verses.kjv: And they shall mock him, and shall scourge him, and shall spit upon him, and shall kill him: and the third day he shall rise again.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσπορεύονται αὐτῷ Ἰάκωβος καὶ Ἰωάννης ⸀οἱ υἱοὶ Ζεβεδαίου λέγοντες ⸀αὐτῷ· Διδάσκαλε, θέλομεν ἵνα ὃ ἐὰν αἰτήσωμέν ⸀σε ποιήσῃς ἡμῖν.
verses.mistral: Approach him Jesus, Jacob and John the sons of Zebedee saying to him: Teacher, we desire that whatever you will ask of you, you do for us.
verses.kjv: ¶ And James and John, the sons of Zebedee, come unto him, saying, Master, we would that thou shouldest do for us whatsoever we shall desire.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί θέλετε ⸀ποιήσω ὑμῖν;
verses.mistral: What do you want me to do for you?
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, What would ye that I should do for you?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Δὸς ἡμῖν ἵνα εἷς ⸂σου ἐκ δεξιῶν⸃ καὶ εἷς ἐξ ⸀ἀριστερῶν καθίσωμεν ἐν τῇ δόξῃ σου.
verses.mistral: They said to Him, "Grant us that one may sit to your right and another to Your left in Your glory."
verses.kjv: They said unto him, Grant unto us that we may sit, one on thy right hand, and the other on thy left hand, in thy glory.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Οὐκ οἴδατε τί αἰτεῖσθε· δύνασθε πιεῖν τὸ ποτήριον ὃ ἐγὼ πίνω, ⸀ἢ τὸ βάπτισμα ὃ ἐγὼ βαπτίζομαι βαπτισθῆναι;
verses.mistral: You do not know what you are asking. Can you drink the cup that I drink, or be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?
verses.kjv: But Jesus said unto them, Ye know not what ye ask: can ye drink of the cup that I drink of? and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Δυνάμεθα. ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· ⸀Τὸ ποτήριον ὃ ἐγὼ πίνω πίεσθε καὶ τὸ βάπτισμα ὃ ἐγὼ βαπτίζομαι βαπτισθήσεσθε,
verses.mistral: You can drink the cup that I drink, and you will be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with.
verses.kjv: And they said unto him, We can. And Jesus said unto them, Ye shall indeed drink of the cup that I drink of; and with the baptism that I am baptized withal shall ye be baptized:

verses.greek: τὸ δὲ καθίσαι ἐκ δεξιῶν μου ⸀ἢ ἐξ εὐωνύμων οὐκ ἔστιν ἐμὸν δοῦναι, ἀλλ’ οἷς ἡτοίμασται.
verses.mistral: Sit on my right side is not for me to grant, but for those to whom it has been prepared.
verses.kjv: But to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀκούσαντες οἱ δέκα ἤρξαντο ἀγανακτεῖν περὶ Ἰακώβου καὶ Ἰωάννου.
verses.mistral: And the ten began to grumble about James and John.
verses.kjv: And when the ten heard it, they began to be much displeased with James and John.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος αὐτοὺς ὁ Ἰησοῦς⸃ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Οἴδατε ὅτι οἱ δοκοῦντες ἄρχειν τῶν ἐθνῶν κατακυριεύουσιν αὐτῶν καὶ οἱ μεγάλοι αὐτῶν κατεξουσιάζουσιν αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: Jesus calling them to him said unto them, "The masters of the Gentiles lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them."
verses.kjv: But Jesus called them to him, and saith unto them, Ye know that they which are accounted to rule over the Gentiles exercise lordship over them; and their great ones exercise authority upon them.

verses.greek: οὐχ οὕτως δέ ⸀ἐστιν ἐν ὑμῖν· ἀλλ’ ὃς ⸀ἂν θέλῃ ⸂μέγας γενέσθαι⸃ ἐν ὑμῖν, ἔσται ὑμῶν διάκονος,
verses.mistral: One of you should become the least among all of you to be the servant.
verses.kjv: But so shall it not be among you: but whosoever will be great among you, shall be your minister:

verses.greek: καὶ ὃς ⸀ἂν θέλῃ ⸂ἐν ὑμῖν εἶναι⸃ πρῶτος, ἔσται πάντων δοῦλος·
verses.mistral: He who desires to be first among you shall be a servant of all.
verses.kjv: And whosoever of you will be the chiefest, shall be servant of all.

verses.greek: καὶ γὰρ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἦλθεν διακονηθῆναι ἀλλὰ διακονῆσαι καὶ δοῦναι τὴν ψυχὴν αὐτοῦ λύτρον ἀντὶ πολλῶν.
verses.mistral: And truly the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give His life as a ransom for many.
verses.kjv: For even the Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give his life a ransom for many.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχονται εἰς Ἰεριχώ. καὶ ἐκπορευομένου αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ Ἰεριχὼ καὶ τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ καὶ ὄχλου ἱκανοῦ ⸀ὁ υἱὸς Τιμαίου Βαρτιμαῖος ⸀τυφλὸς ⸂προσαίτης ἐκάθητο παρὰ τὴν ὁδόν⸃.
verses.mistral: And they come to Jericho. And as He went out from Jericho, and His disciples, and a great crowd, the son of Timaeus named Bartimaeus, a blind beggar, sat by the roadside.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they came to Jericho: and as he went out of Jericho with his disciples and a great number of people, blind Bartimaeus, the son of Timaeus, sat by the highway side begging.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀκούσας ὅτι Ἰησοῦς ὁ ⸀Ναζαρηνός ἐστιν ἤρξατο κράζειν καὶ λέγειν· ⸀Υἱὲ Δαυὶδ Ἰησοῦ, ἐλέησόν με.
verses.mistral: And hearing that Jesus the Nazarene is, he began to cry out and say, "Son of David, have mercy on me."
verses.kjv: And when he heard that it was Jesus of Nazareth, he began to cry out, and say, Jesus, thou Son of David, have mercy on me.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπετίμων αὐτῷ πολλοὶ ἵνα σιωπήσῃ· ὁ δὲ πολλῷ μᾶλλον ἔκραζεν· Υἱὲ Δαυίδ, ἐλέησόν με.
verses.mistral: And many admonished him to be silent; but he cried out all the more, "Son of David, have mercy on me."
verses.kjv: And many charged him that he should hold his peace: but he cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.

verses.greek: καὶ στὰς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν· ⸂Φωνήσατε αὐτόν⸃. καὶ φωνοῦσι τὸν τυφλὸν λέγοντες αὐτῷ· Θάρσει, ⸀ἔγειρε, φωνεῖ σε.
verses.mistral: Jesus stood and said, "Call him." And they call the blind man, saying to him, "Take courage, get up; he is calling you."
verses.kjv: And Jesus stood still, and commanded him to be called. And they call the blind man, saying unto him, Be of good comfort, rise; he calleth thee.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀποβαλὼν τὸ ἱμάτιον αὐτοῦ ⸀ἀναπηδήσας ἦλθεν πρὸς τὸν Ἰησοῦν.
verses.mistral: The man threw off his cloak and, leaping up, came to Jesus.
verses.kjv: And he, casting away his garment, rose, and came to Jesus.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ⸂αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν⸃· Τί ⸂σοι θέλεις ποιήσω⸃; ὁ δὲ τυφλὸς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Ραββουνι, ἵνα ἀναβλέψω.
verses.mistral: And answering Him, Jesus said to him, "What do you want Me to do for you?" The blind man said to Him, "Rabboni, that I may see."
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto him, What wilt thou that I should do unto thee? The blind man said unto him, Lord, that I might receive my sight.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ὁ⸃ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Ὕπαγε, ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε. καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἀνέβλεψεν, καὶ ἠκολούθει ⸀αὐτῷ ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ.
verses.mistral: Go, your faith has saved you. Immediately he received his sight and followed Him on the way.
verses.kjv: And Jesus said unto him, Go thy way; thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight, and followed Jesus in the way.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὅτε ἐγγίζουσιν εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα εἰς Βηθφαγὴ καὶ Βηθανίαν πρὸς τὸ Ὄρος τῶν Ἐλαιῶν, ἀποστέλλει δύο τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ
verses.mistral: Approaching Jerusalem, as they were coming near Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, He sends two of His disciples.
verses.kjv: And when they came nigh to Jerusalem, unto Bethphage and Bethany, at the mount of Olives, he sendeth forth two of his disciples,

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ὑπάγετε εἰς τὴν κώμην τὴν κατέναντι ὑμῶν, καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς εἰσπορευόμενοι εἰς αὐτὴν εὑρήσετε πῶλον δεδεμένον ἐφ’ ὃν οὐδεὶς ⸀οὔπω ἀνθρώπων ⸀ἐκάθισεν· ⸂λύσατε αὐτὸν καὶ φέρετε⸃.
verses.mistral: Bring the colt to me that is tied, on which no one yet has sat. Untie it and bring it to me.
verses.kjv: And saith unto them, Go your way into the village over against you: and as soon as ye be entered into it, ye shall find a colt tied, whereon never man sat; loose him, and bring him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐάν τις ὑμῖν εἴπῃ· Τί ποιεῖτε τοῦτο; εἴπατε ⸀ὅτι Ὁ κύριος αὐτοῦ χρείαν ἔχει· καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς αὐτὸν ἀποστέλλει ⸀πάλιν ὧδε.
verses.mistral: And if anyone says to you, "What are you doing here?" Say, "The Lord needs it," and immediately send him here.
verses.kjv: And if any man say unto you, Why do ye this? say ye that the Lord hath need of him; and straightway he will send him hither.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ἀπῆλθον⸃ καὶ εὗρον πῶλον δεδεμένον ⸀πρὸς θύραν ἔξω ἐπὶ τοῦ ἀμφόδου, καὶ λύουσιν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And they went out and found a colt tied at the door outside in the courtyard, and they untied it.
verses.kjv: And they went their way, and found the colt tied by the door without in a place where two ways met; and they loose him.

verses.greek: καί τινες τῶν ἐκεῖ ἑστηκότων ἔλεγον αὐτοῖς· Τί ποιεῖτε λύοντες τὸν πῶλον;
verses.mistral: They are saying to them, "What are you doing untying the colt?"
verses.kjv: And certain of them that stood there said unto them, What do ye, loosing the colt?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ εἶπαν αὐτοῖς καθὼς ⸀εἶπεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς· καὶ ἀφῆκαν αὐτούς.
verses.mistral: They replied to them as Jesus had told them, and they let him go.
verses.kjv: And they said unto them even as Jesus had commanded: and they let them go.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀φέρουσιν τὸν πῶλον πρὸς τὸν Ἰησοῦν, καὶ ⸀ἐπιβάλλουσιν αὐτῷ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτῶν, καὶ ἐκάθισεν ἐπ’ ⸀αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Bring the cloak to Jesus, and laying their clothes on him, he sat on them.
verses.kjv: And they brought the colt to Jesus, and cast their garments on him; and he sat upon him.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ πολλοὶ⸃ τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτῶν ἔστρωσαν εἰς τὴν ὁδόν, ἄλλοι δὲ στιβάδας ⸂κόψαντες ἐκ τῶν ἀγρῶν⸃.
verses.mistral: Many of their garments they spread on the road, others cut branches from the fields.
verses.kjv: And many spread their garments in the way: and others cut down branches off the trees, and strawed them in the way.

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ προάγοντες καὶ οἱ ἀκολουθοῦντες ⸀ἔκραζον· Ὡσαννά· Εὐλογημένος ὁ ἐρχόμενος ἐν ὀνόματι κυρίου·
verses.mistral: Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.
verses.kjv: And they that went before, and they that followed, cried, saying, Hosanna; Blessed is he that cometh in the name of the Lord:

verses.greek: Εὐλογημένη ἡ ἐρχομένη ⸀βασιλεία τοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν Δαυίδ· Ὡσαννὰ ἐν τοῖς ὑψίστοις.
verses.mistral: Blessed is the coming kingdom of our Father David. Hosanna in the highest.
verses.kjv: Blessed be the kingdom of our father David, that cometh in the name of the Lord: Hosanna in the highest.

verses.greek: Καὶ εἰσῆλθεν εἰς ⸀Ἱεροσόλυμα εἰς τὸ ἱερόν· καὶ περιβλεψάμενος πάντα ⸀ὀψὲ ἤδη οὔσης τῆς ὥρας ἐξῆλθεν εἰς Βηθανίαν μετὰ τῶν δώδεκα.
verses.mistral: Entered into Jerusalem into the temple: and looking round about at all things as yet it was late in the evening, he went out to Bethany with the twelve.
verses.kjv: And Jesus entered into Jerusalem, and into the temple: and when he had looked round about upon all things, and now the eventide was come, he went out unto Bethany with the twelve.

verses.greek: Καὶ τῇ ἐπαύριον ἐξελθόντων αὐτῶν ἀπὸ Βηθανίας ἐπείνασεν.
verses.mistral: The next day, as they were leaving Bethany, they were hungry.
verses.kjv: ¶ And on the morrow, when they were come from Bethany, he was hungry:

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδὼν συκῆν ⸀ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ἔχουσαν φύλλα ἦλθεν εἰ ἄρα ⸂τι εὑρήσει⸃ ἐν αὐτῇ, καὶ ἐλθὼν ἐπ’ αὐτὴν οὐδὲν εὗρεν εἰ μὴ φύλλα, ⸂ὁ γὰρ καιρὸς οὐκ ἦν⸃ σύκων.
verses.mistral: He saw a fig tree from afar, having leaves; and going to it he found nothing but leaves, for the season was not yet for figs.
verses.kjv: And seeing a fig tree afar off having leaves, he came, if haply he might find any thing thereon: and when he came to it, he found nothing but leaves; for the time of figs was not yet.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτῇ· Μηκέτι ⸂εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα ἐκ σοῦ⸃ μηδεὶς καρπὸν φάγοι. καὶ ἤκουον οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And answering, he said to her, "May no one ever again eat fruit from you." And his disciples heard it.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto it, No man eat fruit of thee hereafter for ever. And his disciples heard it.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχονται εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα. καὶ ⸀εἰσελθὼν εἰς τὸ ἱερὸν ἤρξατο ἐκβάλλειν τοὺς πωλοῦντας καὶ ⸀τοὺς ἀγοράζοντας ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ, καὶ τὰς τραπέζας τῶν κολλυβιστῶν καὶ τὰς καθέδρας τῶν πωλούντων τὰς περιστερὰς κατέστρεψεν
verses.mistral: And coming into Jerusalem, he began to drive out those who were selling and buying in the temple, and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who were selling pigeons.
