verses.kjv: ¶ And they come to Jerusalem: and Jesus went into the temple, and began to cast out them that sold and bought in the temple, and overthrew the tables of the moneychangers, and the seats of them that sold doves;

verses.greek: καὶ οὐκ ἤφιεν ἵνα τις διενέγκῃ σκεῦος διὰ τοῦ ἱεροῦ,
verses.mistral: No one permitted to carry anything through the temple.
verses.kjv: And would not suffer that any man should carry any vessel through the temple.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐδίδασκεν ⸂καὶ ἔλεγεν⸃ ⸀αὐτοῖς· Οὐ γέγραπται ὅτι Ὁ οἶκός μου οἶκος προσευχῆς κληθήσεται πᾶσιν τοῖς ἔθνεσιν; ὑμεῖς δὲ ⸀πεποιήκατε αὐτὸν σπήλαιον λῃστῶν.
verses.mistral: And He taught them, and said to them: "Is it not written, 'My house shall be called a house of prayer for all the nations'? But you have made it a den of robbers."
verses.kjv: And he taught, saying unto them, Is it not written, My house shall be called of all nations the house of prayer? but ye have made it a den of thieves.

verses.greek: καὶ ἤκουσαν οἱ ⸂ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς⸃, καὶ ἐζήτουν πῶς αὐτὸν ἀπολέσωσιν· ἐφοβοῦντο γὰρ αὐτόν, ⸂πᾶς γὰρ⸃ ὁ ὄχλος ἐξεπλήσσετο ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And the chief priests and the scribes heard it, and sought how to destroy him; for all the multitude were astonished at his teaching.
verses.kjv: And the scribes and chief priests heard it, and sought how they might destroy him: for they feared him, because all the people was astonished at his doctrine.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀ὅταν ὀψὲ ἐγένετο, ⸀ἐξεπορεύοντο ἔξω τῆς πόλεως.
verses.mistral: And when evening came, they went out of the city.
verses.kjv: And when even was come, he went out of the city.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸂παραπορευόμενοι πρωῒ⸃ εἶδον τὴν συκῆν ἐξηραμμένην ἐκ ῥιζῶν.
verses.mistral: Saw in the morning a withered fig tree from the roots.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναμνησθεὶς ὁ Πέτρος λέγει αὐτῷ· Ῥαββί, ἴδε ἡ συκῆ ἣν κατηράσω ἐξήρανται.
verses.mistral: Remembering, Peter said to Him, "Rabbi, look! The fig tree that I cursed has withered."
verses.kjv: And Peter calling to remembrance saith unto him, Master, behold, the fig tree which thou cursedst is withered away.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ἔχετε πίστιν θεοῦ·
verses.mistral: Have faith in God.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering saith unto them, Have faith in God.

verses.greek: ⸀ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ὃς ἂν εἴπῃ τῷ ὄρει τούτῳ· Ἄρθητι καὶ βλήθητι εἰς τὴν θάλασσαν, καὶ μὴ διακριθῇ ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοῦ ἀλλὰ ⸀πιστεύῃ ὅτι ⸂ὃ λαλεῖ γίνεται, ἔσται αὐτῷ⸃.
verses.mistral: He who believes and says to this mountain, 'Be taken up and thrown into the sea,' and does not doubt in his heart but believes that what he says will come to pass, it will be done for him.
verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, That whosoever shall say unto this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea; and shall not doubt in his heart, but shall believe that those things which he saith shall come to pass; he shall have whatsoever he saith.

verses.greek: διὰ τοῦτο λέγω ὑμῖν, πάντα ὅσα ⸂προσεύχεσθε καὶ αἰτεῖσθε⸃, πιστεύετε ὅτι ⸀ἐλάβετε, καὶ ἔσται ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: All things that you ask and seek, you believe that you have received, and it will be yours.
verses.kjv: Therefore I say unto you, What things soever ye desire, when ye pray, believe that ye receive them, and ye shall have them.

verses.greek: καὶ ὅταν ⸀στήκετε προσευχόμενοι, ἀφίετε εἴ τι ἔχετε κατά τινος, ἵνα καὶ ὁ πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς ἀφῇ ὑμῖν τὰ παραπτώματα ⸀ὑμῶν.
verses.mistral: And when you stand praying, forgive if you have anything against anyone, so that your Father in heaven may also forgive you your trespasses.
verses.kjv: And when ye stand praying, forgive, if ye have ought against any: that your Father also which is in heaven may forgive you your trespasses.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχονται πάλιν εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα. καὶ ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ περιπατοῦντος αὐτοῦ ἔρχονται πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι
verses.mistral: And again they come to Jerusalem. And in the temple, as he was walking, come to him the high priests and the scribes and the elders.
verses.kjv: And they come again to Jerusalem: and as he was walking in the temple, there come to him the chief priests, and the scribes, and the elders,

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἔλεγον αὐτῷ· Ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιεῖς; ⸀ἢ τίς σοι ⸂ἔδωκεν τὴν ἐξουσίαν ταύτην⸃ ἵνα ταῦτα ποιῇς;
verses.mistral: And they said to Him, "By what authority are you doing these things? Or who gave you this authority to do so?"
verses.kjv: And say unto him, By what authority doest thou these things? and who gave thee this authority to do these things?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ⸀Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ἐπερωτήσω ⸀ὑμᾶς ἕνα λόγον, καὶ ἀποκρίθητέ μοι, καὶ ἐρῶ ὑμῖν ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιῶ·
verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "I will ask you one question, and if you tell me the answer, I will tell you by what authority I do these things: The baptism of John—was it from heaven, or from men?"
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, I will also ask of you one question, and answer me, and I will tell you by what authority I do these things.

verses.greek: τὸ βάπτισμα ⸀τὸ Ἰωάννου ἐξ οὐρανοῦ ἦν ἢ ἐξ ἀνθρώπων; ἀποκρίθητέ μοι.
verses.mistral: Was the baptism of John from heaven or from men? Answer me.
verses.kjv: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men? answer me.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀διελογίζοντο πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς λέγοντες· ⸂Τί εἴπωμεν;⸃ ἐὰν εἴπωμεν· Ἐξ οὐρανοῦ, ἐρεῖ· Διὰ τί ⸀οὖν οὐκ ἐπιστεύσατε αὐτῷ;
verses.mistral: They debated among themselves, saying, "What shall we say? If we say, 'From heaven,' he will say, 'Why then did you not believe him?'"
verses.kjv: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then did ye not believe him?

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ εἴπωμεν· Ἐξ ἀνθρώπων;— ἐφοβοῦντο τὸν ⸀ὄχλον, ἅπαντες γὰρ εἶχον τὸν Ἰωάννην ⸂ὄντως ὅτι⸃ προφήτης ἦν.
verses.mistral: Fearful of the crowd, for everyone held that John indeed was a prophet.
verses.kjv: But if we shall say, Of men; they feared the people: for all men counted John, that he was a prophet indeed.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθέντες ⸂τῷ Ἰησοῦ λέγουσιν⸃· Οὐκ οἴδαμεν. καὶ ὁ ⸀Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτοῖς· Οὐδὲ ἐγὼ λέγω ὑμῖν ἐν ποίᾳ ἐξουσίᾳ ταῦτα ποιῶ.
verses.mistral: And answering them, they said to Jesus, "We do not know." And Jesus says to them, "Nor do I tell you in what authority I do these things."
verses.kjv: And they answered and said unto Jesus, We cannot tell. And Jesus answering saith unto them, Neither do I tell you by what authority I do these things.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἤρξατο αὐτοῖς ἐν παραβολαῖς ⸀λαλεῖν· Ἀμπελῶνα ⸂ἄνθρωπος ἐφύτευσεν⸃, καὶ περιέθηκεν φραγμὸν καὶ ὤρυξεν ὑπολήνιον καὶ ᾠκοδόμησεν πύργον, καὶ ἐξέδετο αὐτὸν γεωργοῖς, καὶ ἀπεδήμησεν.
verses.mistral: And began to speak to them in parables: "A man planted a vineyard, and put a fence around it, dug a pit for the press, and built a tower, and rented it out to vine-growers, and went away."
verses.kjv: And he began to speak unto them by parables. A certain man planted a vineyard, and set an hedge about it, and digged a place for the winefat, and built a tower, and let it out to husbandmen, and went into a far country.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀπέστειλεν πρὸς τοὺς γεωργοὺς τῷ καιρῷ δοῦλον, ἵνα παρὰ τῶν γεωργῶν λάβῃ ἀπὸ ⸂τῶν καρπῶν⸃ τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος·
verses.mistral: Sent a servant to the vine-growers at harvest time, that he might receive from the vineyard of the owner some of the fruits.
verses.kjv: And at the season he sent to the husbandmen a servant, that he might receive from the husbandmen of the fruit of the vineyard.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ λαβόντες αὐτὸν ἔδειραν καὶ ἀπέστειλαν κενόν.
verses.mistral: Seized him and sent him away empty.
verses.kjv: And they caught him, and beat him, and sent him away empty.

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν ἀπέστειλεν πρὸς αὐτοὺς ἄλλον δοῦλον· ⸀κἀκεῖνον ⸀ἐκεφαλίωσαν καὶ ⸀ἠτίμασαν.
verses.mistral: And again he sent unto them another servant; and that one they also killed, and dishonored.
verses.kjv: And again he sent unto them another servant; and at him they cast stones, and wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully handled.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ἄλλον ἀπέστειλεν· κἀκεῖνον ἀπέκτειναν, καὶ πολλοὺς ἄλλους, ⸂οὓς μὲν δέροντες οὓς δὲ ἀποκτέννοντες⸃.
verses.mistral: They also killed many others.
verses.kjv: And again he sent another; and him they killed, and many others; beating some, and killing some.

verses.greek: ἔτι ⸂ἕνα εἶχεν, υἱὸν ἀγαπητόν· ἀπέστειλεν⸃ αὐτὸν ⸂ἔσχατον πρὸς αὐτοὺς⸃ λέγων ὅτι Ἐντραπήσονται τὸν υἱόν μου.
verses.mistral: Still had one loved son; sent him last unto them, saying, They shall reverence my Son.
verses.kjv: Having yet therefore one son, his wellbeloved, he sent him also last unto them, saying, They will reverence my son.

verses.greek: ἐκεῖνοι δὲ οἱ γεωργοὶ ⸂πρὸς ἑαυτοὺς εἶπαν⸃ ὅτι Οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ κληρονόμος· δεῦτε ἀποκτείνωμεν αὐτόν, καὶ ἡμῶν ἔσται ἡ κληρονομία.
verses.mistral: Those farmers said, "This is the heir; let us kill him, and then his inheritance will be ours."
verses.kjv: But those husbandmen said among themselves, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be our's.

verses.greek: καὶ λαβόντες ⸂ἀπέκτειναν αὐτόν, καὶ ἐξέβαλον αὐτὸν⸃ ἔξω τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος.
verses.mistral: And taking him away, they killed him and threw him outside the vineyard.
verses.kjv: And they took him, and killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.

verses.greek: ⸀τί ποιήσει ὁ κύριος τοῦ ἀμπελῶνος; ἐλεύσεται καὶ ἀπολέσει τοὺς γεωργούς, καὶ δώσει τὸν ἀμπελῶνα ἄλλοις.
verses.mistral: What will the Lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the vineyard-keepers, and give the vineyard to others.
verses.kjv: What shall therefore the lord of the vineyard do? he will come and destroy the husbandmen, and will give the vineyard unto others.

verses.greek: οὐδὲ τὴν γραφὴν ταύτην ἀνέγνωτε· Λίθον ὃν ἀπεδοκίμασαν οἱ οἰκοδομοῦντες, οὗτος ἐγενήθη εἰς κεφαλὴν γωνίας·
verses.mistral: This stone which builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
verses.kjv: And have ye not read this scripture; The stone which the builders rejected is become the head of the corner:

verses.greek: παρὰ κυρίου ἐγένετο αὕτη, καὶ ἔστιν θαυμαστὴ ἐν ὀφθαλμοῖς ἡμῶν;
verses.mistral: From the Lord this has become, and is a wonder in our eyes.
verses.kjv: This was the Lord's doing, and it is marvellous in our eyes?

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐζήτουν αὐτὸν κρατῆσαι, καὶ ἐφοβήθησαν τὸν ὄχλον, ἔγνωσαν γὰρ ὅτι πρὸς αὐτοὺς τὴν παραβολὴν εἶπεν. καὶ ἀφέντες αὐτὸν ἀπῆλθον.
verses.mistral: They sought to seize Him, and they feared the crowd, for they knew that He had spoken the parable to them. And leaving Him, they went away.
verses.kjv: And they sought to lay hold on him, but feared the people: for they knew that he had spoken the parable against them: and they left him, and went their way.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀποστέλλουσιν πρὸς αὐτόν τινας τῶν Φαρισαίων καὶ τῶν Ἡρῳδιανῶν ἵνα αὐτὸν ἀγρεύσωσιν λόγῳ.
verses.mistral: Send some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Him, that they might trap Him with His words.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they send unto him certain of the Pharisees and of the Herodians, to catch him in his words.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ ἐλθόντες λέγουσιν αὐτῷ· Διδάσκαλε, οἴδαμεν ὅτι ἀληθὴς εἶ καὶ οὐ μέλει σοι περὶ οὐδενός, οὐ γὰρ βλέπεις εἰς πρόσωπον ἀνθρώπων, ἀλλ’ ἐπ’ ἀληθείας τὴν ὁδὸν τοῦ θεοῦ διδάσκεις· ἔξεστιν ⸂δοῦναι κῆνσον Καίσαρι⸃ ἢ οὔ; δῶμεν ἢ μὴ δῶμεν;
verses.mistral: Do you pay taxes to Caesar, or not?
verses.kjv: And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to give tribute to Caesar, or not?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ εἰδὼς αὐτῶν τὴν ὑπόκρισιν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τί με πειράζετε; φέρετέ μοι δηνάριον ἵνα ἴδω.
verses.mistral: He, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them: "Why are you testing me? Bring me a denarius and let me see it."
verses.kjv: Shall we give, or shall we not give? But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said unto them, Why tempt ye me? bring me a penny, that I may see it.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἤνεγκαν. καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Τίνος ἡ εἰκὼν αὕτη καὶ ἡ ἐπιγραφή; οἱ δὲ εἶπαν αὐτῷ· Καίσαρος.
verses.mistral: They brought (it). And he says to them, "Whose image is this, and the inscription?" They said to him, "Caesar's."
verses.kjv: And they brought it. And he saith unto them, Whose is this image and superscription? And they said unto him, Caesar's.

verses.greek: ⸂ὁ δὲ⸃ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν ⸀αὐτοῖς· ⸂Τὰ Καίσαρος ἀπόδοτε⸃ Καίσαρι καὶ τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ τῷ θεῷ. καὶ ⸀ἐξεθαύμαζον ἐπ’ αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "Render unto Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's." And they marveled at him.
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's. And they marvelled at him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχονται Σαδδουκαῖοι πρὸς αὐτόν, οἵτινες λέγουσιν ἀνάστασιν μὴ εἶναι, καὶ ⸀ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν λέγοντες·
verses.mistral: And the Sadducees came to Him, who say there is no resurrection, and asked Him, saying...
verses.kjv: ¶ Then come unto him the Sadducees, which say there is no resurrection; and they asked him, saying,

verses.greek: Διδάσκαλε, Μωϋσῆς ἔγραψεν ἡμῖν ὅτι ἐάν τινος ἀδελφὸς ἀποθάνῃ καὶ καταλίπῃ γυναῖκα καὶ ⸂μὴ ἀφῇ τέκνον⸃, ἵνα λάβῃ ὁ ἀδελφὸς αὐτοῦ τὴν ⸀γυναῖκα καὶ ἐξαναστήσῃ σπέρμα τῷ ἀδελφῷ αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man's brother dies and leaves his wife behind, without children, then the brother should take his brother's widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
verses.kjv: Master, Moses wrote unto us, If a man's brother die, and leave his wife behind him, and leave no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.

verses.greek: ἑπτὰ ἀδελφοὶ ἦσαν· καὶ ὁ πρῶτος ἔλαβεν γυναῖκα, καὶ ἀποθνῄσκων οὐκ ἀφῆκεν σπέρμα·
verses.mistral: Seven brothers had no children. The first took a wife, and when he was dying, he left no offspring.
verses.kjv: Now there were seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and dying left no seed.

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ δεύτερος ἔλαβεν αὐτήν, καὶ ἀπέθανεν ⸂μὴ καταλιπὼν⸃ σπέρμα, καὶ ὁ τρίτος ὡσαύτως·
verses.mistral: The second also took her, and he died leaving no offspring, and the third likewise.
verses.kjv: And the second took her, and died, neither left he any seed: and the third likewise.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂οἱ ἑπτὰ⸃ οὐκ ἀφῆκαν σπέρμα· ⸀ἔσχατον πάντων ⸂καὶ ἡ γυνὴ ἀπέθανεν⸃.
verses.mistral: Last of all, the woman also died and left no offspring.
verses.kjv: And the seven had her, and left no seed: last of all the woman died also.

verses.greek: ἐν τῇ ἀναστάσει ⸂ὅταν ἀναστῶσιν⸃ τίνος αὐτῶν ἔσται γυνή; οἱ γὰρ ἑπτὰ ἔσχον αὐτὴν γυναῖκα.
verses.mistral: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? For they had no wife besides her.
verses.kjv: In the resurrection therefore, when they shall rise, whose wife shall she be of them? for the seven had her to wife.

verses.greek: ⸂ἔφη αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς⸃· Οὐ διὰ τοῦτο πλανᾶσθε μὴ εἰδότες τὰς γραφὰς μηδὲ τὴν δύναμιν τοῦ θεοῦ;
verses.mistral: Jesus said to them, "Have you not realized the Scriptures nor the power of God?"
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, Do ye not therefore err, because ye know not the scriptures, neither the power of God?

verses.greek: ὅταν γὰρ ἐκ νεκρῶν ἀναστῶσιν, οὔτε γαμοῦσιν οὔτε ⸀γαμίζονται, ἀλλ’ εἰσὶν ὡς ⸀ἄγγελοι ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς·
verses.mistral: When from the dead they rise, neither do they marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
verses.kjv: For when they shall rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage; but are as the angels which are in heaven.

verses.greek: περὶ δὲ τῶν νεκρῶν ὅτι ἐγείρονται οὐκ ἀνέγνωτε ἐν τῇ βίβλῳ Μωϋσέως ἐπὶ τοῦ βάτου ⸀πῶς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ θεὸς λέγων· Ἐγὼ ὁ θεὸς Ἀβραὰμ καὶ ⸂ὁ θεὸς Ἰσαὰκ καὶ ὁ⸃ θεὸς Ἰακώβ;
verses.mistral: About the dead, that they rise, have you not read in the book of Moses concerning the bush, how God said to him, "I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?"
verses.kjv: And as touching the dead, that they rise: have ye not read in the book of Moses, how in the bush God spake unto him, saying, I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob?

verses.greek: οὐκ ἔστιν ⸀θεὸς νεκρῶν ⸀ἀλλὰ ζώντων· ⸀πολὺ πλανᾶσθε.
verses.mistral: God of the dead, he is not; but of the living, thou art. You are much mistaken.
verses.kjv: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living: ye therefore do greatly err.

verses.greek: Καὶ προσελθὼν εἷς τῶν γραμματέων ἀκούσας αὐτῶν συζητούντων, ⸀ἰδὼν ὅτι καλῶς ⸂ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς⸃, ἐπηρώτησεν αὐτόν· Ποία ἐστὶν ⸂ἐντολὴ πρώτη πάντων⸃;
verses.mistral: What is the first commandment of all?
verses.kjv: ¶ And one of the scribes came, and having heard them reasoning together, and perceiving that he had answered them well, asked him, Which is the first commandment of all?

verses.greek: ⸂ἀπεκρίθη ὁ Ἰησοῦς⸃ ὅτι Πρώτη ⸀ἐστίν· Ἄκουε, Ἰσραήλ, κύριος ὁ θεὸς ἡμῶν κύριος εἷς ἐστιν,
verses.mistral: The first commandment is: "Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one."
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered him, The first of all the commandments is, Hear, O Israel; The Lord our God is one Lord:

verses.greek: καὶ ἀγαπήσεις κύριον τὸν θεόν σου ἐξ ὅλης ⸀τῆς καρδίας σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ψυχῆς σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς διανοίας σου καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ἰσχύος ⸀σου.
verses.mistral: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.
verses.kjv: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind, and with all thy strength: this is the first commandment.

verses.greek: ⸀δευτέρα αὕτη· Ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν. μείζων τούτων ἄλλη ἐντολὴ οὐκ ἔστιν.
verses.mistral: Love your neighbor as yourself. No other commandment is greater than these.
verses.kjv: And the second is like, namely this, Thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself. There is none other commandment greater than these.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ γραμματεύς· Καλῶς, διδάσκαλε, ἐπ’ ἀληθείας εἶπες ὅτι εἷς ἐστιν καὶ οὐκ ἔστιν ἄλλος πλὴν αὐτοῦ·
verses.mistral: These are the words spoken: "Truly, teacher, you have spoken well; there is no one greater than he."
verses.kjv: And the scribe said unto him, Well, Master, thou hast said the truth: for there is one God; and there is none other but he:

verses.greek: καὶ τὸ ἀγαπᾶν αὐτὸν ἐξ ὅλης ⸀τῆς καρδίας καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ⸀συνέσεως καὶ ἐξ ὅλης τῆς ἰσχύος καὶ τὸ ἀγαπᾶν τὸν πλησίον ὡς ἑαυτὸν ⸀περισσότερόν ἐστιν πάντων τῶν ὁλοκαυτωμάτων καὶ θυσιῶν.
verses.mistral: Loving him from the whole heart, and from the whole soul, and from the whole strength, and loving one's neighbor as oneself is greater than all the sacrifices and offerings.
verses.kjv: And to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbour as himself, is more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἰδὼν ⸀αὐτὸν ὅτι νουνεχῶς ἀπεκρίθη εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Οὐ μακρὰν εἶ ἀπὸ τῆς βασιλείας τοῦ θεοῦ. καὶ οὐδεὶς οὐκέτι ἐτόλμα αὐτὸν ἐπερωτῆσαι.
verses.mistral: Jesus, seeing that he had answered with steadfastness, said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And no one dared to question him anymore.
verses.kjv: And when Jesus saw that he answered discreetly, he said unto him, Thou art not far from the kingdom of God. And no man after that durst ask him any question.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἔλεγεν διδάσκων ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ· Πῶς λέγουσιν οἱ γραμματεῖς ὅτι ὁ χριστὸς υἱὸς ⸂Δαυίδ ἐστιν⸃;
verses.mistral: And Jesus, answering, said teaching in the temple: How do the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
verses.kjv: ¶ And Jesus answered and said, while he taught in the temple, How say the scribes that Christ is the Son of David?

verses.greek: ⸀αὐτὸς Δαυὶδ εἶπεν ἐν ⸂τῷ πνεύματι τῷ⸃ ἁγίῳ· ⸀Εἶπεν ⸀κύριος τῷ κυρίῳ μου· ⸀Κάθου ἐκ δεξιῶν μου ἕως ἂν θῶ τοὺς ἐχθρούς σου ⸀ὑποκάτω τῶν ποδῶν σου.
verses.mistral: The Lord said to my Lord: "Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet."
verses.kjv: For David himself said by the Holy Ghost, The LORD said to my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand, till I make thine enemies thy footstool.

verses.greek: ⸀αὐτὸς Δαυὶδ λέγει αὐτὸν κύριον, καὶ πόθεν ⸂αὐτοῦ ἐστιν υἱός⸃; καὶ ὁ πολὺς ὄχλος ἤκουεν αὐτοῦ ἡδέως.
verses.mistral: David himself says to him, "Lord, from where is your Son?" And the great crowd listened to him with delight.
verses.kjv: David therefore himself calleth him Lord; and whence is he then his son? And the common people heard him gladly.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸂ἐν τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ ἔλεγεν⸃· Βλέπετε ἀπὸ τῶν γραμματέων τῶν θελόντων ἐν στολαῖς περιπατεῖν καὶ ἀσπασμοὺς ἐν ταῖς ἀγοραῖς
verses.mistral: In his teaching, he said, "Look at the scribes who like to walk around in long robes and receive greetings in the marketplaces."
verses.kjv: ¶ And he said unto them in his doctrine, Beware of the scribes, which love to go in long clothing, and love salutations in the marketplaces,

verses.greek: καὶ πρωτοκαθεδρίας ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς καὶ πρωτοκλισίας ἐν τοῖς δείπνοις,
verses.mistral: Front seats in the synagogues and front places at feasts
verses.kjv: And the chief seats in the synagogues, and the uppermost rooms at feasts:

verses.greek: οἱ κατεσθίοντες τὰς οἰκίας τῶν χηρῶν καὶ προφάσει μακρὰ προσευχόμενοι· οὗτοι λήμψονται περισσότερον κρίμα.
verses.mistral: Those who exploit the homes of widows and offer prolonged prayers for show will receive greater condemnation.
verses.kjv: Which devour widows' houses, and for a pretence make long prayers: these shall receive greater damnation.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀καθίσας ⸀κατέναντι τοῦ γαζοφυλακίου ἐθεώρει πῶς ὁ ὄχλος βάλλει χαλκὸν εἰς τὸ γαζοφυλάκιον· καὶ πολλοὶ πλούσιοι ἔβαλλον πολλά·
verses.mistral: Watching from beside the treasury, he saw how the crowd was putting money into the treasury. Many rich people were throwing in large amounts.
verses.kjv: And Jesus sat over against the treasury, and beheld how the people cast money into the treasury: and many that were rich cast in much.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλθοῦσα μία χήρα πτωχὴ ἔβαλεν λεπτὰ δύο, ὅ ἐστιν κοδράντης.
verses.mistral: Widow, a poor one, cast in two mites.
verses.kjv: And there came a certain poor widow, and she threw in two mites, which make a farthing.

verses.greek: καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τοὺς μαθητὰς αὐτοῦ ⸀εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι ἡ χήρα αὕτη ἡ πτωχὴ πλεῖον πάντων ⸀ἔβαλεν τῶν βαλλόντων εἰς τὸ γαζοφυλάκιον·
verses.mistral: This poor widow has put in more than all those contributing to the treasury.
verses.kjv: And he called unto him his disciples, and saith unto them, Verily I say unto you, That this poor widow hath cast more in, than all they which have cast into the treasury:

verses.greek: πάντες γὰρ ἐκ τοῦ περισσεύοντος αὐτοῖς ἔβαλον, αὕτη δὲ ἐκ τῆς ὑστερήσεως αὐτῆς πάντα ὅσα εἶχεν ἔβαλεν, ὅλον τὸν βίον αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: All those are contributing from their abundance; she, from her poverty, put in all that she had, her whole life.
verses.kjv: For all they did cast in of their abundance; but she of her want did cast in all that she had, even all her living.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐκπορευομένου αὐτοῦ ἐκ τοῦ ἱεροῦ λέγει αὐτῷ εἷς τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ· Διδάσκαλε, ἴδε ποταποὶ λίθοι καὶ ποταπαὶ οἰκοδομαί.
verses.mistral: Teacher, see what great stones and what buildings!
verses.kjv: And as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildings are here!

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ ⸀Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτῷ· Βλέπεις ταύτας τὰς μεγάλας οἰκοδομάς; οὐ μὴ ἀφεθῇ ⸀ὧδε λίθος ἐπὶ ⸀λίθον ὃς οὐ μὴ καταλυθῇ.
verses.mistral: And Jesus said to him, "Do you see these great buildings? Not one stone will be left here upon another, that will not be thrown down."
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

verses.greek: Καὶ καθημένου αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ Ὄρος τῶν Ἐλαιῶν κατέναντι τοῦ ἱεροῦ ⸀ἐπηρώτα αὐτὸν κατ’ ἰδίαν Πέτρος καὶ Ἰάκωβος καὶ Ἰωάννης καὶ Ἀνδρέας·
verses.mistral: And Peter, James, John, and Andrew asked Him privately.
verses.kjv: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,

verses.greek: Εἰπὸν ἡμῖν πότε ταῦτα ἔσται, καὶ τί τὸ σημεῖον ὅταν μέλλῃ ⸂ταῦτα συντελεῖσθαι πάντα⸃.
verses.mistral: Said to us, "What is this that is happening, and what will be the sign when all these things are about to be fulfilled?"
verses.kjv: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what shall be the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ⸂ἤρξατο λέγειν αὐτοῖς⸃· Βλέπετε μή τις ὑμᾶς πλανήσῃ·
verses.mistral: Jesus began to say to them: "Take heed lest anyone deceive you"
verses.kjv: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any man deceive you:

verses.greek: ⸀πολλοὶ ἐλεύσονται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου λέγοντες ὅτι Ἐγώ εἰμι, καὶ πολλοὺς πλανήσουσιν.
verses.mistral: Many will come in my name saying, 'I am,' and deceive many.
verses.kjv: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am Christ; and shall deceive many.

verses.greek: ὅταν δὲ ⸀ἀκούσητε πολέμους καὶ ἀκοὰς πολέμων, μὴ θροεῖσθε· ⸀δεῖ γενέσθαι, ἀλλ’ οὔπω τὸ τέλος.
verses.mistral: When you hear of wars and rumors of wars, do not be alarmed. Such things must happen, but the end is still to come.
verses.kjv: And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for such things must needs be; but the end shall not be yet.

verses.greek: ἐγερθήσεται γὰρ ἔθνος ἐπ’ ἔθνος καὶ βασιλεία ἐπὶ βασιλείαν, ⸀ἔσονται σεισμοὶ κατὰ τόπους, ⸁ἔσονται ⸀λιμοί· ⸀ἀρχὴ ὠδίνων ταῦτα.
verses.mistral: Nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom; there will be earthquakes in various places, there will be famines. These are the beginnings of sorrows.
verses.kjv: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these are the beginnings of sorrows.

verses.greek: βλέπετε δὲ ὑμεῖς ἑαυτούς· ⸀παραδώσουσιν ὑμᾶς εἰς συνέδρια καὶ εἰς συναγωγὰς δαρήσεσθε καὶ ἐπὶ ἡγεμόνων καὶ βασιλέων σταθήσεσθε ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: Yourselves will be delivered up to councils and flogged in synagogues; you will stand before governors and kings because of me, as a witness to them.
verses.kjv: ¶ But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰς πάντα τὰ ἔθνη ⸂πρῶτον δεῖ⸃ κηρυχθῆναι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον.
verses.mistral: To all the nations, first must be preached the Gospel.
verses.kjv: And the gospel must first be published among all nations.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ ὅταν⸃ ⸀ἄγωσιν ὑμᾶς παραδιδόντες, μὴ προμεριμνᾶτε τί ⸀λαλήσητε, ἀλλ’ ὃ ἐὰν δοθῇ ὑμῖν ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ τοῦτο λαλεῖτε, οὐ γάρ ἐστε ὑμεῖς οἱ λαλοῦντες ἀλλὰ τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον.
verses.mistral: And when they deliver you up, do not be anxious about what to speak, but in that hour whatever is given to you, speak that; for you are not the ones speaking, but the Holy Spirit.
verses.kjv: But when they shall lead you, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.

verses.greek: ⸂καὶ παραδώσει⸃ ἀδελφὸς ἀδελφὸν εἰς θάνατον καὶ πατὴρ τέκνον, καὶ ἐπαναστήσονται τέκνα ἐπὶ γονεῖς καὶ θανατώσουσιν αὐτούς·
verses.mistral: Will also deliver brother against brother, and father against child; and children will rise up against parents and kill them.
verses.kjv: Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against their parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔσεσθε μισούμενοι ὑπὸ πάντων διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου. ὁ δὲ ὑπομείνας εἰς τέλος οὗτος σωθήσεται.
verses.mistral: And you will be hated by all because of my name. But he who endures to the end will be saved.
verses.kjv: And ye shall be hated of all men for my name's sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

verses.greek: Ὅταν δὲ ἴδητε τὸ βδέλυγμα τῆς ἐρημώσεως ⸀ἑστηκότα ὅπου οὐ δεῖ, ὁ ἀναγινώσκων νοείτω, τότε οἱ ἐν τῇ Ἰουδαίᾳ φευγέτωσαν εἰς τὰ ὄρη,
verses.mistral: When you see the abomination standing where it must not, let the reader understand; then those in Judea flee to the mountains
verses.kjv: ¶ But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judaea flee to the mountains:

verses.greek: ⸀ὁ ἐπὶ τοῦ δώματος μὴ ⸀καταβάτω μηδὲ εἰσελθάτω ⸂τι ἆραι⸃ ἐκ τῆς οἰκίας αὐτοῦ,
verses.mistral: Let him who is on the housetop not go down to take anything out of his house.
verses.kjv: And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter therein, to take any thing out of his house:

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ εἰς τὸν ⸀ἀγρὸν μὴ ἐπιστρεψάτω εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω ἆραι τὸ ἱμάτιον αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Return the cloak left behind.
verses.kjv: And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.

verses.greek: οὐαὶ δὲ ταῖς ἐν γαστρὶ ἐχούσαις καὶ ταῖς θηλαζούσαις ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις.
verses.mistral: Woes to those with child and nursing in those days.
verses.kjv: But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!

verses.greek: προσεύχεσθε δὲ ἵνα μὴ ⸀γένηται χειμῶνος·
verses.mistral: Pray that it does not rain
verses.kjv: And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.

verses.greek: ἔσονται γὰρ αἱ ἡμέραι ἐκεῖναι θλῖψις οἵα οὐ γέγονεν τοιαύτη ἀπ’ ἀρχῆς κτίσεως ⸀ἣν ἔκτισεν ὁ θεὸς ἕως τοῦ νῦν καὶ οὐ μὴ γένηται.
verses.mistral: Those days will be a tribulation such as never has been from the beginning of the creation that God created until now, and never will be.
verses.kjv: For in those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰ μὴ ⸂ἐκολόβωσεν κύριος⸃ τὰς ἡμέρας, οὐκ ἂν ἐσώθη πᾶσα σάρξ. ἀλλὰ διὰ τοὺς ἐκλεκτοὺς οὓς ἐξελέξατο ἐκολόβωσεν τὰς ἡμέρας.
verses.mistral: And unless the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh would have been saved. But for the elect whom he chose, he shortened the days.
verses.kjv: And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect's sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.

verses.greek: ⸀καὶ τότε ἐάν τις ὑμῖν εἴπῃ· ⸀Ἴδε ὧδε ὁ ⸀χριστός, ⸁Ἴδε ἐκεῖ, μὴ πιστεύετε·
verses.mistral: Here: And then if anyone says to you, "Look, here is the Christ," or, "Look, there," do not believe.
verses.kjv: And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ; or, lo, he is there; believe him not:

verses.greek: ἐγερθήσονται γὰρ ψευδόχριστοι καὶ ψευδοπροφῆται καὶ δώσουσιν σημεῖα καὶ τέρατα πρὸς τὸ ἀποπλανᾶν εἰ ⸀δυνατὸν τοὺς ἐκλεκτούς·
verses.mistral: False Christs and false prophets will arise, and they will perform signs and wonders to deceive, if possible, the chosen ones.
verses.kjv: For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if it were possible, even the elect.

verses.greek: ὑμεῖς δὲ βλέπετε· ⸀προείρηκα ὑμῖν πάντα.
verses.mistral: You have been warned. (All translations are approximate due to the nuances of language and context.)
verses.kjv: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.

verses.greek: Ἀλλὰ ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις μετὰ τὴν θλῖψιν ἐκείνην ὁ ἥλιος σκοτισθήσεται, καὶ ἡ σελήνη οὐ δώσει τὸ φέγγος αὐτῆς,
verses.mistral: The sun will be darkened, and the moon will not give its light.
verses.kjv: ¶ But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,

verses.greek: καὶ οἱ ἀστέρες ⸂ἔσονται ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ πίπτοντες⸃, καὶ αἱ δυνάμεις αἱ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς σαλευθήσονται.
verses.mistral: The stars will fall from heaven, and the powers in the heavens will be shaken.
verses.kjv: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.

verses.greek: καὶ τότε ὄψονται τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενον ἐν νεφέλαις μετὰ δυνάμεως πολλῆς καὶ δόξης·
verses.mistral: Then they will see the Son of Man coming in clouds with great power and glory.
verses.kjv: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.

verses.greek: καὶ τότε ἀποστελεῖ τοὺς ⸀ἀγγέλους καὶ ἐπισυνάξει τοὺς ⸀ἐκλεκτοὺς ἐκ τῶν τεσσάρων ἀνέμων ἀπ’ ἄκρου γῆς ἕως ἄκρου οὐρανοῦ.
verses.mistral: Then He will send His angels, and gather together His elect from the four winds, from the farthest end of the earth to the farthest end of heaven.
verses.kjv: And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.

verses.greek: Ἀπὸ δὲ τῆς συκῆς μάθετε τὴν παραβολήν· ὅταν ⸂ἤδη ὁ κλάδος αὐτῆς⸃ ἁπαλὸς γένηται καὶ ἐκφύῃ τὰ φύλλα, γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγὺς τὸ θέρος ἐστίν·
verses.mistral: From the fig tree learn its parable: When its branch has become tender and loses its leaves, you know that summer is near.
verses.kjv: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:

verses.greek: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς, ὅταν ⸂ἴδητε ταῦτα⸃ γινόμενα, γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγύς ἐστιν ἐπὶ θύραις.
verses.mistral: So also you, when you see these things happening, know that it is near at the doors.
verses.kjv: So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, even at the doors.

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ παρέλθῃ ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη μέχρις οὗ ⸂ταῦτα πάντα⸃ γένηται.
verses.mistral: Verily I say unto you, this generation will not pass away until all these things be fulfilled.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.

verses.greek: ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ ⸀παρελεύσονται, οἱ δὲ λόγοι μου οὐ ⸀μὴ ⸁παρελεύσονται.
verses.mistral: The heaven and earth will pass away, but my words will not pass away.
verses.kjv: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

verses.greek: Περὶ δὲ τῆς ἡμέρας ἐκείνης ἢ ⸀τῆς ὥρας οὐδεὶς οἶδεν, οὐδὲ οἱ ἄγγελοι ⸀ἐν οὐρανῷ οὐδὲ ὁ υἱός, εἰ μὴ ὁ πατήρ.
verses.mistral: No one knows about that day or hour, not even the angels in heaven, nor the Son, but only the Father.
verses.kjv: ¶ But of that day and that hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.

verses.greek: βλέπετε ⸀ἀγρυπνεῖτε, οὐκ οἴδατε γὰρ πότε ὁ καιρός ἐστιν·
verses.mistral: Watch, be alert; you do not know when the time is.
verses.kjv: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.

verses.greek: ὡς ἄνθρωπος ἀπόδημος ἀφεὶς τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ καὶ δοὺς τοῖς δούλοις αὐτοῦ τὴν ἐξουσίαν, ⸀ἑκάστῳ τὸ ἔργον αὐτοῦ, καὶ τῷ θυρωρῷ ἐνετείλατο ἵνα γρηγορῇ.
verses.mistral: Leaves his house, giving authority to his servants and to each his task, and to the gatekeeper he commanded to be watchful.
verses.kjv: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.

verses.greek: γρηγορεῖτε οὖν, οὐκ οἴδατε γὰρ πότε ὁ κύριος τῆς οἰκίας ἔρχεται, ⸀ἢ ὀψὲ ἢ ⸀μεσονύκτιον ἢ ἀλεκτοροφωνίας ἢ πρωΐ,
verses.mistral: Stay awake then, for you do not know when the master of the house is coming - whether in the evening, or at midnight, or when the rooster crows, or in the morning.
verses.kjv: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:

verses.greek: μὴ ἐλθὼν ἐξαίφνης εὕρῃ ὑμᾶς καθεύδοντας·
verses.mistral: Awake, you might not be found sleeping.
verses.kjv: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.

verses.greek: ⸀ὃ δὲ ὑμῖν λέγω πᾶσιν λέγω· γρηγορεῖτε.
verses.mistral: Watch ye (plural) therefore
verses.kjv: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.

verses.greek: Ἦν δὲ τὸ πάσχα καὶ τὰ ἄζυμα μετὰ δύο ἡμέρας. καὶ ἐζήτουν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς πῶς αὐτὸν ἐν δόλῳ κρατήσαντες ἀποκτείνωσιν,
verses.mistral: The Passover and the unleavened bread were two days away. And the chief priests and scribes sought how to take him by deceit and kill him.
verses.kjv: After two days was the feast of the passover, and of unleavened bread: and the chief priests and the scribes sought how they might take him by craft, and put him to death.

verses.greek: ἔλεγον ⸀γάρ· Μὴ ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ, μήποτε ⸂ἔσται θόρυβος⸃ τοῦ λαοῦ.
verses.mistral: Said, "Lest there be an uproar of the people in the feast."
verses.kjv: But they said, Not on the feast day, lest there be an uproar of the people.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὄντος αὐτοῦ ἐν Βηθανίᾳ ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ Σίμωνος τοῦ λεπροῦ κατακειμένου αὐτοῦ ἦλθεν γυνὴ ἔχουσα ἀλάβαστρον μύρου νάρδου πιστικῆς πολυτελοῦς· ⸀συντρίψασα ⸀τὴν ἀλάβαστρον κατέχεεν ⸀αὐτοῦ τῆς κεφαλῆς.
verses.mistral: Woman brought a vial of expensive perfume made from nard. She broke the vial and poured it on his head.
verses.kjv: ¶ And being in Bethany in the house of Simon the leper, as he sat at meat, there came a woman having an alabaster box of ointment of spikenard very precious; and she brake the box, and poured it on his head.

verses.greek: ἦσαν δέ τινες ἀγανακτοῦντες πρὸς ⸀ἑαυτούς· Εἰς τί ἡ ἀπώλεια αὕτη τοῦ μύρου γέγονεν;
verses.mistral: Were some indignant among themselves, saying, "For what purpose has this waste of the perfume occurred?"
verses.kjv: And there were some that had indignation within themselves, and said, Why was this waste of the ointment made?

verses.greek: ἠδύνατο γὰρ τοῦτο ⸂τὸ μύρον⸃ πραθῆναι ἐπάνω ⸂δηναρίων τριακοσίων⸃ καὶ δοθῆναι τοῖς πτωχοῖς· καὶ ἐνεβριμῶντο αὐτῇ.
verses.mistral: This perfume might have been sold for over three hundred denarii and given to the poor. And they criticized her.
verses.kjv: For it might have been sold for more than three hundred pence, and have been given to the poor. And they murmured against her.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν· Ἄφετε αὐτήν· τί αὐτῇ κόπους παρέχετε; καλὸν ἔργον ἠργάσατο ἐν ἐμοί·
verses.mistral: Jesus said, "Leave her alone; why are you bothering her? She has done a good work for me."
verses.kjv: And Jesus said, Let her alone; why trouble ye her? she hath wrought a good work on me.

verses.greek: πάντοτε γὰρ τοὺς πτωχοὺς ἔχετε μεθ’ ἑαυτῶν, καὶ ὅταν θέλητε δύνασθε ⸀αὐτοῖς εὖ ποιῆσαι, ἐμὲ δὲ οὐ πάντοτε ἔχετε·
verses.mistral: Have with you always, but you do not have me always.
verses.kjv: For ye have the poor with you always, and whensoever ye will ye may do them good: but me ye have not always.

verses.greek: ὃ ⸀ἔσχεν ἐποίησεν, προέλαβεν μυρίσαι ⸂τὸ σῶμά μου⸃ εἰς τὸν ἐνταφιασμόν.
verses.mistral: Presented aromatic spices for my burial.
verses.kjv: She hath done what she could: she is come aforehand to anoint my body to the burying.

verses.greek: ἀμὴν ⸀δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν, ὅπου ⸀ἐὰν κηρυχθῇ τὸ ⸀εὐαγγέλιον εἰς ὅλον τὸν κόσμον, καὶ ὃ ἐποίησεν αὕτη λαληθήσεται εἰς μνημόσυνον αὐτῆς.
verses.mistral: Verily I say unto you, where the gospel is preached throughout the whole world, what she has done will be told in remembrance of her.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, Wheresoever this gospel shall be preached throughout the whole world, this also that she hath done shall be spoken of for a memorial of her.

verses.greek: ⸀Καὶ Ἰούδας ⸂Ἰσκαριὼθ ὁ⸃ εἷς τῶν δώδεκα ἀπῆλθεν πρὸς τοὺς ἀρχιερεῖς ἵνα αὐτὸν ⸂παραδοῖ αὐτοῖς⸃.
verses.mistral: Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went to the chief priests and delivered him to them.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Judas Iscariot, one of the twelve, went unto the chief priests, to betray him unto them.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἀκούσαντες ἐχάρησαν καὶ ἐπηγγείλαντο αὐτῷ ἀργύριον δοῦναι. καὶ ἐζήτει πῶς ⸂αὐτὸν εὐκαίρως παραδοῖ⸃.
verses.mistral: They were pleased and promised to give him silver. And they sought by what means to betray him.
verses.kjv: And when they heard it, they were glad, and promised to give him money. And he sought how he might conveniently betray him.

verses.greek: Καὶ τῇ πρώτῃ ἡμέρᾳ τῶν ἀζύμων, ὅτε τὸ πάσχα ἔθυον, λέγουσιν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ· Ποῦ θέλεις ἀπελθόντες ἑτοιμάσωμεν ἵνα φάγῃς τὸ πάσχα;
verses.mistral: And in the first day of unleavened bread, when they sacrificed the Passover, his disciples say to him: Where do you desire to go that we may prepare, that you may eat the Passover?
verses.kjv: ¶ And the first day of unleavened bread, when they killed the passover, his disciples said unto him, Where wilt thou that we go and prepare that thou mayest eat the passover?

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποστέλλει δύο τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Ὑπάγετε εἰς τὴν πόλιν, καὶ ἀπαντήσει ὑμῖν ἄνθρωπος κεράμιον ὕδατος βαστάζων· ἀκολουθήσατε αὐτῷ,
verses.mistral: Go into the city, and a man carrying a jar of water will meet you. Follow him.
verses.kjv: And he sendeth forth two of his disciples, and saith unto them, Go ye into the city, and there shall meet you a man bearing a pitcher of water: follow him.

verses.greek: καὶ ὅπου ⸀ἐὰν εἰσέλθῃ εἴπατε τῷ οἰκοδεσπότῃ ὅτι Ὁ διδάσκαλος λέγει· Ποῦ ἐστιν τὸ κατάλυμά ⸀μου ὅπου τὸ πάσχα μετὰ τῶν μαθητῶν μου φάγω;
verses.mistral: The teacher says, "Where is my resting place where I will eat the Passover with my disciples?"
verses.kjv: And wheresoever he shall go in, say ye to the goodman of the house, The Master saith, Where is the guestchamber, where I shall eat the passover with my disciples?

verses.greek: καὶ αὐτὸς ὑμῖν δείξει ἀνάγαιον μέγα ἐστρωμένον ἕτοιμον· ⸀καὶ ἐκεῖ ἑτοιμάσατε ἡμῖν.
verses.mistral: He himself will show you a large upper room, prepared and ready; there make preparations for us.
verses.kjv: And he will shew you a large upper room furnished and prepared: there make ready for us.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐξῆλθον οἱ ⸀μαθηταὶ καὶ ἦλθον εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ εὗρον καθὼς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς, καὶ ἡτοίμασαν τὸ πάσχα.
verses.mistral: And the disciples went out and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them; and they prepared the Passover.
verses.kjv: And his disciples went forth, and came into the city, and found as he had said unto them: and they made ready the passover.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὀψίας γενομένης ἔρχεται μετὰ τῶν δώδεκα.
verses.mistral: Dinner having come, he comes with the twelve.
verses.kjv: And in the evening he cometh with the twelve.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀνακειμένων αὐτῶν καὶ ἐσθιόντων ⸂ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν⸃· Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι εἷς ἐξ ὑμῶν παραδώσει με ὁ ἐσθίων μετ’ ἐμοῦ.
verses.mistral: One of you will betray me while eating with me.
verses.kjv: And as they sat and did eat, Jesus said, Verily I say unto you, One of you which eateth with me shall betray me.

verses.greek: ⸀ἤρξαντο λυπεῖσθαι καὶ λέγειν αὐτῷ εἷς κατὰ εἷς· Μήτι ⸀ἐγώ;
verses.mistral: Begin: They began to grieve and say to Him, "Is it I?"
verses.kjv: And they began to be sorrowful, and to say unto him one by one, Is it I? and another said, Is it I?

verses.greek: ὁ ⸀δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· ⸀Εἷς τῶν δώδεκα, ὁ ἐμβαπτόμενος μετ’ ἐμοῦ εἰς ⸀τὸ τρύβλιον·
verses.mistral: One of you will betray me to death, as it is written of him: 'He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.'
verses.kjv: And he answered and said unto them, It is one of the twelve, that dippeth with me in the dish.

verses.greek: ⸀ὅτι ὁ μὲν υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ὑπάγει καθὼς γέγραπται περὶ αὐτοῦ, οὐαὶ δὲ τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ ἐκείνῳ δι’ οὗ ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου παραδίδοται· ⸀καλὸν αὐτῷ εἰ οὐκ ἐγεννήθη ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος.
verses.mistral: For the Son of Man is going, as it has been written about him; but woe to that man by whom the Son of Man is betrayed! It would have been good for that man if he had not been born.
verses.kjv: The Son of man indeed goeth, as it is written of him: but woe to that man by whom the Son of man is betrayed! good were it for that man if he had never been born.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἐσθιόντων αὐτῶν ⸀λαβὼν ἄρτον εὐλογήσας ἔκλασεν καὶ ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς καὶ εἶπεν· ⸀Λάβετε, τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ σῶμά μου.
verses.mistral: And taking bread, he gave thanks and broke it, and gave them, saying, "Take: this is my body."
verses.kjv: ¶ And as they did eat, Jesus took bread, and blessed, and brake it, and gave to them, and said, Take, eat: this is my body.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀λαβὼν ποτήριον εὐχαριστήσας ἔδωκεν αὐτοῖς, καὶ ἔπιον ἐξ αὐτοῦ πάντες.
verses.mistral: And they all drank from it.
verses.kjv: And he took the cup, and when he had given thanks, he gave it to them: and they all drank of it.

verses.greek: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ αἷμά μου ⸀τῆς διαθήκης τὸ ⸂ἐκχυννόμενον ὑπὲρ πολλῶν⸃.
verses.mistral: This is my blood of the covenant, which is poured out for many.
verses.kjv: And he said unto them, This is my blood of the new testament, which is shed for many.

verses.greek: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐκέτι οὐ μὴ πίω ἐκ τοῦ γενήματος τῆς ἀμπέλου ἕως τῆς ἡμέρας ἐκείνης ὅταν αὐτὸ πίνω καινὸν ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τοῦ θεοῦ.
verses.mistral: I assure you, I will not drink again of the fruit of the vine until that day when I drink it new in the kingdom of God.
verses.kjv: Verily I say unto you, I will drink no more of the fruit of the vine, until that day that I drink it new in the kingdom of God.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὑμνήσαντες ἐξῆλθον εἰς τὸ Ὄρος τῶν Ἐλαιῶν.
verses.mistral: Departed, and they hymned; and they went out into the mount of Olives.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when they had sung an hymn, they went out into the mount of Olives.

verses.greek: Καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὅτι Πάντες ⸀σκανδαλισθήσεσθε, ὅτι γέγραπται· Πατάξω τὸν ποιμένα, καὶ ⸂τὰ πρόβατα διασκορπισθήσονται⸃.
verses.mistral: All will be offended, as it is written: "I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered."
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto them, All ye shall be offended because of me this night: for it is written, I will smite the shepherd, and the sheep shall be scattered.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ μετὰ τὸ ἐγερθῆναί με προάξω ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν.
verses.mistral: Lead me on, after I have been raised, to Galilee.
verses.kjv: But after that I am risen, I will go before you into Galilee.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πέτρος ἔφη αὐτῷ· ⸂Εἰ καὶ⸃ πάντες σκανδαλισθήσονται, ἀλλ’ οὐκ ἐγώ.
verses.mistral: The disciple Peter replied to Him, "Even if all are offended, I will not be."
verses.kjv: But Peter said unto him, Although all shall be offended, yet will not I.

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς· Ἀμὴν λέγω σοι ὅτι σὺ σήμερον ⸂ταύτῃ τῇ νυκτὶ⸃ πρὶν ἢ δὶς ἀλέκτορα φωνῆσαι τρίς ⸂με ἀπαρνήσῃ⸃.
verses.mistral: Jesus said to him, "Truly, I tell you, before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times."
verses.kjv: And Jesus saith unto him, Verily I say unto thee, That this day, even in this night, before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ⸂ἐκπερισσῶς ἐλάλει⸃· Ἐὰν ⸂δέῃ με⸃ συναποθανεῖν σοι, οὐ μή σε ⸀ἀπαρνήσομαι. ὡσαύτως δὲ καὶ πάντες ἔλεγον.
verses.mistral: He denied [Jesus] vehemently. If I must die with you, I will not deny [you]. And all of them said the same.
verses.kjv: But he spake the more vehemently, If I should die with thee, I will not deny thee in any wise. Likewise also said they all.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἔρχονται εἰς χωρίον οὗ τὸ ὄνομα Γεθσημανί, καὶ λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ· Καθίσατε ὧδε ἕως προσεύξωμαι.
verses.mistral: They are coming to a place called Gethsemane, and He says to His disciples, "Sit here until I pray."
verses.kjv: And they came to a place which was named Gethsemane: and he saith to his disciples, Sit ye here, while I shall pray.

verses.greek: καὶ παραλαμβάνει τὸν Πέτρον καὶ ⸂Ἰάκωβον καὶ⸃ Ἰωάννην μετ’ ⸀αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἤρξατο ἐκθαμβεῖσθαι καὶ ἀδημονεῖν,
verses.mistral: And He took Peter and James and John with Him, and began to be deeply distressed and troubled
verses.kjv: And he taketh with him Peter and James and John, and began to be sore amazed, and to be very heavy;

verses.greek: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Περίλυπός ἐστιν ἡ ψυχή μου ἕως θανάτου· μείνατε ὧδε καὶ γρηγορεῖτε.
verses.mistral: And He says to them, 'My soul is very sorrowful, even to death; remain here and watch.'
verses.kjv: And saith unto them, My soul is exceeding sorrowful unto death: tarry ye here, and watch.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂προελθὼν μικρὸν ἔπιπτεν⸃ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, καὶ προσηύχετο ἵνα εἰ δυνατόν ἐστιν παρέλθῃ ἀπ’ αὐτοῦ ἡ ὥρα,
verses.mistral: And a little while He fell on the ground and prayed that if it were possible, the hour might pass from Him.
verses.kjv: And he went forward a little, and fell on the ground, and prayed that, if it were possible, the hour might pass from him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγεν· Αββα ὁ πατήρ, πάντα δυνατά σοι· παρένεγκε τὸ ποτήριον ⸂τοῦτο ἀπ’ ἐμοῦ⸃· ἀλλ’ οὐ τί ἐγὼ θέλω ἀλλὰ τί σύ.
verses.mistral: And He said, "Abba, Father, all things are possible for You; remove this cup from Me; yet not what I will, but what You will."
verses.kjv: And he said, Abba, Father, all things are possible unto thee; take away this cup from me: nevertheless not what I will, but what thou wilt.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔρχεται καὶ εὑρίσκει αὐτοὺς καθεύδοντας, καὶ λέγει τῷ Πέτρῳ· Σίμων, καθεύδεις; οὐκ ἴσχυσας μίαν ὥραν γρηγορῆσαι;
verses.mistral: And he comes and finds them sleeping, and says to Peter: Simon, are you sleeping? Couldn't you watch one hour?
verses.kjv: And he cometh, and findeth them sleeping, and saith unto Peter, Simon, sleepest thou? couldest not thou watch one hour?

verses.greek: γρηγορεῖτε καὶ προσεύχεσθε, ἵνα μὴ ⸀ἔλθητε εἰς πειρασμόν· τὸ μὲν πνεῦμα πρόθυμον ἡ δὲ σὰρξ ἀσθενής.
verses.mistral: Remain alert and pray, that you may not enter into temptation; the spirit indeed is willing, but the flesh is weak.
verses.kjv: Watch ye and pray, lest ye enter into temptation. The spirit truly is ready, but the flesh is weak.

verses.greek: καὶ πάλιν ἀπελθὼν προσηύξατο τὸν αὐτὸν λόγον εἰπών.
verses.mistral: Again, departing, he prayed the same words.
verses.kjv: And again he went away, and prayed, and spake the same words.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂πάλιν ἐλθὼν εὗρεν αὐτοὺς⸃ καθεύδοντας, ἦσαν γὰρ ⸂αὐτῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοὶ⸃ ⸀καταβαρυνόμενοι, καὶ οὐκ ᾔδεισαν τί ⸂ἀποκριθῶσιν αὐτῷ⸃.
verses.mistral: Found them sleeping, and they were very drowsy; they did not know what to answer Him.
verses.kjv: And when he returned, he found them asleep again, (for their eyes were heavy,) neither wist they what to answer him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔρχεται τὸ τρίτον καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς· Καθεύδετε ⸀τὸ λοιπὸν καὶ ἀναπαύεσθε· ἀπέχει· ἦλθεν ἡ ὥρα, ἰδοὺ παραδίδοται ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου εἰς τὰς χεῖρας τῶν ἁμαρτωλῶν.
verses.mistral: The third time He came and said to them, "Sleep on now and take your rest; the hour has come. Behold, the Son of Man is betrayed into the hands of sinners."
verses.kjv: And he cometh the third time, and saith unto them, Sleep on now, and take your rest: it is enough, the hour is come; behold, the Son of man is betrayed into the hands of sinners.

verses.greek: ἐγείρεσθε ἄγωμεν· ἰδοὺ ὁ παραδιδούς με ἤγγικεν.
verses.mistral: Awaken, let us go; behold, the one who is betraying me has come near.
verses.kjv: Rise up, let us go; lo, he that betrayeth me is at hand.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἔτι αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος παραγίνεται ⸀Ἰούδας ⸀εἷς τῶν δώδεκα καὶ μετ’ αὐτοῦ ⸀ὄχλος μετὰ μαχαιρῶν καὶ ξύλων παρὰ τῶν ἀρχιερέων καὶ τῶν γραμματέων καὶ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων.
verses.mistral: Immediately, as Jesus was still speaking, Judas, one of the twelve, arrives with a crowd carrying swords and clubs from the chief priests, scribes, and elders.
verses.kjv: ¶ And immediately, while he yet spake, cometh Judas, one of the twelve, and with him a great multitude with swords and staves, from the chief priests and the scribes and the elders.

verses.greek: δεδώκει δὲ ὁ παραδιδοὺς αὐτὸν σύσσημον αὐτοῖς λέγων· Ὃν ἂν φιλήσω αὐτός ἐστιν· κρατήσατε αὐτὸν καὶ ⸀ἀπάγετε ἀσφαλῶς.
verses.mistral: Delivered him therefore the one betraying Him to them, saying, Whom I will kiss, He is; hold Him and take Him away securely.
verses.kjv: And he that betrayed him had given them a token, saying, Whomsoever I shall kiss, that same is he; take him, and lead him away safely.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐλθὼν ⸀εὐθὺς προσελθὼν αὐτῷ λέγει· ⸀Ῥαββί, καὶ κατεφίλησεν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And coming immediately, drawing near to Him, said to Him: Rabbi, and kissed Him.
verses.kjv: And as soon as he was come, he goeth straightway to him, and saith, Master, master; and kissed him.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἐπέβαλαν ⸂τὰς χεῖρας αὐτῷ⸃ καὶ ἐκράτησαν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: They seized Him and arrested Him.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they laid their hands on him, and took him.

verses.greek: εἷς δέ ⸀τις τῶν παρεστηκότων σπασάμενος τὴν μάχαιραν ἔπαισεν τὸν δοῦλον τοῦ ἀρχιερέως καὶ ἀφεῖλεν αὐτοῦ τὸ ⸀ὠτάριον.
verses.mistral: One of those standing near drew his sword and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his ear.
verses.kjv: And one of them that stood by drew a sword, and smote a servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀποκριθεὶς ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν αὐτοῖς· Ὡς ἐπὶ λῃστὴν ἐξήλθατε μετὰ μαχαιρῶν καὶ ξύλων συλλαβεῖν με;
verses.mistral: With him went out as against a robber, seizing me with swords and clubs?
verses.kjv: And Jesus answered and said unto them, Are ye come out, as against a thief, with swords and with staves to take me?

verses.greek: καθ’ ἡμέραν ἤμην πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ διδάσκων καὶ οὐκ ἐκρατήσατέ με· ἀλλ’ ἵνα πληρωθῶσιν αἱ γραφαί.
verses.mistral: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and you did not seize me. But let the Scriptures be fulfilled.
verses.kjv: I was daily with you in the temple teaching, and ye took me not: but the scriptures must be fulfilled.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀφέντες αὐτὸν ⸂ἔφυγον πάντες⸃.
verses.mistral: Fled all away from him.
verses.kjv: And they all forsook him, and fled.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸂νεανίσκος τις⸃ ⸀συνηκολούθει αὐτῷ περιβεβλημένος σινδόνα ἐπὶ γυμνοῦ, καὶ κρατοῦσιν ⸀αὐτόν,
verses.mistral: Young man, dressed only in a linen cloth, was following him. They seized him.
verses.kjv: And there followed him a certain young man, having a linen cloth cast about his naked body; and the young men laid hold on him:

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ καταλιπὼν τὴν σινδόνα γυμνὸς ⸀ἔφυγεν.
verses.mistral: Abandons the cloak, leaving behind, naked flees
verses.kjv: And he left the linen cloth, and fled from them naked.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀπήγαγον τὸν Ἰησοῦν πρὸς τὸν ἀρχιερέα, καὶ ⸀συνέρχονται πάντες οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ πρεσβύτεροι καὶ οἱ γραμματεῖς.
verses.mistral: Brought Jesus to the high priest, and all the chief priests and elders and scribes were gathered.
verses.kjv: ¶ And they led Jesus away to the high priest: and with him were assembled all the chief priests and the elders and the scribes.

verses.greek: καὶ ὁ Πέτρος ἀπὸ μακρόθεν ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ ἕως ἔσω εἰς τὴν αὐλὴν τοῦ ἀρχιερέως καὶ ἦν συγκαθήμενος μετὰ τῶν ὑπηρετῶν καὶ θερμαινόμενος πρὸς τὸ φῶς.
verses.mistral: And Peter was following from afar and entered into the courtyard of the high priest, and he was sitting with the servants and warming himself by the fire.
verses.kjv: And Peter followed him afar off, even into the palace of the high priest: and he sat with the servants, and warmed himself at the fire.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ ὅλον τὸ συνέδριον ἐζήτουν κατὰ τοῦ Ἰησοῦ μαρτυρίαν εἰς τὸ θανατῶσαι αὐτόν, καὶ οὐχ ηὕρισκον·
verses.mistral: The high priests and the entire council were seeking testimony against Jesus to put him to death, but they found none.
verses.kjv: And the chief priests and all the council sought for witness against Jesus to put him to death; and found none.

verses.greek: πολλοὶ γὰρ ἐψευδομαρτύρουν κατ’ αὐτοῦ, καὶ ἴσαι αἱ μαρτυρίαι οὐκ ἦσαν.
verses.mistral: Many testified falsely against him, and the testimonies were not consistent.
verses.kjv: For many bare false witness against him, but their witness agreed not together.

verses.greek: καί τινες ἀναστάντες ἐψευδομαρτύρουν κατ’ αὐτοῦ λέγοντες
verses.mistral: Rising, some falsely testified against him
verses.kjv: And there arose certain, and bare false witness against him, saying,

verses.greek: ὅτι Ἡμεῖς ἠκούσαμεν αὐτοῦ λέγοντος ὅτι Ἐγὼ καταλύσω τὸν ναὸν τοῦτον τὸν χειροποίητον καὶ διὰ τριῶν ἡμερῶν ἄλλον ἀχειροποίητον οἰκοδομήσω·
verses.mistral: We have heard him saying, "I will destroy this temple that is made without hands, and in three days I will build another not made with hands."
verses.kjv: We heard him say, I will destroy this temple that is made with hands, and within three days I will build another made without hands.

verses.greek: καὶ οὐδὲ οὕτως ἴση ἦν ἡ μαρτυρία αὐτῶν.
verses.mistral: And their testimony was not equal
verses.kjv: But neither so did their witness agree together.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναστὰς ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς εἰς μέσον ἐπηρώτησεν τὸν Ἰησοῦν λέγων· Οὐκ ἀποκρίνῃ οὐδέν; τί οὗτοί σου καταμαρτυροῦσιν;
verses.mistral: And the high priest arose to the midst and questioned Jesus, saying: Art thou able not to answer? What these witness against thee?
verses.kjv: And the high priest stood up in the midst, and asked Jesus, saying, Answerest thou nothing? what is it which these witness against thee?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἐσιώπα καὶ ⸂οὐκ ἀπεκρίνατο οὐδέν⸃. πάλιν ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς ἐπηρώτα αὐτὸν καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ· Σὺ εἶ ὁ χριστὸς ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ εὐλογητοῦ;
verses.mistral: He remained silent and did not answer at all. Again, the high priest asked him and said to him: Art thou the Christ, the Son of the blessed?
verses.kjv: But he held his peace, and answered nothing. Again the high priest asked him, and said unto him, Art thou the Christ, the Son of the Blessed?

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς εἶπεν· Ἐγώ εἰμι, καὶ ὄψεσθε τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐκ δεξιῶν καθήμενον τῆς δυνάμεως καὶ ἐρχόμενον μετὰ τῶν νεφελῶν τοῦ οὐρανοῦ.
verses.mistral: Jesus said, "I am, and you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand, coming with the clouds of heaven."
verses.kjv: And Jesus said, I am: and ye shall see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἀρχιερεὺς διαρρήξας τοὺς χιτῶνας αὐτοῦ λέγει· Τί ἔτι χρείαν ἔχομεν μαρτύρων;
verses.mistral: The high priest tore his clothes and said, "What further need have we of witnesses?"
verses.kjv: Then the high priest rent his clothes, and saith, What need we any further witnesses?

verses.greek: ἠκούσατε τῆς βλασφημίας· τί ὑμῖν φαίνεται; οἱ δὲ πάντες κατέκριναν αὐτὸν ⸂ἔνοχον εἶναι⸃ θανάτου.
verses.mistral: Heard the blasphemy; what seems this to you? But all condemned him as deserving of death.
verses.kjv: Ye have heard the blasphemy: what think ye? And they all condemned him to be guilty of death.

verses.greek: καὶ ἤρξαντό τινες ἐμπτύειν αὐτῷ καὶ περικαλύπτειν ⸂αὐτοῦ τὸ πρόσωπον⸃ καὶ κολαφίζειν αὐτὸν καὶ λέγειν αὐτῷ· Προφήτευσον, καὶ οἱ ὑπηρέται ῥαπίσμασιν αὐτὸν ⸀ἔλαβον.
verses.mistral: Began some to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to strike him, and to say unto him, Prophesy; and the servants did seize him with slaps.
verses.kjv: And some began to spit on him, and to cover his face, and to buffet him, and to say unto him, Prophesy: and the servants did strike him with the palms of their hands.

verses.greek: Καὶ ὄντος τοῦ Πέτρου ⸂κάτω ἐν τῇ αὐλῇ⸃ ἔρχεται μία τῶν παιδισκῶν τοῦ ἀρχιερέως,
verses.mistral: The woman at the temple's gate approaches Peter.
verses.kjv: ¶ And as Peter was beneath in the palace, there cometh one of the maids of the high priest:

verses.greek: καὶ ἰδοῦσα τὸν Πέτρον θερμαινόμενον ἐμβλέψασα αὐτῷ λέγει· Καὶ σὺ μετὰ τοῦ Ναζαρηνοῦ ⸂ἦσθα τοῦ Ἰησοῦ⸃·
verses.mistral: And looking at Peter as he was warming himself, she said to him: "And you also with the Nazarene were [you] the one called Jesus?"
verses.kjv: And when she saw Peter warming himself, she looked upon him, and said, And thou also wast with Jesus of Nazareth.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἠρνήσατο λέγων· ⸂Οὔτε οἶδα οὔτε ἐπίσταμαι σὺ τί⸃ λέγεις, καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἔξω εἰς τὸ προαύλιον ⸂καὶ ἀλέκτωρ ἐφώνησεν⸃.
verses.mistral: He denied, saying, "I neither know nor acknowledge whom you are speaking about, and he went out outside to the porch. And a cock crowed."
verses.kjv: But he denied, saying, I know not, neither understand I what thou sayest. And he went out into the porch; and the cock crew.

verses.greek: καὶ ἡ παιδίσκη ἰδοῦσα αὐτὸν ⸂ἤρξατο πάλιν⸃ λέγειν τοῖς ⸀παρεστῶσιν ὅτι Οὗτος ἐξ αὐτῶν ἐστιν.
verses.mistral: And the maidservant, seeing him, began again to say to those who were present that This man is one of them.
verses.kjv: And a maid saw him again, and began to say to them that stood by, This is one of them.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ πάλιν ἠρνεῖτο. καὶ μετὰ μικρὸν πάλιν οἱ παρεστῶτες ἔλεγον τῷ Πέτρῳ· Ἀληθῶς ἐξ αὐτῶν εἶ, καὶ γὰρ Γαλιλαῖος εἶ ⸂καὶ ἡ λαλιά σου ὁμοιάζει⸃·
verses.mistral: He again denied. And soon after, again those present said to Peter, " Truly you are one of them, and because your speech resembles a Galilean."
verses.kjv: And he denied it again. And a little after, they that stood by said again to Peter, Surely thou art one of them: for thou art a Galilaean, and thy speech agreeth thereto.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ ἤρξατο ἀναθεματίζειν καὶ ὀμνύναι ὅτι Οὐκ οἶδα τὸν ἄνθρωπον τοῦτον ὃν λέγετε.
verses.mistral: He began to curse and swear, saying, "I do not know this man about whom you speak."
verses.kjv: But he began to curse and to swear, saying, I know not this man of whom ye speak.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς ἐκ δευτέρου ἀλέκτωρ ἐφώνησεν· καὶ ἀνεμνήσθη ὁ Πέτρος τὸ ῥῆμα ⸀ὡς εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὅτι Πρὶν ἀλέκτορα ⸂φωνῆσαι δὶς τρίς με ἀπαρνήσῃ⸃, καὶ ἐπιβαλὼν ἔκλαιεν.
verses.mistral: Immediately the rooster crowed a second time. And Peter remembered the word that Jesus had said to him, "Before the rooster crows twice, you will deny me three times." And he began to weep.
verses.kjv: And the second time the cock crew. And Peter called to mind the word that Jesus said unto him, Before the cock crow twice, thou shalt deny me thrice. And when he thought thereon, he wept.

verses.greek: Καὶ ⸀εὐθὺς πρωῒ συμβούλιον ποιήσαντες οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς μετὰ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων καὶ γραμματέων καὶ ὅλον τὸ συνέδριον δήσαντες τὸν Ἰησοῦν ἀπήνεγκαν καὶ ⸀παρέδωκαν Πιλάτῳ.
verses.mistral: Immediately, in the morning, the leaders with the elders and scribes and the whole council bound Jesus, handed Him over to Pilate.
verses.kjv: And straightway in the morning the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council, and bound Jesus, and carried him away, and delivered him to Pilate.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐπηρώτησεν αὐτὸν ὁ Πιλᾶτος· Σὺ εἶ ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν Ἰουδαίων; ὁ δὲ ἀποκριθεὶς ⸂αὐτῷ λέγει⸃· Σὺ λέγεις.
verses.mistral: Pilate asked Him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" And He answered him, "You say so."
verses.kjv: And Pilate asked him, Art thou the King of the Jews? And he answering said unto him, Thou sayest it.

verses.greek: καὶ κατηγόρουν αὐτοῦ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς πολλά.
verses.mistral: And the high priests accused Him much.
verses.kjv: And the chief priests accused him of many things: but he answered nothing.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος πάλιν ⸀ἐπηρώτα αὐτὸν λέγων· Οὐκ ἀποκρίνῃ οὐδέν; ἴδε πόσα σου ⸀κατηγοροῦσιν.
verses.mistral: The centurion asked him again, saying, "Are you unable to speak? See how many are accusing you."
verses.kjv: And Pilate asked him again, saying, Answerest thou nothing? behold how many things they witness against thee.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς οὐκέτι οὐδὲν ἀπεκρίθη, ὥστε θαυμάζειν τὸν Πιλᾶτον.
verses.mistral: Jesus said nothing more, so the centurion marveled at him.
verses.kjv: But Jesus yet answered nothing; so that Pilate marvelled.

verses.greek: Κατὰ δὲ ἑορτὴν ἀπέλυεν αὐτοῖς ἕνα δέσμιον ⸂ὃν παρῃτοῦντο⸃.
verses.mistral: Released to them one prisoner, whom they were asking for.
verses.kjv: Now at that feast he released unto them one prisoner, whomsoever they desired.

verses.greek: ἦν δὲ ὁ λεγόμενος Βαραββᾶς μετὰ τῶν ⸀στασιαστῶν δεδεμένος οἵτινες ἐν τῇ στάσει φόνον πεποιήκεισαν.
verses.mistral: Barabbas was with the insurrectionists, who in the insurrection had committed murder.
verses.kjv: And there was one named Barabbas, which lay bound with them that had made insurrection with him, who had committed murder in the insurrection.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἀναβὰς ὁ ὄχλος ἤρξατο αἰτεῖσθαι ⸀καθὼς ἐποίει αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: And the crowd began to demand as was their custom.
verses.kjv: And the multitude crying aloud began to desire him to do as he had ever done unto them.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος ἀπεκρίθη αὐτοῖς λέγων· Θέλετε ἀπολύσω ὑμῖν τὸν βασιλέα τῶν Ἰουδαίων;
verses.mistral: The Pilate answered them, saying: "Do you want to release to you the King of the Jews?"
verses.kjv: But Pilate answered them, saying, Will ye that I release unto you the King of the Jews?

verses.greek: ἐγίνωσκεν γὰρ ὅτι διὰ φθόνον παραδεδώκεισαν αὐτὸν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς.
verses.mistral: Recognized, for envy they had delivered Him up by the chief priests.
verses.kjv: For he knew that the chief priests had delivered him for envy.

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ ἀρχιερεῖς ἀνέσεισαν τὸν ὄχλον ἵνα μᾶλλον τὸν Βαραββᾶν ἀπολύσῃ αὐτοῖς.
verses.mistral: The high priests dismissed the crowd, so they might release Barabbas to them instead.
verses.kjv: But the chief priests moved the people, that he should rather release Barabbas unto them.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος ⸂πάλιν ἀποκριθεὶς ἔλεγεν⸃ αὐτοῖς· Τί οὖν ⸀θέλετε ποιήσω ⸂ὃν λέγετε⸃ ⸀τὸν βασιλέα τῶν Ἰουδαίων;
verses.mistral: Pilate again spoke to them, saying: "What do you want me to do with the King of the Jews?"
verses.kjv: And Pilate answered and said again unto them, What will ye then that I shall do unto him whom ye call the King of the Jews?

verses.greek: οἱ δὲ πάλιν ἔκραξαν· Σταύρωσον αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Crucify him.
verses.kjv: And they cried out again, Crucify him.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς· Τί γὰρ ⸂ἐποίησεν κακόν⸃; οἱ δὲ ⸀περισσῶς ἔκραξαν· Σταύρωσον αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Pilate said to them, "What evil has he done?" But they shouted all the more, "Crucify him!"
verses.kjv: Then Pilate said unto them, Why, what evil hath he done? And they cried out the more exceedingly, Crucify him.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος βουλόμενος τῷ ὄχλῳ τὸ ἱκανὸν ποιῆσαι ἀπέλυσεν αὐτοῖς τὸν Βαραββᾶν, καὶ παρέδωκεν τὸν Ἰησοῦν φραγελλώσας ἵνα σταυρωθῇ.
verses.mistral: Releases Barabbas to the crowd, and delivers Jesus after scourging, so that he may be crucified.
verses.kjv: ¶ And so Pilate, willing to content the people, released Barabbas unto them, and delivered Jesus, when he had scourged him, to be crucified.

verses.greek: Οἱ δὲ στρατιῶται ἀπήγαγον αὐτὸν ἔσω τῆς αὐλῆς, ὅ ἐστιν πραιτώριον, καὶ συγκαλοῦσιν ὅλην τὴν σπεῖραν.
verses.mistral: The soldiers led him inside the palace (praetorium), and they called out the whole cohort.
verses.kjv: And the soldiers led him away into the hall, called Praetorium; and they call together the whole band.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸀ἐνδιδύσκουσιν αὐτὸν πορφύραν καὶ περιτιθέασιν αὐτῷ πλέξαντες ἀκάνθινον στέφανον·
verses.mistral: And they clothe him in purple, and weave a crown of thorns, placing it on him.
verses.kjv: And they clothed him with purple, and platted a crown of thorns, and put it about his head,

verses.greek: καὶ ἤρξαντο ἀσπάζεσθαι αὐτόν· Χαῖρε, ⸀βασιλεῦ τῶν Ἰουδαίων·
verses.mistral: Greetings, King of the Jews
verses.kjv: And began to salute him, Hail, King of the Jews!

verses.greek: καὶ ἔτυπτον αὐτοῦ τὴν κεφαλὴν καλάμῳ καὶ ἐνέπτυον αὐτῷ, καὶ τιθέντες τὰ γόνατα προσεκύνουν αὐτῷ.
verses.mistral: And they spitted upon Him, and took the reed and struck Him; and bowing their knees worshipped Him.
verses.kjv: And they smote him on the head with a reed, and did spit upon him, and bowing their knees worshipped him.

verses.greek: καὶ ὅτε ἐνέπαιξαν αὐτῷ, ἐξέδυσαν αὐτὸν τὴν πορφύραν καὶ ἐνέδυσαν αὐτὸν τὰ ἱμάτια ⸂τὰ ἴδια⸃. καὶ ἐξάγουσιν αὐτὸν ἵνα ⸀σταυρώσωσιν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And when they had mocked Him, they took the purple robe off Him and put His own clothes on Him. And they led Him away to crucify Him.
verses.kjv: And when they had mocked him, they took off the purple from him, and put his own clothes on him, and led him out to crucify him.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἀγγαρεύουσιν παράγοντά τινα Σίμωνα Κυρηναῖον ἐρχόμενον ἀπ’ ἀγροῦ, τὸν πατέρα Ἀλεξάνδρου καὶ Ῥούφου, ἵνα ἄρῃ τὸν σταυρὸν αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: And they enlist Simon of Cyrene coming from the field, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to carry His cross.
verses.kjv: And they compel one Simon a Cyrenian, who passed by, coming out of the country, the father of Alexander and Rufus, to bear his cross.

verses.greek: καὶ φέρουσιν αὐτὸν ἐπὶ ⸀τὸν ⸀Γολγοθᾶν τόπον, ὅ ἐστιν ⸀μεθερμηνευόμενον Κρανίου Τόπος.
verses.mistral: Bring Him to the place called Golgotha, which means Skull Place.
verses.kjv: And they bring him unto the place Golgotha, which is, being interpreted, The place of a skull.

verses.greek: καὶ ἐδίδουν ⸀αὐτῷ ἐσμυρνισμένον οἶνον, ⸀ὃς δὲ οὐκ ἔλαβεν.
verses.mistral: Offered him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it.
verses.kjv: And they gave him to drink wine mingled with myrrh: but he received it not.

verses.greek: καὶ ⸂σταυροῦσιν αὐτὸν καὶ⸃ διαμερίζονται τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτοῦ, βάλλοντες κλῆρον ἐπ’ αὐτὰ τίς τί ἄρῃ.
verses.mistral: And they crucify him and divide his garments, casting lots upon them, who will take what.
verses.kjv: And when they had crucified him, they parted his garments, casting lots upon them, what every man should take.

verses.greek: Ἦν δὲ ὥρα τρίτη καὶ ἐσταύρωσαν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Third hour, they crucified him.
verses.kjv: And it was the third hour, and they crucified him.

verses.greek: καὶ ἦν ἡ ἐπιγραφὴ τῆς αἰτίας αὐτοῦ ἐπιγεγραμμένη· Ὁ βασιλεὺς τῶν Ἰουδαίων.
verses.mistral: The inscription of the reason for him was written: The King of the Jews.
verses.kjv: And the superscription of his accusation was written over, THE KING OF THE JEWS.

verses.greek: καὶ σὺν αὐτῷ σταυροῦσιν δύο λῃστάς, ἕνα ἐκ δεξιῶν καὶ ἕνα ἐξ εὐωνύμων ⸀αὐτοῦ.
verses.mistral: Crucified with him were two robbers, one on his right and one on his left.
verses.kjv: And with him they crucify two thieves; the one on his right hand, and the other on his left.

verses.greek: Καὶ οἱ παραπορευόμενοι ἐβλασφήμουν αὐτὸν κινοῦντες τὰς κεφαλὰς αὐτῶν καὶ λέγοντες· Οὐὰ ὁ καταλύων τὸν ναὸν καὶ ⸂οἰκοδομῶν ἐν τρισὶν ἡμέραις⸃,
verses.mistral: And those passing by blasphemed him, wagging their heads and saying, "Ah, the one destroying the temple and building it in three days."
verses.kjv: And they that passed by railed on him, wagging their heads, and saying, Ah, thou that destroyest the temple, and buildest it in three days,

verses.greek: σῶσον σεαυτὸν ⸀καταβὰς ἀπὸ τοῦ σταυροῦ.
verses.mistral: Save yourself by coming down from the cross.
verses.kjv: Save thyself, and come down from the cross.

verses.greek: ὁμοίως καὶ οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς ἐμπαίζοντες πρὸς ἀλλήλους μετὰ τῶν γραμματέων ἔλεγον· Ἄλλους ἔσωσεν, ἑαυτὸν οὐ δύναται σῶσαι·
verses.mistral: Similarly, the high priests mocking among themselves with the scribes said, "Let another be saved; he cannot save himself."
verses.kjv: Likewise also the chief priests mocking said among themselves with the scribes, He saved others; himself he cannot save.

verses.greek: ὁ χριστὸς ὁ ⸀βασιλεὺς Ἰσραὴλ καταβάτω νῦν ἀπὸ τοῦ σταυροῦ, ἵνα ἴδωμεν καὶ ⸀πιστεύσωμεν. καὶ οἱ συνεσταυρωμένοι ⸀σὺν αὐτῷ ὠνείδιζον αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: The King of Israel, the Christ, descends now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And those crucified with Him reviled Him.
verses.kjv: Let Christ the King of Israel descend now from the cross, that we may see and believe. And they that were crucified with him reviled him.

verses.greek: ⸂Καὶ γενομένης⸃ ὥρας ἕκτης σκότος ἐγένετο ἐφ’ ὅλην τὴν γῆν ἕως ὥρας ἐνάτης.
verses.mistral: Darkness came over the whole land for three hours.
verses.kjv: And when the sixth hour was come, there was darkness over the whole land until the ninth hour.

verses.greek: καὶ τῇ ⸂ἐνάτῃ ὥρᾳ⸃ ἐβόησεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς φωνῇ ⸀μεγάλῃ· Ἐλωῒ ἐλωῒ ⸀λεμὰ σαβαχθάνι; ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον Ὁ θεός μου ὁ θεός μου, εἰς τί ⸂ἐγκατέλιπές με⸃;
verses.mistral: Jesus cried out with a loud voice, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"
verses.kjv: And at the ninth hour Jesus cried with a loud voice, saying, Eloi, Eloi, lama sabachthani? which is, being interpreted, My God, my God, why hast thou forsaken me?

verses.greek: καί τινες τῶν παρεστηκότων ἀκούσαντες ἔλεγον· ⸀Ἴδε Ἠλίαν φωνεῖ.
verses.mistral: Behold, Elijah is calling.
verses.kjv: And some of them that stood by, when they heard it, said, Behold, he calleth Elias.

verses.greek: δραμὼν δέ ⸂τις καὶ⸃ γεμίσας σπόγγον ὄξους ⸀περιθεὶς καλάμῳ ἐπότιζεν αὐτόν, λέγων· Ἄφετε ἴδωμεν εἰ ἔρχεται Ἠλίας καθελεῖν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: Running up, a certain man, having soaked sponges in wine, and wrapping a reed in them, was giving it to him to drink, saying: "Let us see if Elijah comes to save him."
verses.kjv: And one ran and filled a spunge full of vinegar, and put it on a reed, and gave him to drink, saying, Let alone; let us see whether Elias will come to take him down.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς ἀφεὶς φωνὴν μεγάλην ἐξέπνευσεν.
verses.mistral: Jesus breathed out a great breath.
verses.kjv: And Jesus cried with a loud voice, and gave up the ghost.

verses.greek: καὶ τὸ καταπέτασμα τοῦ ναοῦ ἐσχίσθη εἰς δύο ἀπ’ ἄνωθεν ἕως κάτω.
verses.mistral: The veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
verses.kjv: And the veil of the temple was rent in twain from the top to the bottom.

verses.greek: ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ κεντυρίων ὁ παρεστηκὼς ἐξ ἐναντίας αὐτοῦ ὅτι ⸀οὕτως ἐξέπνευσεν εἶπεν· Ἀληθῶς ⸂οὗτος ὁ ἄνθρωπος⸃ υἱὸς ⸂θεοῦ ἦν⸃.
verses.mistral: This man was the Son of God.
verses.kjv: ¶ And when the centurion, which stood over against him, saw that he so cried out, and gave up the ghost, he said, Truly this man was the Son of God.

verses.greek: Ἦσαν δὲ καὶ γυναῖκες ἀπὸ μακρόθεν θεωροῦσαι, ἐν ⸀αἷς καὶ ⸀Μαρία ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ Μαρία ⸀ἡ Ἰακώβου τοῦ μικροῦ καὶ ⸀Ἰωσῆτος μήτηρ καὶ Σαλώμη,
verses.mistral: Women were watching from afar, among whom were Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James the younger and of Joses, and Salome.
verses.kjv: There were also women looking on afar off: among whom was Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James the less and of Joses, and Salome;

verses.greek: ⸀αἳ ὅτε ἦν ἐν τῇ Γαλιλαίᾳ ἠκολούθουν αὐτῷ καὶ διηκόνουν αὐτῷ, καὶ ἄλλαι πολλαὶ αἱ συναναβᾶσαι αὐτῷ εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα.
verses.mistral: Following Him and serving Him in Galilee were also many others who accompanied Him into Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: (Who also, when he was in Galilee, followed him, and ministered unto him;) and many other women which came up with him unto Jerusalem.

verses.greek: Καὶ ἤδη ὀψίας γενομένης, ἐπεὶ ἦν παρασκευή, ὅ ἐστιν ⸀προσάββατον,
verses.mistral: Evening came, and that day was the preparation day, which is called Sabbath
verses.kjv: ¶ And now when the even was come, because it was the preparation, that is, the day before the sabbath,

verses.greek: ⸀ἐλθὼν Ἰωσὴφ ⸀ὁ ἀπὸ Ἁριμαθαίας εὐσχήμων βουλευτής, ὃς καὶ αὐτὸς ἦν προσδεχόμενος τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ, τολμήσας εἰσῆλθεν πρὸς ⸀τὸν Πιλᾶτον καὶ ᾐτήσατο τὸ σῶμα τοῦ Ἰησοῦ.
verses.mistral: Arriving, Joseph of Arimathaea, a distinguished council member who was also himself expecting the kingdom of God, dared to enter Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
verses.kjv: Joseph of Arimathaea, an honourable counsellor, which also waited for the kingdom of God, came, and went in boldly unto Pilate, and craved the body of Jesus.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ Πιλᾶτος ἐθαύμασεν εἰ ἤδη τέθνηκεν, καὶ προσκαλεσάμενος τὸν κεντυρίωνα ἐπηρώτησεν αὐτὸν εἰ ⸀πάλαι ἀπέθανεν·
verses.mistral: Pilate marveled if He already had died, and calling for the centurion, he asked him whether He had died yet.
verses.kjv: And Pilate marvelled if he were already dead: and calling unto him the centurion, he asked him whether he had been any while dead.

verses.greek: καὶ γνοὺς ἀπὸ τοῦ κεντυρίωνος ἐδωρήσατο τὸ ⸀πτῶμα τῷ Ἰωσήφ.
verses.mistral: And knowing from the centurion, he gave the corpse to Joseph.
verses.kjv: And when he knew it of the centurion, he gave the body to Joseph.

verses.greek: καὶ ἀγοράσας ⸀σινδόνα καθελὼν αὐτὸν ἐνείλησεν τῇ σινδόνι καὶ ⸀ἔθηκεν αὐτὸν ἐν ⸀μνημείῳ ὃ ἦν λελατομημένον ἐκ πέτρας, καὶ προσεκύλισεν λίθον ἐπὶ τὴν θύραν τοῦ μνημείου.
verses.mistral: He purchased a linen cloth, took him down, wrapped him in the linen cloth and placed him in a tomb cut out of rock, and rolled a stone against the entrance of the tomb.
verses.kjv: And he bought fine linen, and took him down, and wrapped him in the linen, and laid him in a sepulchre which was hewn out of a rock, and rolled a stone unto the door of the sepulchre.

verses.greek: ἡ δὲ Μαρία ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ Μαρία ⸂ἡ Ἰωσῆτος⸃ ἐθεώρουν ποῦ ⸀τέθειται.
verses.mistral: The two Marys, Mary Magdalene and Mary of Joses, went to see where he was laid.
verses.kjv: And Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses beheld where he was laid.

verses.greek: Καὶ διαγενομένου τοῦ σαββάτου ⸀Μαρία ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ Μαρία ⸂ἡ τοῦ⸃ Ἰακώβου καὶ Σαλώμη ἠγόρασαν ἀρώματα ἵνα ἐλθοῦσαι ἀλείψωσιν αὐτόν.
verses.mistral: And on the Sabbath, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices that they might come and anoint him.
verses.kjv: And when the sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, had bought sweet spices, that they might come and anoint him.

verses.greek: καὶ λίαν πρωῒ ⸂τῇ μιᾷ τῶν⸃ σαββάτων ἔρχονται ἐπὶ τὸ μνημεῖον ἀνατείλαντος τοῦ ἡλίου.
verses.mistral: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb.
verses.kjv: And very early in the morning the first day of the week, they came unto the sepulchre at the rising of the sun.

verses.greek: καὶ ἔλεγον πρὸς ἑαυτάς· Τίς ἀποκυλίσει ἡμῖν τὸν λίθον ⸀ἐκ τῆς θύρας τοῦ μνημείου;
verses.mistral: Who will roll away the stone for us from the door of the tomb?
verses.kjv: And they said among themselves, Who shall roll us away the stone from the door of the sepulchre?

verses.greek: καὶ ἀναβλέψασαι θεωροῦσιν ὅτι ⸀ἀποκεκύλισται ὁ λίθος, ἦν γὰρ μέγας σφόδρα.
verses.mistral: And looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled away, for it was very large.
verses.kjv: And when they looked, they saw that the stone was rolled away: for it was very great.

verses.greek: καὶ εἰσελθοῦσαι εἰς τὸ μνημεῖον εἶδον νεανίσκον καθήμενον ἐν τοῖς δεξιοῖς περιβεβλημένον στολὴν λευκήν, καὶ ἐξεθαμβήθησαν.
verses.mistral: Entered the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe; and they were amazed.
verses.kjv: And entering into the sepulchre, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, clothed in a long white garment; and they were affrighted.

verses.greek: ὁ δὲ λέγει αὐταῖς· Μὴ ἐκθαμβεῖσθε· Ἰησοῦν ζητεῖτε τὸν Ναζαρηνὸν τὸν ἐσταυρωμένον· ἠγέρθη, οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε· ἴδε ὁ τόπος ὅπου ἔθηκαν αὐτόν·
verses.mistral: He says to them, "Do not be amazed. Seek Jesus the Nazarene who was crucified. He has risen; he is not here. See the place where they laid him."
verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Be not affrighted: Ye seek Jesus of Nazareth, which was crucified: he is risen; he is not here: behold the place where they laid him.

verses.greek: ἀλλὰ ὑπάγετε εἴπατε τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ καὶ τῷ Πέτρῳ ὅτι Προάγει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν· ἐκεῖ αὐτὸν ὄψεσθε, καθὼς εἶπεν ὑμῖν.
verses.mistral: He commands you to go and say to His disciples and to Peter that He is going before you into Galilee; there you will see Him, as He said to you.
verses.kjv: But go your way, tell his disciples and Peter that he goeth before you into Galilee: there shall ye see him, as he said unto you.
